prompt: Create a vertically oriented promotional page that is divided into two main vertical columns across its full height, with a wider left column and a narrower right column that are top-aligned. In the top area of the left column, build a hero section that itself uses a two-column layout: in the left sub-column stack a small heading announcing a cooking workshop, a larger title about Japanese sushi rolls, a short descriptive paragraph, a bold price label, and a call-to-action button, all aligned to the left and grouped closely; in the right sub-column place a single large rectangular food photo that occupies most of the hero height. Directly below this hero in the left column, add a single full-width paragraph of descriptive text explaining the workshop, left-aligned. Beneath that paragraph, still in the left column, add a section about what participants will learn: place a section heading at the top of this section spanning the column, then below split the area into two main parts in one horizontal row, where the left part contains a vertical list of multiple bullet points each preceded by an icon, and the right part contains one large circular photo positioned so it occupies the right half of the section and sits slightly lower than the top of the bullet list so that it visually anchors the lower-right corner. In the right column, create at the top an instructor section composed of two stacked rows: the upper row has text heading lines introducing the instructor; the lower row is a horizontal arrangement with a circular instructor photo on one side and a vertical list of several short bullet lines with small icons on the other, both centered within the overall right column width. Immediately below this instructor section, keep within the right column and add an audience suitability section consisting of a centered heading followed by a row of three equal-width cards arranged in a single horizontal line; each card contains a rectangular photo at the top and a text caption block beneath it. Under this in the right column, add a benefits section that begins with a centered heading on its own row; beneath the heading, structure the content as two side-by-side vertical columns: the left column contains a small internal heading, directly beneath it a short horizontal row of two adjacent pill-like buttons, and below them a food image with two small price labels near it, all stacked; the right column contains a vertical stack of short paragraphs or bullet-like statements, each starting with a short bold phrase and followed by a brief explanation, listing several reasons to join. Below the benefits section, insert a full-width horizontal band that spans the entire width of the right column to act as a separator. Finally, at the bottom of the right column, create a limited-spots and schedule section built as a single-column vertical stack: place a centered heading about limited spots at the top; below it, add one row that shows the event day, specific date, time range, and location in a single horizontal line with small icons separating the pieces of information; and under that row, add a countdown-style grid consisting of four equal-sized rectangular boxes arranged horizontally, each box containing a large number with a smaller label beneath, all centered within each box. Ensure all these groups stay within their respective columns so that the left column presents the workshop overview and learning outcomes, while the right column presents instructor details, target audience, benefits, and scheduling information in a clearly segmented vertical flow.

prompt: Create a desktop webpage mockup displayed inside a centered browser-like frame on a neutral background. Inside this frame, place a narrow window control bar at the very top containing three small circular controls grouped on the left side and otherwise empty. Directly beneath this bar, span a full-width header composed of a single main row divided into three horizontal regions: a left region with a compact site logo, a central region with a long search input field, and a right region with two small account-related elements (a sign-in item and a basket item) arranged side-by-side, all aligned in one horizontal line. Immediately below this main header row, add a secondary navigation bar consisting of one horizontal row of five navigation items, each item containing an icon above a short label, spaced evenly from left to right across the content width. Directly beneath this navigation bar, add a full-width promotional strip that forms one horizontal band subdivided into three separate text blocks laid out side-by-side. Below the promo strip, begin the main content area. At the top of this area, create a section header row where the text heading “shop for” is left-aligned, and on the same horizontal line to its right place a group of rectangular filter buttons arranged in a short horizontal row, including an emphasized “My pets” option among them. Immediately under the “shop for” heading, aligned to the same left content margin, place a sub-row that contains a small label followed by two circular pet avatar chips with names, all arranged in a single horizontal line. Beneath this sub-row, insert a horizontal list of category items: create one row of seven category elements where each element consists of a circular graphic on top and a short label centered directly below it; arrange these seven items side-by-side with equal spacing, allowing the seventh item on the far right to be partially cropped by the page edge to imply additional scrollable content. Continue the vertical flow by placing another section header below the categories row, left-aligned with the same content margin and containing the text “New and featured.” Directly below this heading, construct a row of rectangular promotional cards: create a single horizontal row of four cards laid out side-by-side, each card being a wide rectangle containing generic promotional imagery and text, and ensure that the fourth card on the far right is only partially visible to again suggest horizontal scrolling. Maintain consistent horizontal margins so that all main content, from the “shop for” heading through the “New and featured” cards, is aligned within the same left and right content boundaries, with each major section stacked vertically in the order: window control bar, three-part header row, icon navigation row, promo strip, shop-for header and filters, pet avatar row, category circles row, new-and-featured heading, and row of new-and-featured cards.

prompt: Recreate a canvas that shows two tall web page mockups centered horizontally and placed side by side, with the left mockup slightly narrower than the right one and both framed against a plain background. The left page mockup is a single-column landing page composed of five vertically stacked sections. At the top of the left page, place a full-width rectangular hero banner that fills the page width; inside this banner, divide the content into two columns, with a product image occupying the left side and a right-side column containing a heading, a short paragraph below it, and a single call-to-action button directly beneath the paragraph, all left-aligned within that right column. Directly below this hero banner, add a new arrivals section: first a left-aligned section title on its own line near the top of the section, and beneath it a clustered product layout where one large circular product image sits on the left with its product name and price text placed below, while on the right side two smaller circular product images are arranged one above the other in a staggered right column, each with its own name and price below; beneath the large left circle, place a rectangular button near the lower-left of this product cluster. Below the new arrivals section, add a full-width feature section organized as two equal columns: the left column holds a heading at the top, a descriptive paragraph beneath it, and a rectangular button below the text, all stacked vertically; the right column contains a vertical list of several short feature lines, each preceded by a small icon, aligned so that the list starts roughly level with the text in the left column. Under this feature section, create a footer-style area with several narrow vertical columns of text links arranged side by side in a single horizontal row, each column containing a small heading and a short list of items beneath, all aligned along a common top baseline. At the very bottom of the left page, centered horizontally, place a large brand wordmark as the only element in a bottom logo area. The right page mockup is a taller single-column marketing page with four main vertical sections. At the very top of the right page, place a horizontal navigation bar that spans the full width of the page; inside this bar, align the brand wordmark on the left, and on the right place a horizontal row of several navigation links ending with a shopping cart icon aligned to the far right. Directly beneath the navigation bar, add a hero section divided into two equal-width columns: the left column contains a large headline near the top, a descriptive paragraph below the headline, and a single call-to-action button directly beneath the paragraph, all left-aligned; the right column contains a large circular product image centered within its column, with small circular carousel controls positioned adjacent to the right edge of this image. Below this hero area, add a full-width row of text consisting of two side-by-side blocks: a left-aligned section heading on the left and a short descriptive paragraph on the right, aligned so that both blocks share the same vertical baseline. Beneath this introductory row, place a three-column grid of collection cards in a single horizontal line, each card equal in width and height; within each card, stack a small icon at the top, a collection name below the icon, and a brief descriptive sentence beneath the name, all center-aligned within the card. Finally, immediately under the row of three cards, place one rectangular call-to-action button centered horizontally across the width of the card grid, forming the bottom portion of the right page layout.

prompt: Create a web page layout with a three-column structure beneath a top-left logo row. At the very top-left, place a horizontal header strip containing a compact logo mark on the far left and the site name to its right, with this strip sitting above the main content. Below this, divide the page into three vertical columns: a wider left column, a narrower middle column, and a similarly narrow right column, all top-aligned. In the left column, stack three sections vertically. The top section is a large hero card that spans the width of the left column; inside this hero card, place a slim header row at the top containing a small logo block on the left, an optional center label, and a pair of circular contact or menu icons on the right. Beneath this header row, divide the remaining hero card area into two vertical halves: the left half holds a stacked text block with a heading, a short descriptive paragraph, and a call-to-action button placed directly below the text and aligned with the left text block; the right half contains a single product or cosmetic-related still-life image centered within its area. Directly below the hero card in the same left column, add a section titled with a phrase such as “Our Popular Brands,” followed by a subtitle if desired, and under this title area place a grid of six brand logos arranged in three rows and two columns, centered within the section. Below that, still in the left column, add another section titled with a phrase such as “Our Best Services,” containing a heading, a multi-line descriptive paragraph, and then a single call-to-action button centered horizontally beneath the paragraph; this section also spans the full width of the left column. In the middle column, create four service cards of equal width stacked vertically with clear separation between them. Each middle-column card follows the same internal structure: an image occupying the upper portion of the card and, along the bottom edge within the same card, a horizontal label row containing the service name text aligned to the left and a small circular arrow button aligned to the right. In the rightmost column, place two cards stacked vertically. The upper right card contains only a single portrait-style image centered within the card and does not include separate descriptive paragraphs or buttons. The lower right card is a text and button card: at its top, place a heading such as “What We Do,” below it several lines of descriptive paragraph text, and at the bottom a single call-to-action button centered horizontally under the text block. Ensure that the hero card in the left column, the topmost middle-column service card, and the upper right-column image card start at approximately the same vertical position under the top logo header, and ensure that all sections and cards remain confined to their respective columns without any card spanning across more than one column.

prompt: Design a UI illustration where the entire canvas is organized into three horizontal bands stacked vertically, each band divided into three main vertical columns of content. In the topmost band, place three equal-width informational cards aligned in a single row, one in each column; each card should contain a combination of heading text, a short descriptive sentence, and a simple geometric radius example, grouped as a single unit and centered within its column. Directly below, in the middle band, place a compact dashboard-style card in the left column that has a horizontal header row at the top with a title label on the left and a pill-shaped button on the right, and three list rows stacked vertically below it, each row containing a text label on the left, a status pill near the center, and a small flag icon aligned to the right. In the middle column of the middle band, place a tall rectangular photographic image card that fills most of the column width, and overlay near its lower center a chat-style speech bubble card containing an avatar circle on the left and two horizontal text lines on the right. In the right column of the middle band, place a vertical profile-and-stats card consisting of a top row with a small circular avatar and two text lines, a middle section with several horizontally aligned indicator bars stacked vertically, and a bottom row with a row of icon buttons; align this card with a second narrow card partially visible at the far right edge of the canvas, positioned at the same vertical level. In the bottom band, place in the left column a tall container card split into an upper and lower section, where the upper section is a 2 by 2 grid of compact person-row cards each containing a small avatar circle and adjacent text, and the lower section is a single horizontal process row with an icon label on the left connected by a flow line and pin marker to a destination label on the right. In the bottom band’s middle column, center a grid made of four horizontal rows and three vertical columns of square tiles, each tile containing a single country flag icon. In the bottom band’s right column, construct a composite layout where the left part of the column holds a vertical flow of three stacked horizontal cards linked by downward-pointing connectors: a top row that has a small icon-only card on the left and two rectangular label cards on the right in a single line, a middle card containing a horizontal list of three flag icons, and a lower card representing an audio player with a play or pause control and a timeline, and position a tall rectangular photographic image on the far right of this column, vertically centered and aligned so its top and bottom edges stay within the height of the flow cards to its left. Ensure all cards and grids are aligned to their respective columns so that the three-column structure is clearly visible across the three horizontal bands.

prompt: Create a web layout mockup consisting of two tall page panels positioned side by side within a single canvas, with both panels aligned at the top and each panel treated as its own vertical stack of sections. The left panel is a single-column landing page composed of multiple full-width sections stacked vertically. At the very top of the left panel, place a horizontal navigation header bar that spans the full panel width; inside this bar create three main groups arranged in one row: on the left, a compact branding group with a logo and site name; in the center, a horizontal list of several navigation links; on the right, a single prominent call-to-action button, with the three groups aligned left, center, and right respectively. Directly below this header, add a hero section split into two equal columns: the left column contains a text block with a multi-line main heading, a supporting paragraph, and a pair of call-to-action buttons arranged side by side in a single horizontal row directly under the paragraph, followed by a short single-line note underneath the buttons; the right column contains a large rectangular illustration that fills most of the column height. Immediately beneath the hero, still within the left panel, add a single horizontal strip containing four equal category buttons arranged in one row of four, centered horizontally and spanning most of the width of the panel. Below this strip, add a services area that begins with a brief heading and descriptive line followed by a card grid of four service cards laid out as two rows and two columns; each card is a rectangular container with a small icon or pictogram region on its left side and a short service title and description text on its right side, and all four cards are aligned in a consistent grid. Under the service grid, insert a full-width section divided into two columns: in the left column, place a paragraph introducing contact or inquiry information above a vertical stack of several short bullet or link items arranged in a single column, and put a small call-to-action button directly below this list; in the right column of this same section, place a single wide rectangular image that occupies most of the column’s height. At the bottom of the left panel, add a promotional band that also spans the full width of the left panel and is divided into two vertical columns: the left column contains a small line of promotional label text at the top, followed by a prominent heading, supporting paragraph, and possibly a brief list of benefits stacked vertically; the right column contains a tall rectangular promotional image aligned with the text column. The right panel contains additional sections of the same site, stacked vertically. At the top of the right panel, create a section composed of a single horizontal row of five equal step cards placed side by side; each step card contains a step number and a short explanatory line, and all five cards are evenly distributed across the panel width. Directly below this step row, add a two-column section: the left column includes a heading at the top with a rectangular embedded map image positioned directly beneath it, while the right column contains a heading at the top labeled for payment methods and, below this heading, a grid of six payment provider logos arranged as three rows and two columns within the column. Beneath this section, add another full-width section emphasizing professionalism and quality; at the top of this section place a centered heading spanning the entire width, and below the heading create a content row divided into two parts: the left part contains a feature grid of eight small icon-based cards arranged in two rows and four columns, while the right part holds a single tall rectangular image that matches the height of the two grid rows, so that the grid and the image appear side by side in one row. Finally, at the bottom of the right panel, create a section about upcoming services where the top of the section contains a left-aligned heading, immediately below it a multi-line paragraph of descriptive text spanning most of the panel width, and directly under the paragraph an icon grid consisting of eight service icons, arranged as two rows of four and centered horizontally within the section, each icon accompanied by a short label, forming the last content block of the overall composition.

prompt: Create a single-page website layout composed of several full-width horizontal sections stacked vertically within a centered content area. The topmost section combines a header and hero: at the very top, include a single-row navigation bar spanning the full width, with a logo aligned to the left and a compact group of menu and utility buttons aligned to the right. Directly below the navigation bar, in the same section, place a two-column hero area where the left column contains a vertical stack of text elements with a prominent multi-line headline at the top and a supporting paragraph below it, all left-aligned, while the right column contains a large product image panel; overlay or position a call-to-action button on the right side of this image panel so that the button visually sits in front of or immediately beside the product image but remains within the boundaries of the right column. Immediately below this hero section, add a full-width product showcase section with two main rows: the upper row holds a centered heading and a centered subheading stacked vertically, and the lower row contains three product cards arranged side-by-side in a single horizontal row centered on the page. Each of these three cards should share a similar vertical structure with rating or label information near the top, a main product or photo image in the middle, descriptive product text beneath, and a price badge attached toward the lower right area of each card, with the middle card positioned directly between the leftmost and rightmost cards. Further down, insert a features section that is a full-width horizontal band divided into two columns: the left column begins with a multi-line heading near its top and, beneath the heading, a vertical stack of exactly three horizontally oriented feature rows, each row containing a small icon area on the left and short descriptive text on the right; the right column contains a distinct call-to-action shape centered vertically relative to the stack of features, with a simple connecting line or arrow visually linking the feature list to this call-to-action. Below the features section, add a testimonial section that spans the full width and is organized into three vertical levels: the first level is a centered heading, the second level is a centered descriptive paragraph, and the third level is a large container that holds a grid of eight testimonial cards arranged in two rows and four columns. Each testimonial card inside this grid should contain a small image area at the top, name and location text in the middle, and rating indicators arranged along the bottom, with all cards sharing consistent alignment and spacing inside the single surrounding container. Underneath the testimonials, create another full-width section divided into two vertical columns of equal visual weight. prompt: Create a collage-style canvas composed of four vertical columns of equal width, each column containing a stack of rectangular slide thumbnails that represent pages of a brand presentation. In the leftmost column, place five slides stacked from top to bottom. The top slide is a simple title slide with a single centered line of text. Directly below it, add a slide where the brand symbol and wordmark occupy the lower-left region while supporting text sits in the upper-left, leaving the right side mostly open. The third slide in this column is a single row containing three equal-width vertical panels aligned side-by-side, each panel centered within the slide. Beneath that, place a slide where a single horizontal card is centered and slightly rotated within the slide, surrounded by empty margin. The bottom slide of the left column shows the brand symbol and wordmark centered within a visible construction grid of straight lines. In the second column from the left, also stack five slides. The top slide is divided into two equal-width vertical panels; the left panel contains a heading and several vertically stacked lines of copy, and the right panel contains multiple paragraphs of copy. Directly underneath, place a slide that centers the brand symbol and wordmark both horizontally and vertically. Below that, add a slide built as a horizontal row of three adjacent cards: the left card is the widest and contains a stacked list of short phrases, while the middle and right cards are narrower and contain text or an image each, all sharing a common baseline. Below this card row, place a slide showing a 2-by-3 grid of small logo specimens arranged in two horizontal rows and three columns. The bottom slide in the second column is a two-column composition with a rectangular photo block on one side and a stacked heading and paragraphs on the other side. The third column from the left also contains five slides in a vertical stack. Its top slide is split into two vertical panels, with a wider text block on the left and a narrower column of short phrases on the right. Directly beneath, include a slide divided into a 2-by-2 grid of rectangular panels that demonstrate different treatments of the brand mark and copy. Below this, add a large hero slide dominated by a photo of a person seated on a sofa using a laptop, with the brand mark overlaid near the center of the image. Under the hero, place a slide that is a clear two-column layout: a large rectangular image on the left and, on the right, a vertical stack of heading, supporting text, and a horizontal call-to-action button. The bottom slide in this third column is again a two-column layout where the left column contains two stacked text samples resembling type specimen blocks, and the narrower right column contains a vertically oriented heading placed toward the lower area. The rightmost column contains four vertically stacked slides. The top slide is divided into three equal-width vertical panels in a single row, each panel functioning as an individual card with its own text or image. Directly below, add a tall slide with a single column that contains three equal-height rectangles stacked vertically from top to bottom, with a small logo or text area aligned beneath them. The next slide down is a photo mosaic constructed as a grid of smaller rectangular photos arranged in at least two rows and three columns, filling most of the slide area. The bottom slide in the rightmost column is a tall, narrow text-focused panel organized as a single vertical column, beginning with a compact header area at the top and followed by multiple stacked blocks of text. Ensure that all slides align so that their left and right edges form four straight vertical alignment lines across the composition, that slides within each column share the same width, that no slides overlap, and that internal slide structures use simple combinations of single-column layouts, two-column layouts, three-column rows, and small content grids as described.

prompt: Create an image of a UI mockup displayed on a neutral background, where two tall desktop page designs are shown side by side as two main columns, left and right, aligned along their top edges. Each page is a single-column layout with content centered within its own page frame and with clear margins on both sides. The left page is divided vertically into three main sections stacked from top to bottom. The top section of the left page contains multiple separate rectangular service labels that form a loose cluster across the central upper area, appearing in roughly two staggered rows. Directly below this, the middle section of the left page presents a centered multi-line heading at the top, followed by a testimonial block in which five square or rectangular image containers are arranged as a cross-shaped cluster: one larger square image in the center with four smaller images positioned diagonally around it. Under this image cluster, place a centered block of testimonial quote text, and beneath the text add a small horizontal row of two square arrow buttons positioned side by side. The bottom section of the left page is a two-column band where a large portrait-oriented rectangular image occupies the left column and a text block occupies the right column. In the right column, stack a subheading at the top, a paragraph directly below it, then a primary call-to-action button beneath the paragraph, and place an email address immediately under this button; this entire text stack is left-aligned within the right column. The right page, on the other side of the canvas, consists of four vertically stacked structural areas: a navigation bar at the top, a hero section below it, a name banner beneath the hero section, and a projects section at the bottom. The top navigation bar spans the full content width and contains three functional groups arranged in a single horizontal row: a logo container aligned to the far left, a contact email positioned toward the upper right, and a compact menu button aligned to the far right, all sharing a common baseline. Directly under the navigation bar, the hero section is organized as a two-column layout where the left column contains text and the right column contains a large portrait-style rectangular image. In the left hero column, place a multi-line headline at the top, a descriptive paragraph directly beneath it, and a horizontal row of two call-to-action buttons sitting side by side below the paragraph. In the right hero column, place the large image aligned so its top is roughly level with the headline, and allow decorative circular text shapes to overlap partly behind this image. Immediately below the hero section, insert a full-width horizontal strip containing only a single prominent line of name text centered across the content width. Beneath this name strip, build the projects section, starting with a header row where a multi-line projects heading is left-aligned and, on the same horizontal line at the far right, a pill-shaped “All Projects” button is right-aligned. Directly under this header row, place at least one horizontal row of two project thumbnail containers arranged as two columns, each holding a rectangular project image; ensure these thumbnails align with the page content margins and sit clearly below the header row, with additional project content continuing further down the page as needed.

prompt: Create a fitness dashboard layout with a clean page background and a centered main canvas divided into three horizontal sections stacked vertically from top to bottom. In the top section, place three cards in a single horizontal row: on the far left, a tall hero card that is taller and wider than the other two, and to its right, two smaller cards of equal size aligned side-by-side. The left hero card is divided internally into a vertical stack of two areas: the upper area contains a left-aligned text block with a multi-line headline and a short descriptive paragraph beneath it, and the lower area features a single large image of a person lifting a barbell, centered horizontally and anchored near the bottom of the card. To the right of this hero card, create a heart-rate card on the left and an activity grid card on the right, both sharing the same height and aligned at their top and bottom edges. The heart-rate card uses a two-column internal layout: the left column holds a small icon row at the top, a label reading “Heart Rate” beneath it, and a large numeric bpm value below, all stacked vertically and left-aligned; the right column contains a cropped image of an athletic upper body centered within its column. The activity grid card to its right contains a uniform grid of eight equal-sized rounded tiles arranged in two rows and four columns; each tile has a centered icon with a label below it, and the tile in the top-right grid cell appears selected, with a pointer or cursor graphic positioned adjacent to that selected tile. Directly below the entire width of this top row, add a single wide analytics card that spans from the same left edge as the hero card to the same right edge as the activity grid card. Inside this analytics card, create two main vertical columns: the left column holds textual analytics and a line chart, and the right column holds an anatomical illustration. In the left column, place a title such as “Daily Exercise Score” at the top with a large percentage value beside or below it, then a small horizontal row of filter chips or toggles aligned near the top of the chart area, and beneath that a multi-point line chart running horizontally across most of the column width, including plotted points with small labels at several key positions. In the right column of this card, center a full-body human anatomy figure both vertically and horizontally, occupying most of the column height but leaving clear margins inside the card. Beneath this analytics card, construct the bottom section as a vertical stack of two identical user summary cards, each extending across the same full width as the analytics card and aligned to the same left and right edges. Each user summary card is a single horizontal row of grouped elements: at the far left, place a small circular avatar; immediately to its right, include a vertical stack with the user’s name on the first line and a smaller subtitle such as the current activity or day count on the second line; to the right of this text, add a circular progress indicator showing a percentage in its center; further to the right, insert a horizontal row of several compact stat groups, each composed of a small icon above or beside a numeric distance, time, or similar metric; and at the far right end of the card, align a rectangular Details button vertically centered with the other elements. Ensure that all cards are separated with clear spacing, that the left edges of all major cards line up, that the right edges line up, and that no elements overlap, producing a structured dashboard with a top multi-card row, a middle analytics card, and a bottom stack of two full-width user summary cards.

prompt: Create an image showing three separate tall web page mockups arranged side-by-side in a single horizontal row, representing mobile, tablet, and desktop layouts from left to right. Each mockup is an independent page bounded within its own vertical column. The leftmost mobile page is a single-column layout where all content is stacked vertically; at the top, place an introductory text section with a short heading above a brief paragraph, both left-aligned. Directly below this, add a product carousel section consisting of a single horizontal row that contains two product cards centered within the page; under this row, include a smaller horizontal row containing arrow-like navigation controls aligned beneath the cards. Below the carousel, place a call-to-action text block with a multi-line heading above a descriptive paragraph, both left-aligned within the same single column. The middle tablet page is also a primary single-column layout, but it contains internal grid structures. At the very top of this middle page, create a hero text section where a headline, a supporting line of text, and a single rectangular button are stacked vertically and center-aligned. Directly beneath this hero block, add a product section made of one horizontal row containing exactly three product cards of equal width; each card groups a product image on top with name, short description, and price below, all aligned consistently. Under this row, insert a two-column information section that spans the full width of the middle mockup: in the left column put a product-related visual block, and in the right column place a heading at the top followed by a vertical list of multiple bullet points or short lines of text; both columns are aligned along their own left edges. Below this two-column section, add a centered heading with a brief descriptive line of text beneath it, and then place a row of two feature cards directly below; these two cards are arranged side-by-side in a single horizontal row, aligned along their top edges and centered within the page, each card grouping an icon, a short title, and a description. The rightmost desktop page is a wider layout composed of four main stacked sections. At the top, draw a full-width navigation header bar formed by a single horizontal row: position a brand logo area on the far left, keep empty spacing in the middle, and group a menu trigger, a cart indicator, and a primary action button on the right, all aligned to the right edge of the header. Immediately below this header, create a hero banner that spans the width of the desktop mockup and is internally organized as a two-column layout in its upper area: place the main heading content occupying the left column and a large product imagery composition occupying the right column, with the text in the left column left-aligned and the visual in the right column centered within its half. Within this hero section, place a short descriptive paragraph beneath the main heading in the left column, and position two small circular callout elements around the main product imagery on the right side. Beneath the hero banner, add a product grid section consisting of a single horizontal row of exactly four evenly spaced product cards; each card groups a product image at the top with product name, brief description, and price below, and one of the cards additionally includes a small action button near its lower area. Directly above this four-card row, place a small section label line aligned with the grid. Finally, below the four-card product row, add a full-width text heading section consisting of a centered headline with a short supporting text block beneath it, reinforcing the theme of the page. Ensure that within each mockup all major sections are stacked vertically one above another, that rows and columns are clearly defined as described, and that the top hero headings of the middle and right mockups start at approximately the same vertical height to suggest consistent top spacing across the three-page presentation.

prompt: Create an image showing three website page mockups arranged on a single canvas: one tall homepage on the left and, on the right, a column of two shorter pages stacked one above the other, all aligned horizontally across the center of the canvas with even gaps between them. In the top hero section of the left page, place a full-width navigation bar along the very top edge; inside this bar, position a logo on the far left, a horizontal row of navigation links in the middle, and contact details aligned to the far right. Below the navigation bar within the same hero section, build a two-column layout: on the left column stack a large headline and supporting text, and on the right column place a rectangular form-style card. Inside this form card, add a small toggle row at the top, then several rows of form fields stacked vertically; each of these rows should contain exactly two input fields placed side-by-side, followed by a prominent full-width button near the bottom of the card. Below the hero on the left page, add the process section: at the top of this section center a heading and short description, and beneath it place a single row divided into three equal-width columns, each column showing one step; in every step column, stack an illustration at the top, a step title under it, and a short description at the bottom. At the bottom of the left page, create a full-width call-to-action banner that internally uses a two-column layout, with the left column containing a heading, brief explanatory text, and a button stacked vertically, and the right column containing a single car image. On the right side of the canvas, design two page mockups of equal width stacked vertically. The top-right page is organized as three main sections stacked from top to bottom. Its top section has a centered heading and short intro text above a row of four equal-width feature cards arranged horizontally; each feature card contains an icon at the top, a short title, a short description, and a link-style line at the bottom. Directly below this feature row, add a statistics section composed of a single horizontal row divided into four equal blocks; each block shows a large numeric value over a caption label. Beneath the statistics row, create a featured cars section with a centered heading and, below it, a single row of three equal-width car cards placed side-by-side; inside each card, place a car image at the top, followed by the car title and details, and then a bottom area with price and small meta information. prompt: Create a single canvas organized as a two-column layout, where the left column is divided into two stacked rectangular sections and the right column is a single tall section that spans the entire height of the canvas. In the top section of the left column, place a large rectangular hero area that contains a laptop device centered horizontally; within the laptop screen, divide the content into two internal vertical columns so that the left internal column holds heading text and a call-to-action button and the right internal column holds a compact grid of nine small cards arranged as three columns and three rows, each card showing either a simple icon, a numeric statistic, a short label, a chart line, or a small image. Directly below this hero area, in the bottom section of the left column, create another large rectangular section whose main content is a central cluster of overlapping cards: position a prominent rectangular statistic card in the center foreground, place a vertical profile-style card immediately to its left overlapping part of its left edge, and add a third, narrower rectangular card behind and slightly higher than the central card so only its upper portion is visible, then overlap a pill-shaped brand label near the lower right edge of the central statistic card. Beneath the left side of this overlapping cluster, add a horizontal row of four small circular social media icons aligned in a line, ensuring the entire cluster and icon row are centered within the bottom-left section. The entire right column should be a continuous tall mosaic composed of multiple rectangular and square tiles arranged into three vertical columns and five horizontal rows, with consistent spacing between tiles; vary the tile heights so that some span more vertical space than others, but keep them aligned to the implicit three-column grid. Populate these right-column tiles with a mix of card types including statistic cards that display growth percentages and supporting text, logo or brand cards, product or object photos, profile-style photo cards, and at least one rectangular tile containing a centered circular emblem with surrounding text, maintaining the structural grid and clear separation between each tile. Ensure the left column’s two sections align vertically with one another and sit flush against the continuous right-column mosaic so that the overall page maintains a clear two-column structure with stacked content on the left and a single uninterrupted grid of cards on the right.

prompt: Design a web-style page layout organized around a clear hierarchy of sections and cards. At the very top of the canvas, place a single full-width line of headline text centered horizontally, acting as the topmost header. Directly beneath this header, create a main content band divided into two vertical columns: a narrow left column and a noticeably wider right column, both centered within the page. In the left column, stack two tall rounded-rectangle cards vertically so they share the same left and right edges; the top card contains a large numeric metric with supporting text, and the bottom card is another tall card with overlaid text and a small circular arrow button positioned near its upper right corner. In the wider right column, build a vertical stack of two major groups: a large hero card on top and a bottom row of cards below it, with both groups aligned to the same left and right edges. The hero card itself is a large rounded rectangle split into two internal columns placed side-by-side: a left text column and a right media column that are top-aligned. In the left text column of the hero card, place a small logo-like icon at the upper left, a large multi-line headline directly beneath it, and then two article-style feature rows stacked vertically under the headline; each feature row comprises a title line, a short descriptive line below it, and a small circular arrow button aligned on the far right end of the row. In the right media column of the hero card, add a navigation row at the top containing four textual menu links arranged in a horizontal line, followed by two circular icon buttons grouped at the far right of that line; directly below this navigation row, fill the remaining space with a grid of rounded-rectangle image tiles arranged on a three-column by three-row grid, where all tiles align to common vertical and horizontal grid lines and the bottom-left tile spans across the first and second columns while occupying only a single row, with the remaining tiles occupying single grid cells. Beneath the hero card, still within the broader right column and aligned with it, create a bottom row of cards divided into three vertical segments placed side-by-side. The left segment is one medium-height informational card, and the center segment is another medium-height call-to-action card; both share the same height and are aligned along their top edges. The rightmost segment functions as a nested subcolumn that matches the height of the neighboring cards and contains two smaller rounded-rectangle cards stacked vertically, with the top smaller card featuring a prominent title and a pill-shaped button centered beneath its text and the lower smaller card containing a brief call-to-action line with a pill-shaped button aligned toward its right side. Ensure that the left sidebar column forms a continuous vertical strip from just below the top header down to near the bottom of the page, always remaining to the left of the hero card and the bottom row of cards, so that the overall composition reads as a top header, a middle two-column area with a sidebar and hero, and a lower band of cards in the right column.

prompt: Create a composite web-design mockup where the entire canvas is divided into two vertical panels: a wider left primary webpage and a narrower right column of stacked preview sections, both aligned along the same top edge. In the left panel, build a single-page layout with multiple sections stacked vertically. At the very top of this left column, place a full-width navigation bar composed of one horizontal row that contains the brand name aligned to the far left, several navigation links arranged to the right of it, and a small utility icon or control aligned to the far right. Directly below this navigation bar, add a hero section that fills the width of the left column and is split into two vertical columns: in the left hero column, stack a large multi-line headline, a short supporting paragraph beneath it, a prominent primary call-to-action button below the paragraph, and then a horizontal row of exactly three pill-shaped category buttons under the main button; in the right hero column, place a tall rectangular portrait photograph centered within that column. Overlay or position near the upper right of this hero area a single horizontal search input bar that stretches from near the right edge toward the center above the main body content. Immediately below the hero section, add a narrow full-width strip that reads as an information ticker, composed of repeated short phrases or taglines separated by small markers, all contained in a single horizontal row. Beneath this strip, create a feature section where the upper part is a centered text block containing a small label tag, a prominent section heading, and a short descriptive paragraph, and the lower part forms a four-column grid of feature cards: in this lower row, place four equal rectangular cards arranged side-by-side across the width, each card containing an icon at the top, a short title beneath it, and a block of supporting text at the bottom. Ensure these left-column sections remain vertically ordered without overlap. In the narrower right panel, construct a vertical stack of five distinct preview sections, each filling the width of the right column. The topmost preview is a compact hero-style layout split into two vertical columns, with a portrait photograph occupying the left column and a vertical stack of heading, descriptive text, and call-to-action button in the right column. Directly beneath it, add a product listing preview that has two main horizontal bands: an upper band with a large heading and small interface controls centered or slightly offset, and a lower band forming a single row of four equal product cards placed side-by-side, each card containing an image area, a price label above the image, and a title below. Below this, insert a two-panel information section that is a single horizontal row divided into two equal blocks, each block centered within its half and containing a short label and a heading. Under that, design a brand emphasis section that is a two-column layout where the left column prominently displays a large brand name word occupying most of the space, while the right column holds a smaller arrangement featuring a portrait photograph near the upper part and a compact text block with a secondary call-to-action grouped beneath it. At the bottom of the right panel, place an advice or consultation section that again uses a two-column structure, with a text-focused column on the left containing a heading, descriptive paragraph, and optional small profile or testimonial elements, and a portrait photograph on the right column; ensure all five preview sections in the right column are stacked vertically with clear separation and no overlapping, and that both main columns maintain consistent left alignment within their respective widths.

prompt: Create a single-page web layout consisting of a top header bar and a large hero section below it. The header is a full-width horizontal bar at the top with one row of elements: at the far left place a simple hamburger menu icon, toward the center place a horizontal group of two navigation links, at the exact center place a brand name wordmark, to its right place another horizontal group of two navigation links, and at the far right place a single small circular icon. Directly beneath this header, fill the rest of the page with a two-column hero area. Divide this hero area into a left column and a right column of equal visual importance. At the top of the left column, add a vertically stacked text block with a multi-line heading reading “BEAUTY COSMETICS & SERUM” and directly below it a shorter single-line subtitle about delivery time; position a small decorative symbol slightly off to the right side of this heading block. In the lower portion of the left column, add a horizontal row containing two elements: on the left place a large vertical rectangular product image of a cosmetic serum bottle aligned with the left column edge, and to its right place a vertical stack of a large “100%” percentage text with a smaller label such as “Organic product” underneath, both aligned to each other. In the right column, center near the top a tall rectangular image placeholder that occupies most of the upper half of this column. To the right side of this large rectangle, around its upper-middle height, place a smaller circular product image, and directly to the right of this circle place a short multi-line caption describing suitable skin types. Below the large rectangular image, in the lower central area of the right column, place a large circular product image centered horizontally. Attach a smaller solid circular badge to the upper right edge of this large circle so that it overlaps, and inside the badge place a numeric label such as “4.6K Products.” Along the extreme right edge of the hero section, spanning alongside the lower half of the right column, place a narrow vertical strip containing four small circular avatar images stacked one above the other, and directly below this stack add a single-line text label indicating the total number of reviews, such as “10.8K+ reviews.” Ensure all text elements are left-aligned within their respective blocks, the columns are clearly separated, and all described elements remain contained within the hero area beneath the header.

prompt: Create a composite image that shows two tall screenshots of the same single-page marketing website arranged side-by-side as two equal-width vertical columns, with the earlier sections of the page in the left column and the later sections in the right column. In the left column, place a topmost header-and-hero section composed of two stacked rows: the upper row is a navigation bar that spans the full width of the left column, containing a logo aligned on the left, several navigation links arranged in a horizontal line in the middle, and a call-to-action button aligned on the right; the lower row is the hero area, where a large multi-line headline, a short descriptive paragraph, and a main call-to-action button are stacked vertically in the upper half, and a wide product photo of generic supplement bottles sits directly below this text block, centered within the same column. Directly under this hero, in the middle of the left column, add a two-column section with a single content row: in the left column of this row, place a large vertically oriented panel featuring a rounded or circular container that holds a heading, an explanatory paragraph, and two horizontally aligned buttons grouped near the bottom; in the right column of this row, place a large circular product image panel; between these two panels, run a narrow vertical or slightly curved strip of rotated text connecting the top and bottom edges of the row, and place one horizontal caption line that spans across the entire width of this section along its lower edge beneath both panels. Beneath that, create a bottom-left product section: first a row containing a centered section title that spans the width of the left column, and below it a second row that is split into two vertical columns; in this lower row, the left column contains a large circular image of several generic supplement bottles, while the right column contains a stacked text block with a subheading, a descriptive paragraph, a price line, and a prominent buy button, with a short horizontal row of small circular avatar images and a rating label placed near the lower right of this text column. In the right column of the composite canvas, start at the top with a two-row testimonial section: the first row contains a left-aligned multi-line heading and a short tagline, and the second row directly beneath it contains at least four rounded testimonial cards arranged side-by-side in a single horizontal line, each card including a short quote, a name, and a rating element. Below this, construct a wide FAQ section consisting of two stacked rows: the first row is a full-width heading centered or slightly right of center asking what visitors want to know, and the second row is divided into three vertical columns; in this second row, the left column contains a brief explanatory text block, the central column holds one large centered image element, and the right column holds a vertical stack of four accordion-style FAQ bars, each bar spanning the width of this right column and containing a question with an expand or collapse indicator, stacked one above another. Under the FAQ section, add a centered initiative section in the right column with two rows: the top row is a single-line heading describing an initiative for gut health, and the bottom row is a short paragraph of explanatory text followed by a centered line containing a small social media icon and a handle or invitation to join. Finally, at the very bottom of the right column, create a horizontal band-like social media promotion section: along the far left edge, place a vertical strip of text reading a repeated “Follow us on social media” phrase; across the central area of this band, arrange three circular product photos of supplement bottles in a loose horizontal row from left to right; and have the same phrase “Follow us on social media” run along two curved paths that wrap around these circles, one curved text path placed above the row of circles and another mirrored curved text path below them, all contained within this final bottom band.

prompt: Create a single-page layout with a main rectangular container centered within the canvas; inside this container, arrange content in a vertical stack with a header block at the top and a feature grid section beneath it. The header block spans the full width of the container and consists of three vertically stacked text rows: at the very top of the header block place a narrow label row containing a small icon on the left and a short label text on its right, both aligned in a single horizontal row; directly below the label row place a multi-word main heading line of text, left-aligned with the label row; directly below the main heading place a single short descriptive paragraph, also left-aligned with the heading. Beneath the header block, occupying the rest of the container width, place a feature cards section structured as a two-column by two-row grid of equal-width cards, forming four cards total; the first card is in the top-left cell, the second card is in the top-right cell, the third card is in the bottom-left cell directly below the first, and the fourth card is in the bottom-right cell directly below the second and directly to the right of the third. All cards share the same internal structure: inside each card, stack elements vertically, starting with a wide illustration or interface mockup area at the top, followed immediately by a feature title line of text below it, then a short descriptive paragraph below the title, and finally a call-to-action line of text such as “Learn more in Docs” at the bottom of the card; all text inside each card is left-aligned to a common text column, and no additional columns or sidebars appear to the left or right of this 2x2 grid within the main container.

prompt: Create an image showing two tall web page mockups side-by-side on a single canvas, with a left page representing a product listing view and a right page representing a detailed product view. Both pages should start at the same top level and end at approximately the same bottom level. On the left page, place a full-width header bar at the very top, divided into three horizontal zones: a logo aligned at the far left, a row of navigation links centered, and a small group of utility icons aligned at the far right. Directly below this header, add a hero section that spans the full width of the left page and is divided into three vertical columns in a single row: the left column contains a rectangular portrait-style image, the center column contains a vertically stacked block of large heading text followed by a call-to-action button, and the right column holds a taller rectangular product image; these three items should be clearly grouped as a single hero section. Beneath the hero, add a “Best Selling” section: first a heading row containing the section title aligned to the left and a small “Shop all” link aligned to the right on the same baseline, then directly below this row a grid of product cards arranged in two rows and three columns, forming six cards in total. Each product card is contained within its own box and uses a vertical stack of elements: a top meta row with small icons and rating information, a central product image, and a bottom text block containing product name, vendor line, and price; the cards are aligned so that the two rows form straight horizontal lines and the three columns form straight vertical lines. On the right page, create a top section that also spans the page width but is divided into two columns: a narrower left column and a wider right column. In the left column, stack from top to bottom a product title, a short descriptive paragraph, a small horizontal row containing a “Shop all” style link and simple indicator icon, and beneath that a compact product card with a small image, product title, vendor line, and price. In the right column of this top section, place one large vertical product image aligned with the top of the left column content. Below this entire top section on the right page, add a “Specifications” section structured as a two-column layout: a slim index strip on the left and a wide content area on the right. In the left index strip, stack several numbers vertically (such as 01 through 05) aligned to form a single straight vertical line, with each number horizontally corresponding to a specification item in the right column. In the right content column, place a heading reading “Specifications” at the top, then below it an area of expanded details including at least one subgroup for certifications and one for product description. Within the open product description subgroup, arrange product characteristics in a simple table where each row has a property label in the left cell and a corresponding value in the right cell; include a product image within this expanded area, positioned alongside or beneath the table but clearly grouped with it. Beneath the expanded detail area in the same right column, add a vertical list of additional specification categories represented as four separate rows; each row contains a left-aligned category label (such as features, accessories, bowl, dimensions) and a right-aligned icon indicating that the row can be expanded. Ensure all specification section titles in the right column align along a single vertical axis, all interactive icons align consistently at the right edge of their rows, and the entire composition reads as two related but distinct page layouts presented side-by-side.

continue downward with a pricing section whose white background is uninterrupted, starting with a black regular-weight sans-serif line reading 'The prices you wished for are finally here!' above three product cards, and somewhere near the upper right of this section include a horizontal pill-shaped category filter segmented into three adjacent pills labeled 'Bedroom', 'Livingroom', and 'Outdoor', where the 'Livingroom' pill is filled with dark charcoal and has white text to show it as active while the other two pills have light gray outlines, white interiors, and dark gray text; prompt: Create a high-resolution mockup of a desktop e-commerce homepage for a pet store, shown inside a large white browser window centered on a solid pale beige canvas, with the browser window drawn as a wide rounded rectangle casting a subtle soft shadow. Inside the top-left of the browser frame, place three tiny horizontally aligned circles representing window controls: left muted red, middle muted amber, and right muted green, all solid fill with no borders. Below this, on a white header background, put a bright green pet store wordmark in bold rounded sans-serif on the far left, a long white search bar with a light gray outline and fully rounded ends centered horizontally, and small dark gray line icons with labels for sign in and a shopping basket on the right; the search bar should contain light gray sans-serif placeholder text such as “Search pet store” and a dark gray magnifying glass icon at its left. Directly beneath this header, add a white navigation row with exactly five navigation items arranged from left to right—“Shop”, “My pets”, “Bookings”, “Support”, and “Find us”—each drawn as a small thin-line dark gray icon paired with a short dark gray sans-serif label, evenly spaced with no dividers. Immediately below the navigation row, span the full width with a thin horizontal strip filled with solid dark forest green; inside this strip place three offer sentences in white sans-serif, left, center, and right aligned, and ensure that specific key phrases in each sentence are colored bright green and underlined while the rest remains plain white, all using a solid underline style. Under the strip, on the left of the main content, write the phrase “shop for” in a large dark forest green serif font. To the right of this heading, position two pill-shaped filter buttons aligned horizontally: the left pill labeled “All pet types” with a white fill, light gray border, and dark gray sans-serif text, and the right pill labeled “My pets” styled as the active state with a solid bright green fill, no visible border, and white sans-serif text; both pills should have fully rounded ends. Just below the heading area on the left, show exactly two small pet avatar chips: each chip is a short rounded rectangle with a pale green fill, containing a tiny circular pet photo on the left and a pet name (“Bruno” and “Coco”) in dark green sans-serif on the right. Across the page beneath these controls, create a horizontal row of seven large category tiles, each centered on a white background: render each tile as a circle with no border sitting above a text label. The leftmost circle must be a solid bright green circle containing the stacked white words “Pets” and “Club” in bold sans-serif; the next five circles must all be solid pale beige circles, each containing a photographic cutout related to dogs (a bowl of dry kibble, a small stack of bone-shaped treats, a green and white medicine box, a dog lying in a blue and gray bed, and a brown plush toy), and the rightmost seventh circle must be a dark green circle partially cropped by the right edge of the browser window, suggesting an additional “More categories” tile. Beneath each of the seven circles, include a short dark green sans-serif label such as “Dog Offers”, “Food”, “Treats”, “Treatments”, “Beds”, “Toys”, and a truncated “More categories” under the cropped circle. Leave ample white space between circles and labels, and keep all label text the same small size. Below this band, add another left-aligned heading reading “New and featured” in the same dark forest green serif typeface as “shop for” but slightly smaller. Under this heading, align four equal-sized promotional cards in a single row, each card drawn as a rounded rectangle with no border and a slight separation from the white background: the first card on the left uses a light cream and pale green color scheme with a product image of cat food on the right, a short black or dark green sans-serif headline about a delightful cat’s dinner on the left, and a bright green corner badge on the right stating “25% off” in white; the second card directly beside it has a predominantly deep blue background gradient from darker blue on the left to lighter blue on the right, featuring fantasy wizard–themed imagery and white sans-serif text; the third card has a pale blue gradient background with a pet collar product photo and white and dark blue sans-serif text describing a new collection; and the fourth card, partially cropped at the right edge of the browser window, uses a dark red festive background with holiday pet products and contrasting light-colored text, indicating a seasonal promotion. Maintain consistent margins on all sides of the browser window, use a combination of bold serif headings and regular sans-serif body text as described, and keep all lines and icon strokes solid and relatively thin for a clean, modern interface appearance.

prompt: Create a high-resolution UI mockup of a roofing services website displayed as a large desktop browser page on the left and a vertical column of three smaller section mockups on the right, all centered on a light beige outer background with soft drop shadows. On the right side of this section, place two overlapping rectangular photos of roofing work with bright daylight colors and safety equipment, the larger photo offset slightly behind the smaller one, and overlay a solid orange circular badge near the upper-right of the image cluster containing centered white sans-serif text such as “25+ Years Of Experience.” Beneath this, create a white “Trusted Brands We Work With” section with a centered bold black sans-serif heading and a single horizontal row of several simple gray wordmark logos beneath it. Near the lower portion of the main mockup, add a wide dark banner with a black or very dark gray background and white sans-serif text encouraging users to get their roof ready, with the headline left-aligned, a short supporting line below it, and two pill-shaped call-to-action buttons in a row—one solid vivid orange with white text and one darker inverted or outlined style button beside it; at the right end of this banner, place a construction worker in safety gear integrated into the composition. At the very bottom, build a full-width footer with a pure black background and small light gray and white sans-serif text, containing three or more columns: one column with the logo and brief description, another with a list of top links, and another with working hours, all separated by spacing only and accented by small orange icons or bullets. The middle-right mockup displays a FAQ section on a pale yellow background with an orange eyebrow label, a centered bold black “Frequently Asked Questions” heading, and eight white horizontal accordion items arranged as two columns of four rows; each accordion row is a pill-like rounded rectangle with black question text on the left and a small dark circular icon containing a white plus-like symbol on the right. The bottom-right mockup has an upper white heading area followed by a light gray content area that features three equal-width article cards aligned side by side; each card is a rounded rectangle with a photographic thumbnail on its upper half and text on colored lower strips, with one card’s text strip in vivid orange and the other strips in more muted tones, and each card including bold sans-serif titles and smaller metadata; beneath these cards, add another dark CTA banner similar in style to the one in the main mockup, with a dark background, white text, and a vivid orange pill-shaped button. Ensure all headings across the design are bold sans-serif, all body text is regular-weight sans-serif, all prominent call-to-action buttons share the same pill-shaped silhouette with rounded ends, and no visible borders are used except for soft shadows and spacing to separate elements.

prompt: Create a tall, full-page vertical web landing screen with a solid white background and a clean modern layout for a fortune-telling service called 'For Teller'; at the very top, add a full-width white header bar with the 'For Teller' logo on the left rendered in a dark gray cursive script with a small triangular emblem, a centered-right navigation row reading HOME, TYPES, FAQ, CONTACT in uppercase sans-serif where the HOME item is a pill-shaped button filled with solid teal green and white text while the others are medium gray text links without underlines, and on the far right a compact language selector showing one circular red-white-blue flag icon followed by an orange 'EN' label and a tiny down-arrow; below the hero, center a section header where a small light-gray uppercase sans-serif caption 'TYPES OF DIVINATION' sits above a larger mixed-weight sans-serif title 'Fortune Teller TYPES' with 'Fortune Teller' in black and 'TYPES' in lighter gray, followed by a single light-gray descriptive sentence, and beneath this title position a grid of exactly six service cards arranged in two rows of three, each card being a soft light cool-gray rounded rectangle with a subtle shadow, a white interior and slightly darker border, containing at the top center a dark gray minimalist line-art icon with thin strokes, under it a dark gray bold sans-serif title and below that a smaller lighter-gray sans-serif description; prompt: Create a wide 16:9 image on a clean white background that displays three tall modern landing-page mockups for a natural supplement brand arranged side by side from left to right, with the rightmost mockup slightly wider than the others; use a consistent color palette of dark olive green as the primary background color, bright lemon-yellow as an accent, and white, off-white, light beige, and light gray for content areas, with all text in black or very dark gray using a clean sans-serif typeface where major headings are bold, condensed, and all-uppercase while body text is lighter and regular weight. In the rightmost mockup, place at the top a full-width dark olive green hero banner: centered near its upper edge, add a small lowercase brand wordmark in bright yellow, and to the upper right include a compact white pill-shaped “Menu” button outlined with a thin gray stroke, containing dark gray sans-serif text and a tiny two-line hamburger icon; beneath this, set a large multi-line headline in heavy uppercase sans-serif where some words are bright yellow and the rest white, aligned left; under the headline, add a short white sentence in smaller regular sans-serif. On the right side of this hero, position a light beige rounded rectangle card slightly rotated clockwise that carries a photo-realistic matte green cylindrical supplement container with a slightly darker lid shown in three-quarter view, standing in front of a matching green rectangular box, with six to eight glossy brown capsules scattered near the base; cast a soft shadow under the beige card to separate it from the dark green background. Overlapping the right side of the hero, add a bright lemon-yellow horizontally oriented pill-shaped call-to-action badge with no gradient, containing centered bold black sans-serif text like “Shop Now,” and draw a thin white curved arrow with a small arrowhead pointing from the badge toward the product; include two additional thin white curved doodle lines behind the product for visual interest. Below this hero in the rightmost mockup, create a white content section: at the top, place a bold uppercase black heading about elevating the body with supplements and a short gray paragraph centered underneath; in the lower portion of this section, align three equal-width product cards in a horizontal row, each card being a white rounded rectangle with subtle gray drop shadow, a beige cylindrical supplement container rendered in soft photo style and tilted slightly, a “5.0” rating label accompanied by a solid black five-point star near the top, and, overlapping the lower right of each container, a solid green circular price badge with a small shadow and centered bold white text; ensure the right two badges clearly read “$40” and “$80.” Use bold uppercase sans-serif titles under each container such as “Daily Multivitamin” and “Gut Balance,” with smaller gray subtext below. In the center mockup, give the top region a dark olive banner with a mixed white and yellow uppercase heading about organic or pure nutrition, followed by a short paragraph in white; beneath it, insert a large white section whose heading in black uppercase announces nourishing the body and whose opening paragraph is gray and centered; inside this section, place a very light gray rounded rectangle spanning most of the width, and within it arrange six white testimonial cards in a 3-by-2 grid on a slightly darker gray inset area, each card being a small rounded rectangle with a thin light gray border, a circular avatar placeholder at the top, a dark gray name and location, and a horizontal row of five small golden-yellow filled star icons below the name. Directly under this testimonial area, build a dark olive green FAQ section: give it a bold uppercase heading where one keyword is bright yellow and the rest white, then stack four full-width accordion bars with pill-shaped rectangles; make three of these bars dark olive with white text and the right-aligned chevron icons in white, and highlight one central bar in bright lemon-yellow with black text and a black chevron to indicate it is expanded. Beneath this FAQ, include a lower promotional card on an off-white rounded rectangle showing a green supplement pouch and text about energy and focus, keeping all typography consistent with the rest of the page. In the leftmost mockup, at the very top, design a white section with two vertically stacked testimonial cards on a faint gray backdrop, each card a white rounded rectangle with soft shadow, containing a circular avatar placeholder, a dark gray name and location, and a row of five small golden-yellow stars; directly below, add a large dark olive green section whose heading mixes white and bright yellow uppercase words about organic ingredients, and on the left side of this section align three horizontally oriented rounded-rectangle feature pills in a lighter olive tone, stacked vertically with even spacing, each pill containing a small yellow circle icon on the left and one or two lines of white sans-serif text on the right describing premium ingredients, science-driven formulas, and easy daily wellness; on the right side of this section position a rotated square with fully rounded corners (appearing as a diamond) filled with bright yellow and containing centered white sans-serif text like “About Us,” connected by a thin white line to surrounding copy. In the lower part of the left mockup, place an off-white card with a photo-realistic matte green stand-up supplement pouch and subtle shadow, and another card with a white dish holding beige powder, reflecting product variety. Finish the left mockup with a full-width dark olive green footer bar at the bottom containing small yellow and white navigation links in sans-serif and three evenly spaced small solid yellow circular social media icons with white glyphs, ensuring all lines are solid, all corners consistently rounded across buttons and cards, and spacing between sections is even so that the three mockups share a coherent, grid-aligned visual system.
