prompt: Create a high-resolution UI mockup of a modern logistics company website landing experience, displayed as two tall web pages side by side, each rendered as a large rounded-rectangle panel centered on a light neutral-gray background with soft shadows under the panels. The left page has a dark teal-to-navy horizontal gradient navigation bar at the top containing a white circular icon logo and “GLOBAL” brand text in bold uppercase sans-serif on the left, seven evenly spaced white navigation links in medium-weight sans-serif in the center, and a pill-shaped button on the far right with a white fill, thin navy outline, and navy sans-serif text reading “CONTACT US.” Below this, the left hero section shows a full-width photograph of a container ship at sea at sunset, overlaid with a semi-transparent gradient that transitions from teal on the left to warm orange on the right to improve legibility; on the left side of this image, place a large white uppercase sans-serif heading about the confluence of technology and logistics, a smaller regular-weight white paragraph below it, and then two buttons: a primary bright green rounded-rectangle button with centered white uppercase sans-serif text reading “BOOK SHIPPING,” and to its right a secondary pill-shaped button with transparent interior, thin white outline, and white text reading “SUPPLY CHAIN.” Near the lower-left of the hero, overlay a small horizontally oriented testimonial card with a semi-transparent dark teal fill, fully rounded corners, subtle shadow, tiny circular thumbnail images on the left, and small white sans-serif text describing client logistics experience. The next section on the left page has a white background with a centered or left-aligned heading in dark navy bold uppercase sans-serif reading “SERVICES OF OUR GLOBAL LOGISTIC WORLD SUPPLY CHAIN” and a short medium-gray paragraph below in regular sans-serif; beneath this heading, align exactly three equal-sized large rounded-rectangle service cards in a single row with equal spacing: the left card has a solid green background with a photo of a loaded container ship and the label “SEA FREIGHT” in white uppercase sans-serif near its bottom-left, the central card shows a truck on a road over a green ground with the label “LAND FREIGHT” in white uppercase near the bottom-left and includes a small solid black circular play icon with a white triangular play symbol near its top-right corner, and the right card has a light sky-blue gradient background with a climbing airplane photo and the label “AIR FREIGHT” in white uppercase near the bottom-left. Below this row, create a split section: on the left, a white area with a dark navy bold uppercase sans-serif heading “GLOBAL LOGISTIC ENABLES ANY COMPANIES TO EXPAND,” a multi-line medium-gray regular sans-serif paragraph, and two horizontally aligned controls—a bright yellow rounded-rectangle button labeled “ABOUT GLOBAL” in dark navy sans-serif and a secondary minimal-link style button labeled “LEARN MORE” in dark navy text without a heavy border; on the right, place a vertically oriented white stats card with fully rounded corners and soft drop shadow showing the large dark navy number “3000+” in bold sans-serif, a smaller gray line of text about experienced employees below it, and a thin gray line icon of a person connected to several nodes near the bottom. Further down on the left page, include another white rounded-rectangle card showing a chest-up photographic image of a logistics worker wearing a bright neon yellow safety vest over a red shirt and an orange hard hat, centered within the card against a pale gray background, with the card styled with the same soft drop shadow and rounded corners as the other cards. On the right page, at the top, fade in a very light gray world map silhouette as a watermark behind a slim white stats block that stretches across the content width; inside this block, on the left, display dark navy sans-serif text including the large bold number “18,000+” and a short description in lighter-weight text about worldwide delivery shipping, and on the right, show an illustration of a blue shipping container being lifted by a crane. Below this, add a prominent hero card: a wide, horizontally oriented rounded rectangle featuring a crisp photo of stacked multicolored cargo containers under a blue sky, tinted overall with a cool blue-purple gradient; overlapping the left side of this card is a vertically oriented white rounded-rectangle booking form casting a small shadow, with two pill-shaped toggle buttons at the top (one active state filled in blue-purple with white text and one inactive state with only a light outline and dark text), several stacked input fields with light gray outlines and dark gray labels, and at the bottom a bright green rounded-rectangle button matching the green on the left hero and containing centered white uppercase sans-serif text “BOOK SHIPPING.” Beneath the hero card, on a white background, place a left-aligned section heading in dark navy bold uppercase sans-serif reading “THE MOST FREQUENTLY QUESTIONS OF LOGISTICS.” with a short explanatory paragraph in regular sans-serif, and under this heading stack exactly four FAQ items as full-width pill-shaped accordion bars with soft shadows: three of them are white rounded rectangles with dark gray regular sans-serif question text on the left and small plus/minus or chevron icons on the right, while one special FAQ is a composite highlight card consisting of a wide rounded rectangle horizontally split into a small square white left segment containing an orange forklift illustration with a cardboard box, and a larger dark navy right segment with a bold white sans-serif question title and smaller white explanatory copy. Further down, build the “OUR MOST RECENT GUIDE PROFESSIONALS ARTICLES” section: on the right side, show this heading in dark navy bold uppercase sans-serif over a column of article titles in smaller regular dark gray sans-serif, accompanied by exactly two small rectangular thumbnail images with rounded corners—one thumbnail showing a cargo truck and the other an airplane—aligned beside the text; on the left side of this section, place a tall feature card with fully rounded corners and a soft shadow that contains a vivid underwater photograph of a cargo ship in deep blue water, with a dark blue text overlay strip along the bottom spanning the card’s width and containing a short white bold sans-serif title and smaller white sans-serif description about sea transportation. Conclude the right page with a full-width dark navy rectangular footer that has rounded lower corners consistent with the page container and no upper curvature, inside which the brand logo appears on the left above two small circular social media icons filled with bright green and white glyphs, while three columns of vertically stacked navigation links titled “OUR COMPANY,” “QUICK LINKS,” and “HELP CENTER” in white bold uppercase sans-serif sit to the right, each column listing smaller regular sans-serif white or light gray link labels, and along the very bottom a thin light gray copyright line is centered; ensure all headings across both pages use bold uppercase sans-serif fonts, all body copy uses lighter-weight sans-serif, every card and button has fully rounded corners with subtle drop shadows, and the palette consistently features navy blue, teal, bright green, bright yellow accents, white, light gray, and cool blue-purple tints in a clean flat design without heavy textures or 3D effects.

prompt: Create a slide-like canvas with a clear structural layout centered around a vertical flowchart and accompanying right-side text. At the very top of the canvas, place a single-line title text centered horizontally, serving as the header. Below this title, in the upper central area, place one rectangular box that represents the top output unit; this box should be horizontally centered and form the first element of the flowchart. Directly beneath this box, still in the central column, place a second rectangular box that is wider than the others so that it visually spans across the space above three columns of boxes to come; this second box forms a horizontal bar for units and is aligned centrally under the top box. Immediately below this wide box, create a single row of three equal-sized rectangular boxes arranged side-by-side from left to right; these boxes represent learned encodings, and they should share the same vertical position and be evenly distributed across the width of the wide box above. Directly beneath this row, create another row of three equal-sized rectangular boxes aligned vertically with the ones above so that each lower box is centered under one upper encoding box; these lower boxes represent indices and form the bottom layer of the central flowchart. Between each vertically adjacent pair of boxes, add a single vertical arrow: one arrow from each bottom index box to the encoding box directly above it, one arrow from each encoding box to the wide units box above, and one arrow from the wide units box up to the single top output box, with each arrow centered along the vertical axis between its source and target box. To the right of the upper part of the central flowchart, position a separate text block in the upper right quadrant of the canvas; this block should consist of multiple lines of explanatory text arranged as a single left-aligned paragraph that does not overlap the central boxes. At the very bottom right corner of the canvas, beneath both the right-side paragraph and the central boxes, place a small multi-line note about candidate words, left-aligned, and immediately adjacent to this note at the extreme lower right corner place a small page number. Ensure the overall structure clearly shows a header at the top, a centrally aligned four-level vertical flowchart of boxes and arrows in the middle (one box, then one wide box, then a row of three boxes, then another row of three boxes), a descriptive paragraph in the upper right beside the diagram, and a compact footer note with page number in the lower right.

prompt: Create a single-page document with all content contained inside a rectangular page frame and organized as one main vertical stack of sections. At the very top, place a narrow full-width horizontal header strip divided into two text areas: on the left side of this strip include a small circular icon followed by a short line of identifier text, and on the far right include a compact status label, both aligned along the same horizontal line. Directly beneath this header strip, add a full-width main title line that spans the page, with its text left-aligned within the single central column. Below the main title, insert a large single-column text block containing several bullet points; arrange the bullet points as a vertical list with each bullet’s text left-aligned and occupying most of the page width, and end this list with a sentence that explicitly mentions that the diagram shown below illustrates the process. Immediately beneath this bullet list, centered horizontally on the page, create a flowchart-style diagram that conceptually uses three vertical columns. In the center column of the diagram, place four rectangular boxes stacked vertically: one at the top, a second directly below it, a third below the second, and a fourth at the bottom; connect each adjacent pair of these central boxes with a vertical downward-pointing arrow, and place short explanatory text between some of these central boxes to describe their relationships. To the left of this central column, add a left column consisting of six rectangular boxes of similar size arranged in a vertical stack; align this left stack so that it sits to the left of the central column without overlapping it. From at least one of the left-column boxes, draw a horizontal or slightly angled arrow pointing toward the central box that represents project sponsors, and place a brief label near this arrow indicating it is an example connection. To the right of the central column, add a right column containing two rectangular boxes stacked vertically; align this right stack so that it is horizontally in line with the central investor-owned utilities box. From the central investor-owned utilities box, draw two separate diagonal arrows leading to each of the two boxes in the right column, and include short text near these arrows to indicate the types of programs they represent. Add another small text annotation near the vertical arrow between the project sponsors box and the customers box to describe agreements and decisions made in this step. Ensure that the diagram as a whole is visually grouped as one section distinct from the bullet text above and the footer below, with the central column of four boxes forming the main vertical flow and the left and right columns serving as side entities connected by arrows. Finally, at the very bottom of the page below the diagram, create a full-width horizontal footer strip; inside this strip, place a source citation sentence aligned to the left and a page number aligned to the right, both on the same baseline, forming the bottommost elements within the page frame.

prompt: Create a single-page document layout with a strong emphasis on structural organization. At the top, place a full-width header bar consisting of one horizontal row divided into three grouped regions: on the left, a row of five evenly spaced circular icon placeholders; in the center, a prominent page title; on the right, two institutional logo images placed side-by-side. Directly beneath this header, add a second full-width section arranged as a two-column band where the left column is wider than the right: in the left column place a short heading line followed by several paragraphs of guidance text, and in the right column place a single wide, horizontally oriented process-flow diagram graphic. Below this band, construct the main content area as a full-width section divided into a wider left column and a narrower right column aligned side-by-side. Inside the wider left column, build a 3-by-2 grid with three horizontal rows and two vertical columns: along the far left, create a narrow vertical strip containing three stacked label panels (for idea or problem, approach, and next steps) aligned one above another; to the right of these labels, create three corresponding wider panels, also stacked vertically, each containing a bullet-point list of questions related to its label. Inside the narrower right column, create a vertical sidebar made of three main sections stacked from top to bottom: first, a heading panel titled about potential project costs and benefits; second, a cost section containing a short text prompt asking for the approximate cost of the project and, directly below this text, a single horizontal row of three cost-option blocks (small, medium, large) arranged side-by-side, each block including a checkbox element next to descriptive cost-range text; third, a benefits section containing a heading that asks what types of benefits the project might deliver and, beneath this heading, a vertically stacked single-column checklist of multiple benefit options where each line has a small checkbox on the left and descriptive benefit text on the right, ending with an item that allows an “other” description. Ensure that the header, the guidance band, and the main two-column content area all share the same overall page width and are aligned vertically in a single column of sections. Finally, place a thin full-width horizontal band along the very bottom of the page beneath both the left content grid and the right sidebar to act as a simple footer strip without internal subdivisions.

prompt: Create a wide, landscape infographic-style page whose structure emphasizes a clear three-part strategy. At the very top, place a full-width multi-line text header containing a single long headline sentence, center-aligned across the page. Directly beneath this header, add a full-width horizontal band that is divided into three labeled segments arranged left to right and connected by simple mathematical or arrow symbols; these three segments represent the three related strategy components. Below this band, construct the main content as a three-column grid: a left column, a center column, and a right column, laid out side-by-side and separated by slim vertical divider lines, with all three columns top-aligned just under the strategy band. In the leftmost column, place a subheading line at the top, then immediately below it insert a wide rectangular evaluation chart; this chart should have a narrow vertical label strip running along its left edge and, inside the chart, an upper horizontal scale bar with three circular criterion markers aligned in a row beneath it. Under this chart, still in the left column, add a multi-point bullet list of several process steps stacked vertically; to the immediate left of this list, align a narrow vertical process-stage strip that lists several short labels stacked from top to bottom, each label aligned with the corresponding bullet step. Beneath the bullet list, add a short concluding sentence, and at the very bottom-left corner of the entire page, under all content of the left column, place a compact multi-line note block with small text and numbered remarks. In the center column, place a subheading directly under the strategy band, then below it center a large circular process diagram: in its middle is a simple icon, around it a ring is divided into multiple wedge segments bearing short labels positioned around the circle. On the right side of this circular diagram, attach two rectangular callout boxes with dashed-style borders, one positioned above the other and both aligned vertically with the right edge of the circle. Directly beneath the circle, add a downward-pointing base or funnel shape whose narrow end points to a horizontal row of three small rectangular boxes arranged side-by-side and evenly spaced; each box is intended as an editable placeholder label. Centered immediately below these three small boxes, place a single-line caption explaining the outcome, aligned with the center column. In the rightmost column, stack three numbered content blocks vertically: the top block is labeled as section 1 and contains a short heading with a bullet list of integration points; the second block is labeled as section 2 and includes a heading plus several vertically stacked lines in which percentage values appear at the start of each line followed by descriptive text; the third block is labeled as section 3 and has a brief heading with a short explanatory text. Beneath this third numbered block and still within the right column, place a quote-style callout box at the bottom edge of the page, consisting of a tall decorative bracket on the left and a rectangular text area on the right containing a short quotation about cost reduction. Ensure that the overall layout preserves the hierarchy of a top header, a second-level strategy band, and a three-column main body with the described internal groupings and bottom notes, while avoiding any additional sections outside this structural framework.

prompt: Create a landscape information slide organized as a vertical stack of four major sections. At the top, design a full-width header bar consisting of a single horizontal row: place a large main title text group centered horizontally in the row, and in the same row place a smaller English label such as “Work Report” aligned to the far right; no other elements should appear above this header. Directly beneath the header, add a second section made of one horizontal row containing exactly five metric cards arranged side-by-side as equal-height columns; each card is a rectangular container with its own internal structure. In the first three cards, position a short title line at the top and a block of one or two lines of descriptive text directly beneath the title; in the fourth card, place a large numeric percentage as the primary element with a smaller explanatory text block beneath or beside it; in the fifth, rightmost card, include a title at the top and then several lines of paragraph text filling most of the remaining card area, so this rightmost card reads as a textual summary panel. Beneath this metrics row, add a third section composed of a single full-width rectangular banner strip containing one centered sentence of summary text running roughly across the middle of the strip; this strip should clearly separate the metrics above from the timeline below. The fourth and largest section, occupying the entire lower half of the slide, is a timeline grid laid out as a rectangular matrix. Structure this matrix with six horizontal rows and thirteen vertical columns: one header row plus five data rows, and one leftmost label column plus twelve month columns. Inside the top-left cell of the grid, place a combined heading label for “Work Tasks / Time,” and immediately below it in the same leftmost column stack exactly five task category labels, one per row, aligned vertically. To the right of this sidebar, reserve twelve equal-width columns for the months of the year; in the top header row of these twelve columns, place month header boxes labeled from January to December, each box containing a month number on one line and the English month name on a second line beneath it. Below these month headers, form five horizontal task rows that align with the five task categories in the left sidebar; each row consists of twelve cells corresponding to the months. Within these task rows, place multiple milestone labels as rounded or rectangular badges inside specific cells so that each milestone sits entirely within the boundaries of its intersecting task row and month column, directly under the appropriate month header. Distribute milestone labels along each row so that some occur under earlier month columns and others under later columns, visually indicating progression over the year. Ensure all grid rows share the same height, all month columns (excluding the left sidebar) share the same width, and vertical and horizontal grid lines or separations make the matrix structure clear. The entire timeline grid, including the left sidebar and month columns, should form the bottommost full-width block of the slide, with no additional sections beneath it, and all text elements throughout the slide should be contained within clearly defined rectangular or rounded containers.

prompt: Create an infographic-style canvas with a vertically stacked layout consisting of a top header, a subheader strip, and a main content area made of four equal-width vertical cards arranged in a single horizontal row. The topmost full-width section contains one centered headline text block. Directly beneath it, add a narrower full-width strip containing a single centered line of descriptive text composed of several short phrases separated by vertical divider characters. Below this strip, spanning the width of the canvas, place four tall rectangular cards aligned side-by-side in one row, all the same width and height, with consistent spacing between them and with margins separating the group from the canvas edges. Each card is a self-contained vertical layout divided into four stacked bands: a top title band, an upper content band, a middle two-column text band, and a bottom action band. In the upper content band of each card, place a large circular number badge anchored along the left edge, centered vertically within this band, and place a module illustration to the right of the badge so that both elements share the same horizontal zone, with the illustration entirely inside the card and directly below the title. In the bottom band of each card, create a full-width horizontal row of three equal-width rectangular buttons or segments arranged side-by-side, each containing a small icon above a short two- or three-word label; these three buttons fill the width of the card and share the same height. Ensure that the internal structure of all four cards is identical, with the first card positioned at the far left of the row, the second, third, and fourth cards placed sequentially to the right, and that all text and graphical elements are contained entirely within their respective header, subheader, or card areas.

prompt: Create a slide layout structured as a single page with a clear hierarchy of sections. Below the subtitle, in the upper central portion of the slide, place one horizontally centered rounded rectangle containing the starting statement for the flow. From the bottom center of this rectangle, draw a short downward arrow leading to a horizontal row of two decision diamonds: the left diamond labeled with a materiality question and the right diamond labeled with a relevance question. These two diamonds should be aligned in the same row, with a horizontal arrow along their top edges indicating a YES flow from the left diamond into the right diamond. To the left and slightly below the left diamond, place a third decision diamond labeled with a large fund manager question; connect its top point to the left side of the left diamond by an upward arrow, and connect its bottom point by a downward arrow into a main rectangular process box located in the middle of the slide. This main rectangle, labeled with baseline requirements and enhanced standards, should be horizontally centered in the middle region and positioned below the row of two upper diamonds. From the main rectangle, draw three separate arrow branches: one extending rightward toward a vertical stack of two process list panels on the right side, and one or more extending downward to the lower disclosure row. On the right side of the slide, create a single right-aligned column containing two rectangular text panels stacked vertically; the upper panel represents all baseline process requirements, and the lower panel represents enhanced process standards. Each of these right-side panels contains multi-line list text. Place the upper right panel approximately level with the row of central decision diamonds, and place the lower right panel directly beneath it, with a vertical arrow connecting or visually aligning their related flows. Connect at least one arrow from the central baseline rectangle to the upper right process panel, and from that area or from the baseline rectangle also route an arrow to the lower right process panel to show inclusion of enhanced standards. Beneath the central baseline rectangle, near the lower central part of the slide, add a row of two rectangular panels side-by-side: on the left, a panel labeled with all baseline disclosure requirements at entity level, and on the right, a panel labeled with enhanced disclosure standards. These two panels should form a single bottom row, horizontally centered as a pair, and both should be fed by a downward arrow coming from the central baseline rectangle that splits into left and right branches. From the right disclosure panel, extend an arrow path that goes downward and then turns right and upward to feed into a decision diamond positioned near the bottom-right portion of the slide. This bottom-right decision diamond contains a short question about whether a ROOF-type condition is met. From the right side of this diamond, draw a horizontal arrow leading into a rounded or standard rectangle labeled END positioned at the far right edge of the slide, representing process termination. From the bottom point of the ROOF decision diamond, route a downward arrow that rejoins or continues the green arrow path coming from the disclosure panels, indicating a YES outcome that loops back into the main flow. Ensure that all rectangular and diamond nodes, list panels, and arrows are contained within the main body area between the subtitle and the bottom margin. Finally, place a small slide number text element at the extreme bottom-right corner of the slide below the END box, aligned with the bottom edge.

prompt: Create a technical architecture diagram consisting of two main horizontal tiers, with an upper full-width pipeline and a lower full-width row of three detailed module containers. In the top tier, draw a single horizontal sequence of six main processing elements connected by left-to-right arrows: at the far left place a cube-shaped input block, immediately to its right place a vertically oriented rectangular block labeled as shallow feature extraction, to the right of that place a large horizontally oriented residual group block that visually appears as several overlapping rectangles and contains an internal dashed subcontainer holding a row of three repeated residual block boxes followed by a final convolution box, to the right of the residual group add a circular addition symbol, to the right of the addition symbol place a rectangular high-quality reconstruction block, and finally to the right of that place a cube-shaped output block. In the same upper tier but separated from the pipeline, place a small rectangular legend box in the top right corner containing several tiny rows of icons with text labels stacked vertically. Directly beneath the upper pipeline and above the lower tier, draw a long horizontal arrow representing a skip connection that starts near the right side of the shallow feature extraction block, runs under the residual group block, and ends at the addition symbol, and center a text label along this arrow reading "Long-range skip connection." The lower tier is a three-column grid of large dashed rectangular containers aligned side-by-side and sharing the same top and bottom alignment; the left container is the Residual State-Space Block module, the middle container is the Vision State-Space Module, and the right container is the 2D Selective Scan Module. Inside the left bottom container, construct a primary horizontal flow roughly along the midline: a tall narrow block on the far left receives input from the container’s left edge, an arrow leads from this tall block into a circular operator, then into a sequence of several vertically oriented processing blocks arranged in a row, with circular operator symbols interleaved between some of them; above the central part of this row place a tall rectangular sub-module block that connects down into the main row via vertical lines, and include an additional circular operator near the bottom to indicate another residual interaction, so that the internal elements form a horizontally oriented chain with one elevated module. Inside the middle bottom container, make the input enter from the left into a vertical pair of stacked blocks forming a short left column; from this column, route an arrow into a central cluster composed of a single row of four narrow vertical blocks placed side-by-side, positioned above a single horizontal block that spans under them, and then connect this cluster to a tall vertical block on the right that serves as the output of this module. Inside the right bottom container, place a single block representing a 2D tensor along the left edge and have it feed into four parallel horizontal branches stacked vertically one above another; each branch contains a small sequence-like block followed by a rectangular block labeled as a 1D module, and all four branches terminate at a single circular addition symbol located toward the right-center of the container; from this addition symbol, draw an arrow into a rectangular reshape block, and from that reshape block draw an arrow into a cube-shaped 2D output block placed at the far right of the container. Ensure that all arrows throughout the figure are strictly horizontal or vertical, that the three bottom containers are evenly spaced in one row beneath the skip connection band, and that the overall layout clearly shows a top-level end-to-end pipeline with detailed decompositions into three modules in the bottom tier.

prompt: Create a slide-like page with a clear top-to-bottom structure consisting of a header, a central timeline table, a two-column quote area, and a footer with notes and a page number. At the very top, place a single full-width header row divided into two columns: on the left, a multi-line title block aligned to the left edge; on the right, a compact rectangular logo area aligned to the right edge on the same horizontal baseline as the title. Directly beneath this header, add a large timeline table that fills the upper-middle of the page; this table has two main vertical parts: a narrow left column for labels and a wide right column for the timeline itself. The topmost row of this table is a header row where the left cell contains the word “Year” and the right side is subdivided into many adjacent small vertical cells, each containing one year label arranged in a single horizontal sequence. Below this header row, create nine data rows; in each row, the left cell contains a regulation name such as “Basel III capital ratios,” “IFRS 9,” and so on, all left-aligned and stacked vertically so their left edges form a straight column. In each corresponding right cell, draw horizontal timeline graphics aligned with the year header above: for some rows use solid horizontal bars that span across multiple neighboring year positions, for some use bars that end in arrowheads pointing right, and for several rows split the bar into two or more segments on the same line with embedded text labels like “Phased-in,” “Full,” “Start,” or similar placed inside or immediately above the relevant part of the bar; ensure each row’s bar or arrow is horizontally aligned with its label to the left and with the year scale above. Immediately below this entire timeline table, add a quote section that spans the width of the page but is divided into two equal-width columns arranged side-by-side; in each column, place at the upper-left of the column a large quotation mark character, followed by a multi-line paragraph of quote text, and directly beneath that paragraph an attribution line, all left-aligned within that column so the two quote blocks share a common top alignment. Underneath the quote section, create a footer area also spanning the width of the page, divided into two note groups that are aligned underneath their respective quote columns: in the left group place a short source label followed by a vertically stacked list of several numbered explanatory notes, and in the right group place another vertically stacked list containing the remaining numbered notes. Finally, at the extreme bottom-right corner of the page, below and slightly to the right of the right-hand note group, position a small standalone page number aligned with the bottom edge. The entire composition should read in a single vertical flow from the header at the top, down through the timeline grid, then the two quote columns, and finally the split footer notes with the page number at the bottom-right.

prompt: Create a scientific workflow diagram with a clearly structured layout. At the very top, place a single full-width header section containing the main workflow title, centered horizontally. Directly below this header, organize the rest of the page into a two-row by three-column grid. In this grid, the left and right columns each contain two separate rectangular panels stacked vertically (one in the top row and one in the bottom row), while the center column contains one tall rectangular panel that spans the full height of both rows. In the first row, left column, place a process-flow panel for reference generation: inside this panel arrange three main blocks in a single horizontal row. The left block contains a small illustration of dissociated material above a sequencer icon, stacked vertically with a downward arrow between them. The center block, labeled UMAP, is positioned directly to the right of the left block and holds a scatter-plot-style graphic. The right block is positioned directly to the right of the center block and contains a matrix-style graphic; above this matrix place short gene label text in multiple lines, and below it place a label describing annotated cell type profiles. Connect the left block to the center block and the center block to the right block using horizontal arrows pointing from left to right. Place a caption describing scRNA-seq data processing and annotation along the bottom of this panel. In the second row, left column, place a panel for ST data acquisition and preprocessing: arrange the elements so they form an approximate 2x2 grid. On the left side, include a starting ST slide illustration feeding into a sequencer icon and a microscope icon placed slightly above and to the right; from these devices, draw arrows to two output images positioned on the right side of the panel, with a spatial expression matrix image on the upper right and an aligned histology image directly below it, forming a vertical pair. Place a descriptive caption for ST data generation and histological features along the bottom edge of this panel. In the center column, spanning both rows, place a tall panel for multi-modal integration and deconvolution. At the top of this tall panel, insert a scatter-plot-style image aligned to the left, and immediately to its right in the same horizontal row place a narrow vertical legend listing several cell types. Directly below this top row, centered horizontally in the tall panel, place a rectangular box labeled as an integration algorithm; connect the top scatter plot to this box with a downward arrow. Below this box, again centered, place a circular ST capture spot diagram within a circular or magnified inset, connected to the algorithm box by a downward arrow and an optional dashed outline indicating zoom. Under this diagram, near the bottom edge of the tall panel, add a label indicating multi-modal integration. From the right side of both left-column panels draw arrows entering the tall central panel at appropriate vertical positions to indicate their outputs feeding into integration. From the right edge of the central panel, draw two separate horizontal arrows extending toward the right column, one directed toward the upper-right panel and one toward the lower-right panel. In the first row, right column, place a high-resolution mapping panel: inside it, position a large tissue-shaped image on the left and, in the same horizontal row, a narrow vertical list of cell type labels on the right. Below these, within the same panel, center a caption describing a cell type spatial distribution map. In the second row, right column, place a cell-cell interaction panel: in the center of this panel place a network diagram consisting of multiple circular nodes connected by lines, with one node in the middle and several nodes arranged around it. On the right side of this network, add a vertical arrow and label indicating spatial interaction strength. Finally, align a caption describing a spatial cell-cell interaction network along the bottom of this panel. Ensure that all panels are clearly separated but aligned in the three-column, two-row structure, with the central integration panel visibly spanning the full height between the top and bottom rows and serving as the hub connecting the left-column and right-column panels via arrows.

prompt: Create a single landscape slide whose structure can be precisely reconstructed as follows: at the very top, place a full-width header that consists of two lines of title text stacked vertically and centered horizontally, and draw a thin horizontal divider line directly beneath this header to separate it from the body. Below this divider, organize the main body into a two-column layout with a narrower left column and a wider right column positioned side-by-side; insert a vertical dashed separator line running from near the top of the body to near the bottom to mark the boundary between the two columns. In the upper part of the left column, create section 1 as a rectangular box titled as an overview of three drug generations; inside this box, under the heading, arrange three rows stacked vertically, each row representing one generation, and within each row place a smaller label element on the left, a directional arrow next to it, and a larger descriptive text block on the right so that every row forms a consistent two-column horizontal arrangement. Directly below this overview box within the same left column, create section 2 as another rectangular text area; at its top place a numbered heading about the future development direction, and beneath that heading add four bullet points arranged as separate paragraph lines stacked vertically in a single column, occupying the rest of the left column height above the footer. In the right column, align the top of section 3 horizontally with the top of section 1; for section 3, draw a large rounded container that spans the full width of the right column, and within this container place two numbered panels side-by-side in one horizontal row: the left panel contains a horizontal bar chart titled with transacted pipelines by companies, with a legend above the bars and a vertical list of company names along the left side of the chart area; the right panel contains a chart of global market size and licensing transactions, with the plot at the top, a small legend near the plot, and a compact data table directly beneath the chart inside the same right-side panel. Below this dual-panel container in the right column, add section 5 as a wide table-like block that spans the entire width of the right column; at the top of this block, place a numbered title about the history of ADC for multinational companies, followed immediately by a header row with column labels for corporate name, oncology sales, and a sequence of year columns ordered from left to right including 2000, 2005, 2010, 2015, 2020, and now; under the header, add multiple company rows, each row containing the company name in the first column, a numeric sales value in the second column, and a horizontal timeline stretching across the series of year columns with multiple markers placed along that line; at the very bottom of this table block, include a legend row that runs horizontally across the width explaining the different marker types used in the timelines. Finally, beneath both columns and spanning the lower part of the slide, create a narrow footer band; in its left side place a small company logo, and slightly to the right toward the center of the slide place a single line of text giving material sources, ensuring that this footer remains visually separate from the main two-column content above.

prompt: Create a wide, landscape-oriented scientific diagram with a single line of title text centered at the very top, spanning the full width. Directly below the title, place three large rounded rectangles representing stages arranged side-by-side in a single horizontal row: a left panel, a middle panel, and a right panel, all top-aligned with each other. Each panel has its own short label centered along its top inside edge. The left panel is for graph construction and contains a vertically stacked sequence in its upper half: at the top, a small schematic icon of a crystal; directly below it, a downward arrow leading to a second icon depicting a small graph; the icons are centered horizontally within the panel. Near the lower-left interior of this panel, add two medium-sized rectangular boxes placed side-by-side in one horizontal row, representing a node encoder on the left and an edge encoder on the right. Along the bottom interior edge of the same panel, place a smaller horizontal rounded box labeled as the working ion near the left, followed by a rightward arrow that immediately connects to a longer, thin horizontal bar labeled as ion embedding that runs toward the right and touches the right boundary of the panel. The middle panel represents an ion-aware backbone and contains a nested block beneath its header: near the top, draw three overlapping rectangular frames arranged like a stack with a caption indicating repeated layers centered above them. Inside this stacked area, in its upper-middle region, place two rectangular module boxes side-by-side in a single row, the left one labeled as an attention module and the right one as a transformation including normalization and activation, with arrows connecting them to form a small loop. Just below these modules, overlapping the lower part of the stacked frames, show a circular zoom-in illustration anchored toward the left side of the panel. Under this, in the lower-middle area of the middle panel, create a vertical group: a horizontally oriented rectangular box labeled as global mean pooling, and directly beneath it a narrow horizontal bar composed of small adjacent rectangles labeled as a graph embedding; these two elements are stacked vertically and centered. At the very bottom of the middle panel, aligned horizontally and centered, continue the ion-embedding bar as a medium-length horizontal rounded rectangle labeled ion embedding that visually aligns with and extends the bar originating in the left panel, so the two ion-embedding bars share a common baseline. Outside the middle panel, immediately to its right and between it and the right panel, place a tall, narrow vertical bar made up of stacked small rectangles representing a feature vector, positioned above a circular symbol denoting concatenation; both are centered vertically relative to the height of the panels. Draw a downward arrow from the bottom of the vertical bar into the top of the concat circle. From the right side of the graph embedding bar inside the middle panel, draw a rightward arrow that exits the panel, bends downward if needed, and enters the concat circle from the left; from the right end of the ion-embedding bar at the bottom, draw an upward and rightward arrow that also feeds into the concat circle from below. From the right side of the concat circle, add a single arrow pointing into the left edge of the rightmost panel. The right panel represents prediction and screening and is divided into an upper and lower region. In the upper region just below its label, draw a band of elements: on the left two-thirds, place two tall, narrow rectangular boxes labeled as MLP heads standing side-by-side in one horizontal pair, both aligned at their top edges; from the entry arrow coming from the concat circle, branch connections into these MLP head boxes. On the right third of the same band, place two medium-sized rectangular boxes stacked vertically, the top one labeled as average voltage and the bottom one labeled as formation energy, with short connecting arrows from the nearest MLP head box. Below this upper band, occupying most of the lower half of the right panel, draw a large rectangular container labeled high-throughput screening; the label occupies a narrow header strip along the top of this container, and the interior below the label contains a dense grid of small schematic geometric icons arranged in multiple aligned rows and columns, each icon representing a candidate structure. Ensure that this screening container is fully inside the right panel and that the grid of icons does not extend beyond its borders. Finally, along the very bottom of the entire staged region, ensure the ion-embedding bar from the left panel seamlessly aligns with and appears to continue as the ion-embedding bar within the middle panel before connecting upward into the concatenation circle, clearly showing a continuous bottom pathway parallel to the three main panels.

prompt: Create a wide rectangular diagram that is organized into three main vertical columns representing sequential steps, all positioned beneath a shared header band; in the header band, place a single left-to-right horizontal arrow spanning almost the full width, and align three separate bullet-point text blocks above the left, middle, and right thirds of the arrow respectively so that each block visually corresponds to one step column below. prompt: Create a presentation slide with a clear process-flow layout. Above the midpoint of the slide, roughly centered horizontally, add a single row of several equal-sized small rectangular blocks touching or nearly touching each other, each block labeled with a different use case or event-type term; this row should sit above the later central vertical list panel. prompt: Recreate a single-page horizontal infographic-style image whose main focus is on text and layout. At the very top-center, spanning almost the full width, place a two-line main headline reading exactly on line 1: "Underlying U.K. electricity demand is projected to be ~411 TWh in 2030," and on line 2: "excluding the impact of current or future policy". Directly below it, add a second top-level bullet line reading "• U.K. electricity demand to 2030 based on DECC projections including estimated direct impact of policy due to". Under this second bullet, indent three sub-bullets, each starting with an en dash. The first sub-bullet line should read exactly "– Increased demand due to demographics and economic growth". The second sub-bullet line should read exactly "– Reduced demand due to historic trend towards increase in natural energy efficiency". To the right of this panel, occupying the remainder of the page width, construct a vertical bar chart area. At the top of this chart area, just under the main headline, center a single-line chart title reading exactly "Electricity consumption evolution in U.K., 2010–30". Within the plotting region of the chart, near its upper-left inside corner, add a small subtitle or axis label reading "End use consumption, TWh". Inside the chart, create seven vertical bars or bar segments arranged left to right. Above each bar, place a numeric label in the following sequence from leftmost to rightmost: "328" above the first bar, "27" above the second, "355" above the third, "56" above the fourth, "411" above the fifth (the tallest central bar), "54" above the sixth, and "357" above the seventh bar on the far right. All rights reserved.". prompt: Recreate a single presentation slide with a landscape orientation that contains multiple textual and diagram elements. At the very top-left, place a circular seal whose border text reads “DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA,” and at the very top-right place another circular seal whose border text reads “ASSISTANT SECRETARY OF DEFENSE INDUSTRIAL BASE POLICY.” Centered between the two seals at the top, add a small single-line heading that says “Distribution Statement A.” Directly below this, in the top-center, place a large main title line reading “DoD AI Adoption Strategy.” Under the title, still centered, add an uppercase subtitle line that reads “THE OFFICE OF THE SECRETARY OF DEFENSE FOR INDUSTRIAL BASE POLICY.” Beneath this subtitle, spanning nearly the full width of the slide as a horizontal banner text, add the sentence “DoD strategy to accelerate the adoption of advanced AI capabilities to ensure decision superiority.” In the main body of the slide, create a large dashed rectangular box occupying the left half of the slide. At the upper-left inside this dashed box, insert a bold heading that reads “HIERARCHY OF NEEDS¹” including the superscript one character. Immediately below this heading, still inside the dashed box, add a single bullet-style introductory sentence reading “The DoD will focus strategic efforts on several interdependent goals that support the DoD AI Hierarchy of Needs.” Below this sentence, centered within the dashed box, place a pyramid-style diagram. Around the outside of this pyramid, add six goal labels: near the top-left of the pyramid write “Strengthen governance and remove policy barriers”; near the top-right of the pyramid write “Invest in interoperable, federated infrastructure”; along the left-middle side of the pyramid write “Deliver capabilities for enterprise and joint warfighting impact”; along the right-middle side of the pyramid write “Advance the data, analytics, and AI ecosystem”; near the bottom-left of the pyramid write “Improve foundational data management”; and near the bottom-right of the pyramid write “Expand digital talent management.” Inside the pyramid, stack five text elements vertically from top to bottom so they form the internal hierarchy: at the upper interior place the phrase “RESPONSIBLE AI”; directly below it place the smaller word “assurance”; in the middle interior place the phrase “INSIGHTFUL ANALYTICS & METRICS”; beneath that place the smaller word “governance”; and at the lowest interior section of the pyramid place the phrase “QUALITY DATA.” Along the slanted left edge, the slanted right edge, and the bottom edge of the pyramid, add the same word “ENABLERS,” one instance on each edge. Just below the dashed box on the lower-left side of the slide, add two reference lines formatted as a numbered list: the first line reading “1. DoD Data, Analytics, and Artificial Intelligence Adoption Strategy Fact Sheet” and the second line reading “2. DoD Releases AI Adoption Strategy Press Release.” To the right of the dashed box, occupying the right half of the slide, place a text section. At the upper-right of this area add a bold heading that reads “DECISION ADVANTAGE²,” including the superscript two character. Directly under this heading add a descriptive paragraph that reads exactly: “The strategy prescribes an agile approach to AI development and application centered on a tight feedback loop between end users and capability developers, emphasizing speed of delivery and adoption at scale leading to five specific decision advantage outcomes:”. Beneath this paragraph, add a vertical bulleted list of five items, in order: “Superior battlespace awareness and understanding”, “Adaptive force planning and application”, “Fast, precise and resilient kill chains”, “Resilient sustainment support”, and “Efficient enterprise business operations.” Leave some vertical space and then, still on the right side, add a spaced-letter heading that reads “OUTCOME ORIENTED¹,” including the superscript one character at the end. Directly beneath this heading insert a paragraph that reads: “As a result of implementing this strategy, DoD leaders and warfighters will be able to make rapid, well-informed decisions by expertly leveraging high-quality data, advanced analytics, and AI as part of a continuous, outcome-driven, and user-focused development, deployment, and feedback cycle.” Finally, at the extreme bottom-right corner of the slide, below all other right-side text, place a small slide number reading “20.”

prompt: Recreate a single slide-style image organized horizontally with a top title, a subtitle banner, a row of three process arrows, a large comparison table and explanatory text on the left, and calculation plus narrative sections on the right. At the very top-center, place a one-line title reading exactly "Q2_Part 2: Projected Sales Revenue for CDK4 Inhibitors in HR+/HER2- Breast Cancer Treatment". Directly beneath this title, spanning the width of the slide, add a horizontal banner containing the single sentence "An estimated 310,720 new cases of invasive breast cancer will be diagnosed in women in the United States in 2024.". Below the banner in the upper-center, place three adjacent arrow-shaped boxes from left to right with the exact texts "Target Patient Population", "Market Share Evaluation", and "Projected Sales Revenue". Under these arrows, occupying the center-left of the slide, add a section heading reading "Market Dynamics and Competitive Landscape of CDK4/6 Inhibitors in HR+/HER2- Breast Cancer" and immediately below it a five-column, six-row comparison table. The table’s header row from left to right must read: "Factors | Atrimociclib (Pfizer, new CDK4 inhibitor) | Palbociclib (Ibrance) | Ribociclib (Kisqali) | Abemaciclib (Verzenio)". The first data row, for efficacy, should read: "Efficacy | Green: PFS of 35 months | Amber: Strong PFS (24.8 months) | Green: OS benefit and PFS of ~25.3 months | Green: PFS and OS benefit". The second row, for safety, should read: "Safety | Amber: Early-stage trials | Red: High risk of neutropenia | Green: Good safety profile | Amber: Gastrointestinal issue". The third row, for convenience, should read: "Convenience | Amber: oral | Amber: 21 days on, 7 days off | Amber: 21 days on, 7 days off | Green: Continuous dosing (no cycle breaks)". The fourth row, for market share potential, should read: "Market Share Potential | Green: Potential to capture 15-20% market share in its first 3 years | Red: Current 50-55% market share but declining | Green: Growing share of 15-20% | Green: Currently holds 25-30% market share and growing". The fifth row, for pricing, should read: "Pricing | Amber: $15,000 | Amber: ~$12,000 to $15,000 | Amber: similar | Amber: similar". Below this table on the left side, place a small subsection titled "Competitive Landscape:" followed on separate lines by two arrow-style bullet points: first, "➢ Ribociclib & Abemaciclib are challenging Palbociclib's dominant position, signaling a shift in the HR+/HER2- breast cancer market" and second, "➢ Atrimociclib: Emerging as a competitor in the CDK4 inhibitor space, targeting HR+/HER2- late-stage breast cancer for first-line treatment.". To the immediate right of this competitive landscape block, toward the bottom-center-left of the slide, stack three small boxes vertically labeled from top to bottom "First Year", "Second Year", and "Third Year". Align to the right of these labels three corresponding horizontal text paragraphs: to the right of "First Year" place the sentence "10-20% market share: Rapid adoption due to strong PFS (35 months), but cautious uptake without mature OS data."; to the right of "Second Year" place "18% market share: Continued growth as safety profile becomes clearer and PFS data remains strong, while Palbociclib declines due to lack of OS data."; and to the right of "Third Year" place "20-25% market share: Potential rise with emerging OS data, allowing Atrimociclib to further displace Palbociclib and compete with Ribociclib and Abemaciclib.". On the right half of the slide, in the upper-right quadrant, create a block titled on the first line "Final Calculation Logistics:" and beneath it list six check-marked lines in order: "Since $15,000 per month × 12 months = $180,000 per patient/year", "Then $180,000 per patient annually × 6217 cases × Penetration rate/Market share:", "Year 1: $180,000 × 6217 cases × 15% = 181.548 million.", "Year 2: $180,000 × 6217 cases × 18% = 201.431 million.", and "Year 3: $180,000 × 6217 cases × 22.5% = 272.322 million". Directly below this calculation block, in the bottom-right area, add three square-bullet narrative paragraphs stacked vertically. The top one must read: "▢ Ibrance (Palbociclib) reached an 84% market share largely due to its first-mover advantage after its 2015 approval. With no competition for two years, it captured the majority of the HR+/HER2- breast cancer market, driven by strong progression-free survival (PFS) data and high demand for new treatments.". The middle narrative bullet must read: "▢ However, newer drugs like Ribociclib and Abemaciclib have fragmented the market. They face hurdles such as insurance coverage and policy approvals, but factors like overall survival (OS) data, safety, and dosing convenience now play a larger role in market dynamics.". The bottom narrative bullet must read: "▢ As competition grows, the peak market share for newer CDK4/6 inhibitors is expected to stabilize around 22-50%, with Ibrance's established presence ensuring it won't be entirely displaced, despite its gradual decline.". Maintain all text exactly as written, keeping the relative positions: title and banner at the top-center, process arrows just below, the large comparison table and competitive text occupying the left and lower-left center, the year labels and their market share sentences in the lower-center, and the calculation formulas plus narrative bullets occupying the upper-right and lower-right areas respectively.

prompt: Create a slide-like rectangular image with a horizontal top banner that contains, on the left side, a row of five evenly spaced circular icons with simple pictogram illustrations, and on the right side, near the top-right corner, a multicoloured circular ring followed immediately to its right by the single-line logo text "UNIVERSITY OF BATH". Directly below this top banner and centered horizontally, place a large single-line title that spans most of the width and reads exactly: "WHAT IS A PROJECT AND PROJECT MANAGEMENT?". Beneath this title, slightly offset to the left side of the slide, add a tall vertical rectangle representing an illustration of a clipboard with checklist marks; this rectangle contains no text and acts as a decorative icon. To the right of this clipboard illustration, occupying most of the central width of the slide, place a wide blue rectangular text box containing two lines of white text forming one paragraph; the first line should read exactly: "In simple terms, Project Management is the activity of organising, controlling and delivering a Project." and directly below it the second line should read exactly: "Although different project management methodologies have their own ways of defining what it means (see grey boxes below).". Immediately below this first blue text box, aligned with its left and right edges and still to the right of the clipboard illustration, place a second blue rectangular text box with a single line of white text giving a dictionary-style definition that reads exactly: "Project noun (c) 'prɒdʒ.ekt': a piece of planned work or an activity that is finished over a period of time and intended to achieve a particular purpose". Beneath this second blue box, spanning the lower half of the slide, place two adjacent grey rectangular text panels of equal height: one on the bottom-left and one on the bottom-center-right. In the left grey panel at the bottom-left, add an italic-looking heading on the top line that reads exactly: "APM Body of Knowledge 7th edition". Below this heading in the same left grey panel, place three body paragraphs as separate blocks of text stacked vertically: the first paragraph should read exactly: "A project is a unique, transient endeavour undertaken to bring about change and to achieve planned objectives."; the second paragraph, placed directly beneath the first with a small gap, should read exactly: "Project management is the application of processes, methods, skills, knowledge and experience to achieve specific project objectives according to the project acceptance criteria within agreed parameters. Project management has final deliverables that are constrained to a finite timescale and budget."; and the third paragraph, placed beneath the second, should read exactly: "A key factor that distinguishes project management from just 'management' is that it has this final deliverable and a finite timespan, unlike management which is an ongoing process. Because of this a project professional needs a wide range of skills; often technical skills, and certainly people management skills and good business awareness.". In the right grey panel at the bottom-center-right, positioned directly to the right of the APM grey panel and flush with it along the top and bottom edges, add an italic-looking heading on the top line that reads exactly: "PRINCE2 (Axelos)". Beneath this heading in the right grey panel, add two body paragraphs stacked vertically: the first PRINCE2 paragraph should read exactly: "A project is a temporary venture that exists to produce a defined outcome. Each project will have agreed and unique objectives as well as its own project plan, budget, timescale, deliverables and tasks. A project may also involve people from different teams within an organization who are brought together to accomplish a specific goal." and the second PRINCE2 paragraph beneath it should read exactly: "Project management can be defined as the discipline of applying specific processes and principles to initiate, plan, execute and manage the way that new initiatives or changes are implemented within an organization. Project management is different to management of business as usual activity, which is an ongoing process, as it involves creating new work packages to achieve agreed ends or goals.". Ensure all textual content is placed in the specified positions relative to the top banner, title, clipboard illustration, blue boxes, and grey panels so that the overall layout matches the described structure.

The seventh and lowest right rectangle, aligned with the seventh central bullet, should include two lines: line one "Pending all-parties settlement on System" and line two "Resiliency Plan filing." prompt: Create a flat infographic on a horizontal 16:9 canvas with a solid very light warm beige background and no texture, borders, gradients, or shadows. Inside this circle, set four centered lines of bold white all-caps sans-serif text reading, from top to bottom, “7 PILLARS OF CUSTOMER SERVICE,” with even line spacing. Around this circle, arrange seven identical horizontally oriented rounded rectangles representing pillars, all fully opaque, filled with a solid medium warm orange and outlined with a thin uniform solid black stroke, each significantly wider than tall. Position two of these rounded rectangles in the upper middle area above the circle: one directly above and slightly left of the circle titled “COMMUNICATION,” and one above and slightly right titled “PILLAR GOES HERE”; position two more as a vertically stacked left column to the left of the circle, two as a vertically stacked right column to the right of the circle aligned with the left column, and one centered below the circle as a bottom pillar. Connect the central circle to each orange rectangle with a thin straight solid black line segment: two lines angling upward to the two top pillars, two lines angling to the left column pillars, two lines angling to the right column pillars, and one vertical line extending downward from the bottom of the circle to the bottom-center pillar; ensure there are exactly seven such connector lines, each touching the circle’s edge and the midpoint of the nearest side of its rectangle. In the top-left upper pillar rectangle labeled “COMMUNICATION,” place centered bold black all-caps sans-serif title text “COMMUNICATION” on the first line and below it two lines of smaller regular-weight black sans-serif sentence-case text reading “Clear and professional verbal or written communication goes a long way in ensuring customer satisfaction.” In the other six orange pillar rectangles, center a bold black all-caps sans-serif title line reading “PILLAR GOES HERE,” followed by two lines of smaller regular black sans-serif body text reading “Supporting details and examples go here.” At the very top of the canvas, centered horizontally and separate from the pillar system, add a wide horizontal rounded rectangle header bar filled with a darker burnt orange distinct from the pillars and outlined with a thin solid black stroke; make it span more than half the canvas width and be about one quarter as tall as its width-to-height ratio. Inside this header bar, place one line of centered bold white all-caps sans-serif text reading “BUSINESS NAME GOES HERE.” Use the same clean modern sans-serif typeface throughout the graphic, with bold weight for all titles and regular weight for all supporting text, and ensure all text and shapes are fully opaque with no gradients or transparency to maintain a simple flat design.

prompt: Create a 16:9 landscape technical slide with a dark navy blue background that transitions via a smooth diagonal gradient into a rich violet-purple toward the lower right corner, and place at the top center a single line of large bold white sans-serif text reading “Full-stack serverless silo and pool.” Directly beneath this title, arrange three identical minimalist user bust icons drawn in thin white line-art, spaced evenly across the width; under each icon put a white sans-serif label reading from left to right “Tenant 1”, “Tenant 2”, and “Tenant 3..N”, and from each label draw a short vertical solid white arrow pointing downward. prompt: Create a 16:9 landscape presentation slide whose entire background is a dark forest-green color overlay with medium opacity placed over a desaturated photograph of an urban shopping street with people walking, so the street scene is visible but muted under the green tint. At the very top, place a multi-line sentence-case headline reading “In a slower personal luxury market, winners and losers expected also among categories” in large bold white sans-serif font, left-aligned but spanning most of the width. Centered below this rule, add a two-line title block: the first line reads “True Luxury Consumers net appetite by category¹” in medium-large bold white sans-serif font, and directly under it include a single parenthetical explanatory sentence in smaller italic white sans-serif font. In the middle of the slide, create a bar chart with a single thin solid white horizontal axis line serving as the baseline; the line runs horizontally across the chart area. Place eight vertical rectangular bars aligned to this baseline. From left to right, the bars show net appetite values of 40%, 33%, 21%, 15%, 10%, 0%, -7%, and -25%. The first five bars extend upward from the baseline and are filled with a solid bright grass-green color. The sixth bar is a very short rectangle centered on the baseline filled with solid bright lemon-yellow, representing zero. The last two bars extend downward below the baseline and are filled with solid vivid signal-red to indicate negative values, with the -25% bar extending farther from the baseline than the -7% bar and appearing taller in magnitude than every positive bar except the 40% bar. Inside each bar, center a bold sans-serif percentage label: place “40%”, “33%”, “21%”, “15%”, “10%”, “-7%”, and “-25%” in solid white text within their respective green or red bars, and place “0%” in solid black text centered inside the yellow bar. Around the “40%” label inside the first green bar, draw a single medium-thickness bright yellow ellipse with transparent fill, forming a smooth continuous oval that closely encircles the number to highlight it. Directly under the row of bars, add a horizontal row of eight flat white silhouette icons, each centered under its corresponding bar: a high-heeled shoe under the first bar, a pair of cosmetic or perfume bottles under the second, a wristwatch with a circular face and small ring under the third, a handbag with a curved handle under the fourth, a combination of a top and skirt representing clothing under the fifth, a pair of sunglasses under the sixth, a small wallet under the seventh, and a straight necktie under the eighth. Beneath each icon, center a white sentence-case sans-serif text label naming the category: “Shoes”, “Fragrances & Cosmetics”, “Watches & Jewelry”, “Handbags”, “Clothing²”, “Sunglasses”, “Small leather goods”, and “Silk goods”. Along the bottom left of the slide, include several lines of very small white regular sans-serif footnote text, left-aligned, that explain the index calculation, note that some categories include multiple apparel types, and reference a 2016 ad hoc study with 12,000 luxury consumers across 10 countries; use superscript-like numerals “1” and “2” in the text where appropriate. In the bottom right corner, place two small flat white company logos side by side, rendered as simple text or abstract marks without color, and just beneath or beside them put a tiny white sans-serif page number “11” in the extreme corner. Ensure that every piece of text other than the “0%” inside the yellow bar is pure white, that all fonts are clean sans-serif, and that no shapes use gradients or outlines beyond the specified yellow oval and the white axis line.

prompt: Create a clean corporate presentation slide on a solid white background, laid out in two vertical columns, with modern sans-serif typography in dark blue, black, and gray. At the very top center place the title text “Expanding regional banking franchise” in large bold dark steel blue sans-serif letters, horizontally centered. In the top right corner add a compact corporate logo made of three narrow vertical red bars followed by a short word in blue capital letters, with a thin red horizontal line running beneath the word, all on the white background. On the upper left half, directly beneath the title, place a wide dark blue horizontal banner with centered bold white sans-serif text reading “Extensive Regional Footprint with c.500 Offices.” Below this banner, insert a large light gray rounded rectangle acting as a panel that fills most of the upper-left quadrant; inside it, draw a stylized map of Southeast Asia as dozens of small bright turquoise circular dots connected by thin, straight turquoise lines, forming a network pattern without borders. Around this map panel, place nine white rounded rectangles with light gray borders: four stacked along the left side labeled, from top to bottom, “MYANMAR” with “2 offices,” “THAILAND” with “156 offices,” “MALAYSIA” with “48 offices,” and “SINGAPORE” with “72 offices”; and five along the right side labeled, from top to bottom, “GREATER CHINA¹” with “29 offices,” “VIETNAM” with “2 offices,” “PHILIPPINES” with “1 office,” “INDONESIA” with “183 offices,” and “AUSTRALIA” with “3 offices.” In every label box, render the country or region name in uppercase black sans-serif letters slightly larger and bolder than the office count, and place the office count in smaller regular black text on the line below, left-aligned within each box; include a superscript-style numeral “1” after “GREATER CHINA.” Beneath the map panel in the left column, add a three-item vertical bullet list of explanatory text about regional franchise advantages, each line starting with a tiny solid dark blue square bullet and followed by black regular-weight sans-serif body text aligned left. Near the bottom of the slide, spanning almost the full width, add a prominent medium blue banner shaped as a long horizontal strip with a slight rightward-offset darker blue strip beneath its lower edge to create a beveled 3D ribbon effect; inside the main blue strip center the italic white sans-serif sentence “Established regional network with key Southeast Asian pillars, supporting fast-growing trade, capital and wealth flows.” At the very bottom left on the white background, include a small dark gray sans-serif footnote beginning with “1.” followed by a short explanation that this numbered region comprises Mainland China, Hong Kong SAR, and Taiwan; in the bottom right corner place a small dark gray page number “7.” On the right half of the slide, beneath the title and aligned with the left header, position another wide dark blue banner reading “Performance by Segment in 1Q20” in centered bold white sans-serif text. Below this, construct a 2-by-3 grid of six evenly spaced light gray rounded rectangular metric cards with subtle drop shadows, occupying roughly the right half of the slide. The top-left card contains a flat vector icon of a blue shopfront with a red awning and a tiny blue wordmark block, and underneath or beside the icon add the black sans-serif label “Group Retail,” left-aligned. The top-middle card contains the black heading “Operating profit” near the top, a large bold dark blue number “$556m” centered as the main figure, and a smaller dark blue line “+9% YoY” beneath it, all left-aligned or centered as a compact block. The top-right card features a large bold dark blue “$124b” on the left, with the black phrase “Assets under management” and a smaller black “+8% YoY” beneath that figure, and on the right side of the same card place a large bold dark blue “61%” with smaller black text “AUM from overseas customers” beneath it, forming two vertical text blocks within the single card. In the bottom row, the left card holds a flat dark blue vector icon of a classical bank building with a triangular roof and columns, accompanied below by the black sans-serif two-line label “Group Wholesale Banking,” left-aligned. The bottom-middle card mirrors the layout of the top-middle card, showing a black heading “Operating Profit,” a large bold dark blue “$740m” figure, and a smaller dark blue “–5% YoY” line below it. The bottom-right card displays a large bold dark blue “27%” centered or left-aligned near the top with smaller black multiline text underneath reading “Cross-border income to Group Wholesale Banking’s income.” Ensure all cards share the same light gray fill, rounded corners, and drop shadow strength, and that all text across the slide uses only this clean sans-serif typeface with consistent color usage: dark blue for major headings and numeric figures, black or dark gray for descriptive copy, and white only when text appears on blue banners; maintain a clear white-space gap between the left map-and-bullets section and the right metrics grid without any vertical dividing line.
