prompt: Create a wide 16:9 landscape business infographic on a solid white background, divided into three equal-width vertical sections by two thin light gray vertical dotted lines, using dark navy, medium red, and neutral gray as the main colors and a clean modern sans-serif typeface throughout. Centered near the very top, place one bold dark navy headline sentence spanning most of the width; directly below it, draw a long medium-red horizontal banner bar across almost the full canvas, with large bold dark navy text "3S" at its left and three labeled segments reading "Standardization", "Smart digitalization", and "Scale economy" in white or dark navy sans-serif on top of red rectangular areas, separated by plus and arrow symbols, and end this banner with a small rightward-pointing red arrowhead. In the left section under the banner, add a compact horizontal quality-scale chart: a light gray outlined rectangle filled with a continuous row of small adjacent rectangles forming a left-to-right gradient from pale pink through progressively darker grays to very dark navy, with small dark navy labels above such as "imperfection", "Average", and "Premium"; directly beneath this bar, place three small outlined circles arranged horizontally and label them A, B, and C in dark navy, standing for criteria like appearance, tasting, and fragrance & safety. Along the far left edge of this section, draw a tall narrow light gray vertical bar subdivided into four equal-height stacked segments by thin solid gray lines and label these segments with small uppercase dark navy words "plant", "test", "pack", and "trans" running vertically from bottom to top. To the right of this bar, add four horizontal bullet lines of body text in regular dark navy sans-serif, each starting with a small solid red square icon containing a simple white pictogram (a leaf for planting, a checklist for testing, a machine for packing, and a truck for transport), aligning these bullets vertically to correspond to the four process stages. Near the bottom of the left section, insert a dark navy horizontal parallelogram (a skewed rectangle whose top and bottom edges are parallel but angled) without border, containing a centered bold white sans-serif sentence about systematic, refined standardization to ensure quality. In the central section beneath the banner, construct a large light gray circular wheel: form a ring of six pale gray trapezoidal segments arranged radially around a central dark navy circle, and inside that circle draw an atom-like icon with three orbits; label the six outer segments in dark navy sans-serif with short supply-chain stage names "product", "production", "purchasing", "transport", "storage", and "store app". Over the top of this wheel, place a medium-red curved ribbon-like banner that gently arcs across the upper rim and carries the white lowercase word "criteria" in bold sans-serif. Around the wheel, add a few small light gray or red-outlined rectangular annotation boxes connected to specific segments by thin solid gray connector lines, all written in small sans-serif, and beneath the wheel attach a wide light gray triangular base shape pointing downward to suggest platform support. Directly under the triangle, center a row of three equal-sized dark navy rectangular cards with small gaps between them; on each card, draw a simple white line-art device icon (clipboard on the left, computer monitor in the middle, smartphone on the right) with the lowercase placeholder text "write here" in white centered near the bottom of every card. In the rightmost section, align a vertical medium-red bar along the far right edge and attach to it three stacked white horizontal rectangular blocks outlined or sided by red lines, forming a tall column; on the left side of each block place a solid red square badge containing the white number 1, 2, or 3 respectively from top to bottom, and next to each badge write a bold dark navy heading in sans-serif reading "Integration" in the top block, "Utilization" in the middle block, and "Cost-saving" in the bottom block. Within each block, add two or three lines of smaller dark navy explanatory text with occasional red-highlighted keywords; in the "Utilization" block, list three bold red percentages 33%, 70%, and 100% as separate vertical entries. Beneath the third block and still aligned with the right column, include a compact quote area: a large red opening quotation-mark symbol on the left, a short dark navy sentence about how smart digitalization can reduce logistics cost, and a thin vertical red line emphasizing the quote. Ensure that dark navy is the dominant text color everywhere, red is used for banner fills, icons, numbered badges, percentages, and key phrases, gray is used for diagram shapes and connectors, and all connector and annotation lines aside from the vertical dotted section dividers are thin, straight, and solid gray.

prompt: Create a 16:9 corporate presentation slide with a clean white background and a full-width solid medium-blue banner occupying about the top 15% of the canvas. Inside this banner, place a left-aligned line of very large bold white sans-serif title text reading “E) Simple, Modest, & Medium JBP/QBR Options:”, and directly beneath it, still on the blue banner, add a second left-aligned line of smaller regular-weight white sans-serif subtitle text reading “Select, by Partner, Which JBP & MBR / QBR Elements are Needed”. Centered below the banner on the white background, draw a large rounded rectangle that fills most of the slide width and about half of the slide height; give this container a thin medium-blue dashed outline, no shadow, and a pure white interior. Near the top of this container, create a horizontal row of exactly eight equal-size right-pointing chevron arrows that touch edge-to-edge and span almost the full container width, each with flat color fills and no gradient: from left to right color them dark navy, medium blue, bright cyan-blue, bright green, darker forest green, yellow-ochre, medium orange, and bright red. Place centered bold white sans-serif labels inside these chevrons respectively as “Partnership Performance Targets”, “Business Action Plan”, “Capabilities Scorecard”, “Task Management”, “Partner Accept / Brand Approve”, “Account & Deal Planning”, “Deal Progression”, and “Review Progress”. Divide the space beneath the chevrons into eight equal vertical columns using seven very thin vertical dashed light-gray lines that run from just below the chevron row to the bottom edge of the container. In each column, add a centered medium-blue sans-serif heading directly under the corresponding chevron, usually broken onto two lines, reading from left to right: “Set Goals”, “Plan Actions”, “Build Capabilities”, “Track Progress”, “Gain Commitments”, “Support Sales”, “Progress Opportunities”, and “Work Plan”. Under each heading, center an identical flat logo composed of two interlocking curved leaf-like shapes forming a circular emblem, with the left leaf shape in blue and the right leaf shape in green, placed above a small gray uppercase wordmark representing a generic company name; all eight logos are the same size and style. Below each logo, add a single line of bold dark-gray or black sans-serif text centered in the column, with the following phrases from left to right: “Performance Dashboards”, “Business & Sales Action Plans”, “Capabilities Scorecard”, “Task Manager”, “Plan Accept & Approve”, “Opportunity Planning”, “Marketing Plan & MDF”, and “One-Click MBR / QBR”. Leave a small white gap and then place a second wide rounded rectangle directly beneath the main container, aligned in width with it, filled with a very light beige or off-white color and outlined with the same thin medium-blue dashed border. Continue the same seven vertical dashed light-gray separators through this lower strip so it is divided into eight equal vertical cells that align perfectly with the columns above. Inside each cell, place two stacked lines of sans-serif text: a top line in dark gray regular-weight font and a second line immediately beneath it in medium-blue regular-weight font suggesting hyperlink text. From left to right, set the top and bottom lines in each of the eight cells as follows: cell 1 top “Build Partner Commitments”, bottom “Track Progress”; cell 2 top “Plan to Achieve”, bottom “Set and Manage Tasks”; cell 3 top “Assess Capabilities”, bottom “Create Enablement Actions”; cell 4 top “Manage Actions”, bottom “Notify and update status”; cell 5 top “Gain Acceptance”, bottom “Align partner w/ Plans”; cell 6 top “Manage Pipeline”, bottom “Organize push to close”; cell 7 top “Increase Demand”, bottom “ROI investment Planning”; cell 8 top “Review Success”, bottom “Celebrate & Correct Action”. Use only sans-serif fonts throughout the slide, with bold weight reserved for the title, chevron labels, and central descriptive phrases, while other text remains regular weight. Ensure all shapes are flat without gradients, all borders and separators are thin solid or dashed lines as specified, and there is no imagery beyond the eight identical logos and the geometric rectangles, chevrons, and dashed lines, resulting in a precise, symmetrical eight-column layout.

prompt: Create a clean 16:9 landscape presentation slide with a solid white background and no texture. At the top left, place the small section label text "SECURITIES SERVICES" in uppercase bold sans-serif, colored a medium warm brown; directly beneath this label draw a thin solid horizontal brown line extending about one third of the slide width to the right. Slightly lower and centered across the top, add the main title "Our strategic focus areas will drive further growth" in large regular-weight sans-serif font in black or very dark charcoal, and run a thin solid horizontal light gray rule almost the full width of the slide directly underneath this title. Below this rule, construct two main vertical areas. On the left, create a tall rectangular panel aligned to the left margin that occupies roughly one third of the slide width and extends nearly to the bottom; fill this panel with a very light cool gray and give it no border, gradient, or shadow. Above this panel, centered over it, place the header "FACTORS IMPACTING THE BUSINESS" in uppercase bold sans-serif in the same medium brown as the other headings, and draw a thin solid horizontal light gray line beneath this header spanning the width of the left column. Inside the gray panel, arrange four icon-and-text pairs stacked vertically and horizontally centered with even spacing; all icons are simple thin line drawings in the same medium warm brown and have no fill, and all captions are centered below each icon in regular-weight sans-serif dark charcoal text. From top to bottom, use: (1) a globe icon consisting of a circle with longitudinal and latitudinal lines above the caption "Concentration, consolidation of AUM into the largest players"; (2) a computer monitor icon, a rectangle on a short stand, above the caption "Rise of Alts and ETFs"; (3) a downward-sloping bar chart with a descending arrow above the caption "Price compression"; and (4) a small network of several circles connected by straight lines above the caption "New outsourcing needs (data)". Between this left gray panel and the right content area, insert a narrow white chevron-shaped wedge whose color matches the white background; the wedge should taper from the full height of the left panel on its left edge into a pointed triangular form directed to the right, acting as a directional divider. On the right two thirds of the slide, beneath the top light gray rule, place the header "KEY FOCUS AREAS" in uppercase bold sans-serif, colored the same medium brown as the other section titles, aligned left with the cards beneath it; immediately below this header draw another thin solid horizontal light gray rule spanning the width of the right column. Under this header, stack four identical-width rounded-rectangle cards vertically with equal spacing; align them left under the header, and make each card a very light gray rounded rectangle slightly darker than the white background, with large-radius corners, no explicit border, and a soft subtle drop shadow along its lower edge to lift it off the page. Within each card, reserve a square area on the left for an icon and use the remaining width for left-aligned multiline text. For every card, draw a thin brown line-style icon in the left area and use sans-serif text on the right where the first phrase is bold and colored brown and the continuation is regular-weight in black or very dark charcoal. On the first (top) card, use an icon of three ascending bars with an upward-pointing arrow above them, and to its right write "Grow with long-term clients" in bold brown followed immediately by " by providing exceptional service" in regular black. On the second card, draw a cloud outline containing an upward arrow and a downward arrow, and to the right write the bold brown phrase "Invest in data solutions and growth products" followed by the black text " to deepen client relationships and grow revenue". On the third card, use an icon of four arrows pointing outward from a central node like a cross, with the bold brown phrase "Continue to scale our operating model" followed by the black explanatory text " to process higher volumes at a lower cost". On the fourth card, draw a semicircular gauge or speedometer with tick marks and a short arrow pointing diagonally upward, and to the right write the bold brown phrase "Improve productivity" followed by the black text " though automation and investments in AI/ML". At the very bottom-left corner of the slide, add a small horizontal serif wordmark logo in black representing a generic global financial institution, and at the bottom-right corner place a small page number "11" in tiny regular sans-serif black text aligned to the right margin.

prompt: Create a wide, landscape presentation slide with a solid white background and no texture. At the very top left, place a small cyan italic sans-serif phrase giving design credit; directly beneath it, add a single-line bold black sans-serif headline reading about adding to data assets to drive financial inclusion, with the final phrase “content is virtual” in the same line but colored medium cyan-blue. Below this headline, add a slightly smaller, regular-weight black sans-serif subtitle sentence stretching most of the slide width, describing how the platform now scores the entire applying-for-credit population using an expanded range of data. Under the subtitle, draw a thin solid black horizontal rule that spans almost the full width of the slide to separate the header from the main content. On the left side below the rule, create a flush-left column of explanatory text directly on the white background: first a one- or two-line introductory sentence in regular-weight black sans-serif explaining that a premium credit score aggregates expanded compliant data; under this, add three short paragraphs of body text in smaller black sans-serif, each on separate lines, where the first word of each paragraph is “Increased” in bold medium cyan-blue and the remaining words of each sentence remain black. On the center-right of the slide, draw a large perfect circle filled with light neutral grey and no border; it should dominate the right half of the slide. Inside this large circle, center three stacked lines of dark grey to black sans-serif text that read “Traditional Credit Data,” using a medium font size with regular weight and centered both horizontally and vertically. Around this central circle, build a network of smaller circles as data nodes, all with flat solid fills and no outlines or shadows, and all containing centered white sans-serif labels. At the upper-left edge of the big grey circle, place exactly four medium-sized circles filled with medium grey that is slightly darker than the central circle; arrange them so they overlap each other and the large circle, forming a small cluster extending into the upper-left quadrant. Label these four circles with multi-line white sans-serif phrases such as “Buy now pay later,” “Rental,” “Income and employment,” and “Bank transactions,” with each label centered and broken over two to four lines to fit. Along the lower edge of the large central circle, add three overlapping blue circles forming a horizontal cluster that slightly protrudes below the grey circle: the leftmost circle is the largest and filled with a light sky-blue, the middle circle is medium in size and a somewhat deeper sky-blue, and the rightmost circle is slightly smaller than the middle one and in a mid blue; label them respectively with centered white sans-serif multi-line phrases representing “Social security administration records,” “Bank transactions,” and “Alternative finance,” keeping the label font small and regular-weight. On the upper-right edge of the large grey circle, create another cluster of exactly three bright blue circles: one large saturated royal-blue circle at the top, and two slightly smaller vivid blue circles overlapping beneath and to the right; label the large top circle “Consumer-permissioned data,” the lower-left one “Property,” and the rightmost one “Utility and telecom,” each in small centered white sans-serif text on multiple lines. Near the bottom-right edge of the slide, overlapping the lower-right portion of the central light-grey circle, place exactly three dark charcoal-grey circles to form another cluster: the rightmost circle should be the largest and darkest, while the two on the left are slightly smaller and a bit lighter; position one of the smaller circles above and one below the large right circle so that all three overlap. Beneath this header, construct four equally tall horizontal data rows separated by narrow pure white gaps, and divide each row into three vertical rectangles: a narrow left category panel, a wide middle description cell, and a narrower right numeric cell. Fill all left, middle, and right rectangles with the same flat light gray color, slightly darker than the page background, and do not add any borders; use only the white gaps and the gray blocks to define the grid. In the left column of each row, include a light gray vertical panel; in each panel, centered vertically and overlapping its left edge so that about one-third of the shape protrudes into the white margin, place a circular badge with a white interior and a thin mid-gray outline, containing a solid bright red pictogram: in the first row, a simplified processing/ore-handling icon; in the second, a simple factory silhouette with a smokestack; in the third, a human figure swinging a pickaxe; in the fourth, a gear-like symbol suggesting recycling. To the right of each circle, inside the same light gray panel, write the category label in dark gray, medium-weight sans-serif text, left-aligned: first row “Additional processing”, second row “Existing mines”, third row “New projects”, and fourth row “Recycling boost”. In the middle column, draw four matching light gray rectangles aligned with the rows; inside each, create a single bullet paragraph starting near the top left, with a small solid black circular bullet followed by dark gray sans-serif text, left-aligned and wrapping within the cell. For row one, write “Reprocessing of old mines’ tailings accumulated from the mines’ operations”, with the word “Reprocessing” in bold; for row two, “Additional production from available resources not included in current publicly announced production plans”, with the phrase “Additional production from available resources” bolded; for row three, “Projects in early phase of evaluation or financially distressed projects could become more attractive as global supply decreases and prices grow (e.g., BK11, Lace, Jericho, Bunder, Tongo-Tonguma, Chidliak, Lemphane, Kennady North, Naujaat)”, with the central phrase about becoming more attractive as supply decreases and prices grow in bold; and for row four, “Recycling of secondhand diamonds”, with the word “Recycling” bolded. In the right column, place four light gray rectangles aligned with the rows; inside each, add two left-aligned lines of text in regular-weight sans-serif font where the words “Up to”, “or”, and “of supply value” are dark gray but every numeric value, its preceding plus sign, the range dash, and the percent symbol are in the same bright red as the icons. For row one, the first line should read “Up to +8–10 Mct” and the second “or +5–7% of supply value”; for row two, “Up to +15–18 Mct” and “or +10–13% of supply value”; for row three, “Up to +3–5 Mct” and “or +2–3% of supply value”; and for row four, “Up to +1–2 Mct” and “or +0.5–1% of supply value”. Ensure all text outside the header and red numbers is dark gray rather than pure black, that all fonts are clean sans-serif with the header and selected phrases bolded and the rest regular weight, and that no additional lines or borders are drawn. Finally, below the table on the white background, align to the left edge of the table a single small dark gray sans-serif line reading exactly “Sources: Company data; Kimberley Process; expert interviews; Bain analysis”.

prompt: Create a widescreen presentation slide with a solid medium blue rectangular header band spanning the entire top fifth of the canvas, decorated only inside this band with a subtle pattern of thin, lighter-blue intersecting network lines and small circular nodes. Within this blue band, place two lines of left-aligned bold white sans-serif text near the left edge: a very large first line reading “E) Simple, Modest, & Medium JBP/QBR Options:” and, directly below it, a smaller bold subtitle reading “3-Levels of Partner Joint Partner Business Plan and QBR Processes.” Below the header, make the rest of the slide a plain white background. On this white area near the upper right, add a compact company logo: three overlapping curved leaf or flame shapes in green, teal, and dark blue arranged in a circular icon, with a two-line uppercase blue sans-serif wordmark reading “COMPANY NAME” underneath, the second line smaller and slightly letter-spaced. Under the header on the left, add the Level 1 heading row as a single line of text: bold dark-blue sans-serif “Level 1: Set Targets & Track Perf. Plans”, immediately followed by a medium-gray parenthetical phrase “(Plan & Track For all Partners)” and then a lighter gray italic sans-serif phrase “(Easy Effort)” aligned further right on the same baseline. Directly beneath this heading, center a short Level 1 process flow made of two adjacent right-pointing chevron arrows of equal height and forming a continuous row with a small white triangular notch between them; the left arrow is filled with dark navy blue and contains centered bold white sans-serif text “Partnership Performance Targets”, and the right arrow is filled with bright red and labeled in the same style “Review Progress.” Under these arrows, place two small bold sans-serif captions centered beneath their respective arrows: under the navy arrow the dark-blue text “Plan Work” and under the red arrow the red text “Work Plan.” Leave a clear gap, then add the Level 2 heading row: bold dark-blue sans-serif text “Level 2: Sales Development Plans and Reviews” followed by a gray parenthetical phrase “(Sales & Opp.-Focus For Many Partners)” and a lighter gray italic descriptor “(Modest Effort)” on the right. Below this, create the Level 2 process row using five equal-height right-pointing chevron arrows in a single continuous sequence across most of the slide width, each separated by narrow white triangular notches; from left to right, fill them with navy blue, medium blue, olive green, golden yellow, and bright red. Center bold white sans-serif labels in each arrow reading respectively “Partnership Performance Targets”, “Business Action Plan”, “Partner Accept / Brand Approve”, “Account & Deal Planning”, and “Review Progress.” Directly beneath these arrows, add five bold sans-serif captions, each centered under its arrow and using a text color that matches or closely corresponds to the arrow above: from left to right the captions read “Set Goals” (dark blue), “Plan Actions” (medium blue), “Gain Commitments” (green), “Support Sales” (golden yellow), and “Work Plan” (red). After another vertical gap, add the Level 3 heading row: bold dark-blue sans-serif text “Level 3: Comprehensive Business Plans and Reviews” followed by a medium-gray parenthetical description in regular sans-serif such as “(Sales, Marketing, Tech., & Services, For Top Partners)” and then a lighter gray italic descriptor “(Medium Effort)” aligned near the right side. Beneath this heading, construct a long Level 3 process row comprising eight equal-height right-pointing chevron arrows spanning almost the full width, again with small white triangular notches between them; from left to right color them navy blue, medium blue, a slightly lighter or equal medium blue, bright green, olive green, golden yellow, orange, and bright red. Inside these arrows, center bold white sans-serif labels reading in order: “Partnership Performance Targets”, “Business Action Plan”, “Capabilities Scorecard”, “Task Management”, “Partner Accept / Brand Approve”, “Account & Deal Planning”, “Deal Progression”, and “Review Progress.” Under this row, place eight bold sans-serif captions centered beneath each arrow, mostly in dark blue but aligned to their functions: “Set Goals”, “Plan Actions”, “Build Capabilities”, “Track Progress”, “Gain Commitments”, “Support Sales”, “Progress Opportunities” (split onto two stacked lines), and “Work Plan.” Ensure all headings, arrow rows, and caption rows are horizontally aligned within their levels, that every arrow label is centered and in bold white sans-serif type, that all chevron shapes share the same height within a level, and that no patterns or graphics appear on the white background besides the text, arrows, and the small logo at the upper right.

prompt: Create a wide landscape infographic on a solid white background, where the left two-thirds of the canvas is occupied by a large light sky-blue rectangular chart panel and the right third by a tall turquoise side panel. At the top-left on the white background, place the title “Current and planned policies span all 3 sectors with 7 key mechanisms” in large bold dark navy-blue sans-serif text, left-aligned over two lines; at the top-right above the chart, add the small label “DRAFT REPORT” in light gray, uppercase, narrow sans-serif, right-aligned. Inside the light blue chart panel, draw a thin solid medium-blue line along the bottom edge and divide the interior into exactly three equal-height horizontal sector bands using two thin light gray dashed lines. Along the left side of these bands, print three labels in medium-sized dark blue sans-serif text: in the top band “Residential (~119 TWh/yr)”, in the middle band “Services (~96 TWh/yr)”, and in the bottom band “Industrial (~104 TWh/yr)”, each left-aligned without background boxes. Across the very top of the chart panel, on the light blue background, draw a thin solid dark blue horizontal timeline axis with short vertical tick marks, and center beneath it dark blue year labels such as 2007, 2010, 2015, and 2020 at regular intervals; above the left end of this axis, place the label “2010 demand” in medium dark blue sans-serif text. Near the top-right portion of the chart panel, overlay a compact legend consisting of exactly eight horizontal entries stacked vertically; for each entry, draw a small solid square color swatch followed by a left-aligned dark gray sans-serif label in small regular-weight text reading, from top to bottom, “Tax”, “Incentives”, “Financing”, “Information/labelling”, “Standards”, “Supplier obligations”, “Voluntary agreements”, and “In consultation/under review”. Color the legend squares respectively pale yellow, light green, muted orange, pale beige, lavender purple, bright spring green, saturated orange, and pale beige with a subtle hatched or patterned look for the consultation category. Within each sector band, represent policies as horizontal rounded rectangles aligned with the timeline axis and positioned within their respective bands. In the residential (top) band, include bars labeled “Products policy (25 TWh/yr)” in solid lavender purple, “Building regulations (3.3 TWh/yr)” in a slightly darker lavender, “EEC” in medium green, “CERT/CESP” in teal, “ECO” in pale turquoise with a dashed border, “Green Deal (7.5 TWh/yr)” in light cyan-blue with a fine dotted or dashed outline, “Smart metering (3.2 TWh/yr)” in saturated orange, “Green Investment Bank” in pale yellow-green, and “EU ETS (4 TWh/yr)” in pale yellow extending across most of the band; give all these bars low height, rounded corners, and centered small bold sans-serif labels in white on darker fills and dark gray on paler fills. In the services (middle) band, add similar horizontal rounded bars: “Products policy (11 TWh/yr)” in lavender, “Building regulations (6 TWh/yr)” in the slightly darker lavender, “Climate Change Levy” in saturated orange, “CRC energy efficiency scheme (4 TWh/yr)” in orange-toned fill, “Green Investment Bank” in pale yellow-green, “Green Deal” in the same light cyan-blue with dotted outline as above, and “EU ETS (2 TWh/yr)” in pale yellow spanning much of the band width, again with centered small bold sans-serif labels. In the industrial (bottom) band, add “Products policy (2.7 TWh/yr)” in lavender, “Climate Change Levy” and “Climate Change Agreements” both in vivid orange, “Green Deal (0.7 TWh/yr)” in light cyan-blue with a dotted outline, “EU ETS (1 TWh/yr)” in pale yellow running across a large portion of the band, and a long “Enhanced Capital Allowance (2 TWh/yr)” bar at the bottom of the band in bright grass-green matching the incentives legend color, all as low rounded rectangles with centered bold sans-serif labels, using white text on orange and green bars and dark gray on pale bars. On the far right of the canvas, align a tall vertical turquoise rectangle from just below the top margin to the bottom margin; within its top portion, write the heading “The policy landscape is changing” in bold white sans-serif text, and below it place several left-aligned bullet points in smaller white sans-serif text describing review of policies, new policies coming into effect, dominance of Products Policy, and estimated demand reduction in 2020 and 2030, ensuring each bullet uses a solid white circular bullet symbol. Along the bottom of the light blue chart panel, add a thin horizontal band of small black sans-serif footnote text including numbered notes and the line “Source: DECC/DEFRA projections; team analysis”; at the extreme bottom-right corner of the canvas, place a small dark blue circular dot followed by the dark blue sans-serif page number “23”.

prompt: Create a wide 16:9 infographic with a dark navy blue background that has a subtle vertical gradient and is covered by semi-transparent light blue and cyan circuit-board patterns made of thin, straight right-angled lines and small circular pads; on this background, center near the top a large bold white sans-serif title reading "Timeline of Major AI Milestones" and directly below it a smaller italic white sans-serif subtitle reading "From Foundational Ideas to Frontier Models (1950-2025)." In the upper-right corner, place a small abstract tech-lab logo composed of several bright magenta, orange, yellow, green, and cyan circular nodes connected by thin white lines, forming a compact network shape, with two-line white sans-serif text to its right that includes the phrase "AI Lab" on the second line. Below the titles, add a large horizontally oriented rounded rectangle panel that spans almost the full width of the canvas; fill it with a deep royal blue vertical gradient slightly lighter toward the bottom and give it no stroke, just smooth rounded corners. Through the center of this panel draw a single thin solid white horizontal line from left to right as the main timeline, and at each milestone position attach a small filled cyan circular joint on the line and a straight solid white vertical stem up or down from the line; create exactly sixteen such stems and joints, nine extending upward and seven extending downward, all evenly spaced. At the ends of the stems place uniformly sized fully opaque milestone circles: nine circles above the line and seven circles below it, all perfect disks of equal diameter. Fill the top-row circles, from left to right, with bright magenta, violet, medium blue, cyan, bright green, golden yellow, orange, coral, and red respectively, and inside each place a white pictogram icon and a white year label at the bottom in a small bold sans-serif font; the top-row years, in order, must be 1950, 1966, 1997, 2006, 2012, 2016, 2018, 2022, and 2025. The top icons should suggest AI milestones: for example, the 1950 magenta circle shows two opposing human head silhouettes, 1966 shows a simple robot or chatbot face, 1997 shows chess pieces such as a rook and knight, 2006 shows a neural network or microchip symbol, 2012 shows a camera or image icon, 2016 shows a Go board or game icon, 2018 shows a speech-document or language icon, 2022 shows an abstract generative swirl, and 2025 shows interconnected gears or nodes. Fill the bottom-row circles, from left to right, with bright pink, dark blue, green, lime green, amber, teal, and deep red; inside them place white pictograms and white year labels in small bold sans-serif digits corresponding respectively to 1956, the range 1987–1993, 2011, 2014, 2017, 2020, and 2023, with the year range written as "1987–1993"; use icons such as a conference or presentation symbol for 1956, a snowflake for AI winter, a person with a buzzer or TV screen for a quiz victory, stacked blocks or arrows for generative models, a connected-node diagram for the transformer era, a spiral language-model icon for large-scale language models, and a multimodal icon combining text and image symbols for modern models. For each of the nine upper milestones, place directly above its circle a cluster of white text on the blue panel with a bold uppercase sans-serif title naming the milestone (e.g., key tests, chatbots, deep learning breakthroughs, image challenges, game-playing systems, major language-model releases, diffusion image models, and agentic AI), followed by two to four short lines of smaller regular-weight sentence-case description explaining the significance. For each of the seven lower milestones, place its explanatory text below the circle inside a tall narrow darker-blue vertical rectangle column that extends downward from just beneath the circle toward the bottom of the panel; these columns align with their circles and have flat tops and bottoms, no strokes, and slightly darker blue than the main panel so they read as text panels. In each column, use white uppercase bold sans-serif for the milestone title on the top line and smaller white regular-weight sentence-case sans-serif for one or two descriptive sentences below. At the very bottom of the blue panel, add a narrow dark navy-blue horizontal strip running across its width; on the left of this strip write the label "YEAR" in small all-caps white sans-serif, and to its right place evenly spaced white year labels "1950," "1960," "1970," "1980," "1990," "2000," "2010," "2020," and "2025" aligned above their approximate positions along the timeline. Keep every stroke in the infographic, including the circuit traces and timeline connectors, as solid lines with no dashes or dotting, and use only modern sans-serif fonts throughout for all text. Finally, in the extreme bottom-right corner of the entire canvas, add a tiny white italic sans-serif credit phrase indicating a research group, acting as a subtle signature.

prompt: Create a widescreen corporate presentation slide with a very light cool gray background and, centered on it, a large white rectangular card with slightly rounded corners and a subtle drop shadow; at the top of the card place a narrow header: on the far left a bright medium green rounded-rectangle tab labeled “Crop Science” in bold white sans-serif, immediately to its right a circular company logo composed of thin green and blue rings around central text, and on the far right a small light gray tagline reading “Health for all, Hunger for none” in regular sans-serif; below this, center a two-line title block with the main title “Attractive Growing Ag Market to Meet Demand” in large dark teal bold sans-serif and directly beneath it the subtitle “Potential to Double our Accessible Market Through Innovation Investments in Adjacent Spaces” in smaller medium gray regular sans-serif; under the subtitle, place a three-part horizontal pill-shaped bar spanning much of the card width where all three segments have equal height: the left and right segments are filled with medium green and contain centered bold white sans-serif text while the center segment has a white fill with centered dark teal bold text; make the left segment read “Growing Population” and include a simple white line icon of three people on its left side, the center segment read “The Challenge”, and the right green segment read “Pressure on Ecosystem” with a white plant-and-circular-arrows icon at its left; directly beneath the left green segment, add a left-aligned block with a light gray outer circle enclosing a smaller inner solid green circle that contains three stylized white person silhouettes, and to the right of this icon place the bold bright green text “+2.2bn” with two smaller dark gray lines beneath it reading “addition to global population by 2050¹”; beneath the middle white segment, center a circular photo framed by a thin solid teal ring showing a person working in an agricultural field, and around this circle place two short italic dark gray quotes: above-left “Producing More with Less …” and to the right “…in a Changing Climate”; under the right green segment, mirror the left layout by placing a light gray circle with a smaller inner dark green circle that contains a white thermometer-and-leaf icon, and to the left of this icon show large dark green bold text “-17%” with a smaller black line below reading “harvest losses from climate change²”; in the midsection of the slide add a long pill-shaped bar centered horizontally, with the left half filled medium blue and the right half filled dark navy blue, and at the exact center overlay a small white circle containing a solid gray right-pointing arrow icon so it overlaps both color halves; inside the left blue half, left-align bold white sans-serif text “>100bn¹ EUR 2023 Global Ag Input Market”, and inside the right navy half left-align bold white text “>200bn¹ EUR 2030 Global Ag Input Market & Related Adjacencies”; directly below the left part of this bar include a small horizontal legend with two filled circular swatches: a dark forest green circle labeled “Crop Protection” and a light yellow-green circle labeled “Seed & Traits”, both labels in small dark gray regular sans-serif; below this legend, slightly left of center, place a donut chart: a perfect circle with a white center hole and a thick ring where a dark green segment covers about three-quarters of the circumference and a light green segment covers the remaining quarter, both with flat solid fills and no numeric labels; immediately to the right of the donut chart, add a rightward-pointing isosceles triangular photograph of several young bright green seedlings emerging from dark brown soil against a soft-focus background, with the wide base adjacent to the donut and the narrow tip pointing toward the right side of the slide; overlapping the tip of this triangular photo on the right side, draw a large multi-ring circular opportunity graphic larger than the donut chart, composed of at least three concentric rings of equal thickness whose colors transition smoothly around the circle from dark green at the upper left through lighter green and teal to bright blue at the lower right, with two small dark blue dots placed on different ring positions as markers, and at the center a solid white circle containing centered bold dark gray sans-serif text “>2x opportunity”; to the immediate right of this concentric-circle graphic place a left-aligned two-line text block where the first line shows “>2%” in large bright green bold sans-serif and the second line, in smaller bright green regular sans-serif, reads “expected annual growth rate in crop protection and seed & traits market”; beneath this block center a small solid dark blue circle containing a medium-weight white plus sign and directly under it the uppercase label “ADJACENT SPACES” in small medium blue sans-serif; below the label construct a two-column list of six adjacent-space items, each starting with a small light blue circular bullet containing a white right-pointing arrow followed by dark gray regular sans-serif text: in the left column, from top to bottom, “Biofuels”, “Digital Platforms”, and “Carbon”; in the right column, from top to bottom, “Crop Fertility”, “Digital Marketplaces”, and “Precision Application”; finish the slide with a thin bottom footer on the white card showing very small light gray sans-serif text toward the left such as “¹ Company Estimates /// Company AG /// Corporate Overview /// March 2025”, and outside the card in the lower-left corner of the overall light gray background place the slide number “13” in small light teal sans-serif.

between each pair of adjacent panels insert a medium-blue solid downward-pointing arrow with no outline, each positioned vertically between the panels and accompanied by small dark gray sans-serif labels showing rate information such as "Visit rate: 35%-40%" near the top arrow, diagnosis and treatment rates near the second, and subgroup percentages like "HR+HER-: 65-70%" and additional percentage ranges near the third, with some percentage values optionally colored blue for emphasis; to the immediate right of the flow stack, overlapping slightly horizontally into the central area, draw a medium-sized vertical light-neutral-gray rounded rectangle with uniform fill and no border, containing left-aligned black serif text paragraphs that explain the average monthly and annual treatment cost per patient and expected numbers of treated patients, with key dollar amounts (for example "$15,000", "$180,000" and ranges of total sales) and patient counts highlighted using blue or red colored text and in some cases italic; occupying most of the right third of the slide, behind and slightly below this smaller box, add a larger tall light-gray rounded rectangle of the same gray tone that fills most of the right side margin, containing several paragraphs of left-aligned black serif text describing the overall market size for CDK4/6 inhibitors, expected total market values for 2023, 2024, and 2027, the projected market share range for Atirmociclib, the typical development and approval timelines, the anticipated U.S. launch year, and projected revenue ranges, with all key financial figures (market sizes in billions of dollars), year counts, and percentage ranges shown in dark blue or dark red text and sometimes italicized, while the surrounding explanatory words remain regular black; above the bar chart area on the right, place a single-line left-aligned serif subheading in medium blue that reads "2. prompt: Create a clean presentation slide on a solid white background showing a bubble chart titled “Strong retail presence in high potential regional markets.” Place the title as two lines of large bold dark steel blue sans-serif text in the upper left, and in the upper right add a compact generic bank logo made of three narrow vertical red bars followed by dark blue uppercase initials in bold sans-serif. In the left-center of the slide draw an L-shaped bubble chart area with thin solid light gray axes: a vertical axis on the left and a horizontal axis along the bottom. Label the vertical axis in black sans-serif with the stacked text “Banking penetration growth potential,” add the word “High” near the top of this axis and “Low” near the bottom, and label the horizontal axis “Population” centered underneath, with the word “Small” near its left end and “Large” near its right end. Use only filled circles to represent data points, each with a thin solid outline; most bubbles should be light bluish-gray with slightly darker gray borders and gray numerals, while the bank’s core markets use solid dark blue bubbles with matching borders and white numerals. In the lower right of the chart, near the “Large” label, draw the largest light gray bubble labeled “114” in gray, with the country name “Japan” in black sans-serif to its right; just left of this bubble place two short diagonal hash marks across the horizontal axis to indicate a broken axis. Above and slightly left of Japan place a medium-large light gray bubble labeled “37” for “India,” with its country name to the right, and ensure this India bubble lies near the right side of a large dashed rectangle. In the lower center region add a large light gray bubble labeled “53” for “Australia” with its label above or to the right, and to its right add a medium light gray bubble labeled “35” for “South Korea” with its name slightly above it. At the lower left near the origin create a cluster of three overlapping bubbles: two light gray circles labeled “18” for “Hong Kong” and “18” for “Taiwan,” plus one dark blue circle labeled “15” for “Singapore”; all three should have thin borders and their country names in nearby black sans-serif text, with only the Singapore bubble filled in dark blue and its numeral in white. Slightly to the right and higher than this cluster, draw a small light gray bubble labeled “9” for “UAE,” with “UAE” in black text next to it. In the upper middle of the chart, enclose a set of Southeast Asian bubbles inside a large axis-aligned rectangle drawn using medium-thickness dashed lines in medium-dark blue; the rectangle should have no fill so the interior remains white. Along the left edge inside this dashed rectangle add a dark blue bubble at low-to-mid height labeled “9” for “Malaysia,” with its country name in black text; near the central area of the rectangle add another dark blue bubble labeled “12” for “Thailand,” and directly above and slightly left of this bubble place a dark blue checkmark icon with the black sans-serif caption “TMRW launched in Mar 2019” in two stacked lines. On the right side within the same dashed rectangle place a dark blue bubble labeled “21” for “Indonesia” above the light gray India bubble, with “Indonesia” in black text to its right. Near the top of the dashed rectangle add a small dark blue bubble labeled “8” for “Vietnam,” with “Vietnam” in black next to it, and just below or slightly right of Vietnam inside the rectangle add a small light gray bubble labeled “3” for “Philippines,” with “Philippines” in black text beside it. Ensure that in total there are thirteen country bubbles: Japan (114, light gray), India (37, light gray), Australia (53, light gray), South Korea (35, light gray), Taiwan (18, light gray), Hong Kong (18, light gray), Singapore (15, dark blue), UAE (9, light gray), Vietnam (8, dark blue), Philippines (3, light gray), Malaysia (9, dark blue), Thailand (12, dark blue), and Indonesia (21, dark blue), each with a clearly readable numeric value inside and its country name nearby in black sans-serif. To the right of the chart, roughly centered vertically, add a vertically stacked legend headed by the black sans-serif line “2019 retail banking pool sizes”; beneath it show a light bluish-gray gradient circle labeled “USD b” and below that a solid dark blue circle labeled “Denotes the bank’s core markets in Southeast Asia,” all left-aligned. At the very bottom left of the slide include two lines of small black sans-serif footnote text reading “Source: BCG banking pools (2019), World Bank (2017)” and “Note: UAE and Japan retail banking market size as of 2017.” Finally, place a tiny black slide number “32” in the bottom right corner of the slide.

prompt: Create a clean corporate presentation slide on a solid white landscape canvas, with all content centered and aligned on a neat grid, using dark navy blue, medium cool gray, bright red, black, and white as the only colors. At the top center, place a single-line title reading 'Strategic initiatives to tap regional flows' in a large bold dark navy blue sans-serif font, mixed case, with no underline or italics. In the top-right corner, add a generic corporate logo composed of four narrow vertical bright red rectangles of equal size, placed side by side with small gaps, followed to the right by a short blue uppercase sans-serif wordmark, and draw a thin bright red horizontal line directly beneath the wordmark as an underline. Below the title, arrange three equal-width vertical banner-like panels in a horizontal row (left, middle, right), each made of a medium gray rectangular header bar at the top, a taller white rectangular body, and a dark navy blue downward-pointing chevron-shaped base at the bottom; the three panels are separated by equal white gaps. In each gray header, on the left side, insert a white circle with a dark navy blue outline containing a simple dark blue line icon: for the left panel a globe-style icon with latitude and longitude lines, for the center panel an icon suggesting connected people or sectors, and for the right panel a thin star-like symbol with short radiating lines to imply ideas or capabilities. In the left gray header, write the text 'Strengthened Connectivity' in uppercase bold white sans-serif, centered vertically; in the middle header, write 'Sector Specialisation' in uppercase bold white sans-serif; and in the right header, write 'Products and Platforms' in uppercase bold white sans-serif. In the white body of the left panel, place a bold dark navy blue sans-serif subheading at the top that reads 'Tapping Greater China / Southeast Asian flows', and beneath it add two left-aligned bullet points using small solid dark navy blue circular dots and regular-weight dark navy blue sans-serif text: the first line 'Support regional needs of companies from Southeast Asia and Greater China' and the second line 'Singapore remains attractive as hub for region'. In the dark navy blue chevron base of this left panel, center the white sans-serif text 'Cross-border income: +10% growth¹ and 28% of GWB income' on one or two lines, keeping the superscript ¹ attached to the percentage. In the white body of the middle panel, place a bold dark navy blue sans-serif subheading 'Offering tailored solutions', then two left-aligned bullet points with the same small solid dark navy blue circular dots and regular-weight dark navy blue sans-serif text: 'Improve customer engagement with insights and sectoral benchmarking' and 'Well-positioned to bank opportunities from clients’ ongoing diversification'. In the dark navy blue chevron base of the middle panel, center two separate lines of white sans-serif text: the first line 'Non-loan income: +8%¹' and directly below it 'Non-real estate income: +6%¹', both retaining the superscript ¹. In the white body of the right panel, add a bold dark navy blue sans-serif subheading 'Building new capabilities', followed by three left-aligned bullet points using the same small solid dark navy blue circular dots and regular dark navy blue sans-serif text: 'Financing frameworks² to support green and sustainable development', 'Re-designed customer journeys', and 'Faster speed to market', preserving the superscript ² in the first bullet. In the dark navy blue chevron base of the right panel, center two stacked sections of white sans-serif text: first 'Total Sustainable Financing³: > SGD 6 billion' and beneath it 'Targeted cost productivity improvement: ~10%⁴', keeping the superscript numerals ³ and ⁴ in place. Ensure all panel body text is left-aligned within its white area while all chevron text is horizontally centered, and that bullets across all three panels share the same size and spacing. Along the bottom left of the slide, add four small regular-weight sans-serif footnotes in dark gray or black, each starting with an Arabic numeral followed by a period: '1. 2019 year on year growth.', '2. Real Estate Sustainable Finance Framework and Green Infrastructure Framework.', '3. Includes green loans, sustainability-linked loans and loans for green certified buildings.', and '4. 2021 target.'. At the extreme bottom-right corner of the slide, place a small light gray sans-serif page number reading '35'.

prompt: Create a corporate presentation slide on a solid white background in a wide 16:9 landscape format. Across the upper part of the slide place a single left-aligned sentence in a large sans-serif font reading "Maximizing long term shareholder value is our priority, and 17% ROTCE is an outcome, not a constraint"; render most of the title text in dark charcoal gray but make only the words "shareholder value" bold and colored warm medium brown. Just above and slightly to the left of this title, draw a short solid horizontal line segment in the same medium brown. Below the title, divide the main content area into two equal-width panels, each enclosed by a thin solid very light gray rectangular border on a white interior. In the left panel, add a small uppercase brown sans-serif heading near the top left that reads "17% IS AN EXCEPTIONAL ROTCE" and under it construct a heatmap-style table: along the top, create a header row of ten equal-width pale beige rectangular cells labeled in centered dark gray sans-serif text "2015", "2016", "2017", "2018", "2019", "2020" with a small superscript "2", "2021" with a small superscript "2", "2022", "2023", and "2024"; along the left, form a first column of twelve label cells, with the top label cell filled dark brown and containing centered white bold sans-serif text (a generic three-letter abbreviation such as "AAA"), and the remaining eleven label cells filled very light beige with centered dark gray sans-serif three-letter uppercase abbreviations (for example "BBB", "CCC", etc.). Fill the interior 10-by-12 grid of data cells so that each small rectangle is a solid, fully opaque color—either vivid red, mustard yellow, or bright grass green—with no gradients or patterns; populate only the top institution row of these colored cells with centered white bold sans-serif percentage values such as "13%", "17%", "21%", and "22%" corresponding to the cell colors, leaving the remaining colored cells blank. Directly beneath the table, place a horizontal legend consisting of three equal-width adjacent rectangles touching edge to edge: the left block bright red with centered white text "<10%", the middle block mustard yellow with centered white text "10-16%", and the right block vivid grass green with centered white text "≥17%"; do not draw borders between these three blocks. Under this legend, add a tiny mid-gray sans-serif footnote reading "For footnoted information, refer to slide 22" and beneath that, near the bottom left margin, include a generic gray serif wordmark resembling a financial institution name without using any real company brand. In the right panel, at the top left inside the border, place a small uppercase brown sans-serif heading "ALLOCATING CAPITAL TO MAXIMIZE SHAREHOLDER VALUE ≠ MAXIMIZING ROTCE" and just beneath it a legend row containing two small horizontal color swatches: first a dark chocolate brown rectangle with the brown sans-serif label "Capital intense" to its right, and second a light tan-brown rectangle with the label "Capital light" to its right. Below this legend, construct a vertical bar chart: on the left side, draw a vertical axis with the title "ROE (%)" in small uppercase brown sans-serif text, and place dark gray sans-serif tick labels at 0%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 25%, and 30% with faint thin solid light gray horizontal gridlines extending across the chart at each tick. Populate the chart with multiple solid vertical bars of varying heights and widths arranged from left to right, using fully opaque dark chocolate brown and light tan-brown fills that alternate to match the legend; the leftmost cluster should be a wide dark brown bar representing a low-to-mid-teens return, the middle several bars should be medium-width and reach around or above the 17% level, and the rightmost two bars should be tall and narrow, extending beyond the 30% gridline. Across the entire width of the bar area at approximately the 17% level, draw a horizontal dashed line in medium green and place the green sans-serif label "17% target" just above the left portion of this line. Around the 10% level, overlay a semi-transparent pale beige horizontal band with softly blurred top and bottom edges stretching across all bars, and on top of this band write the underlined italic dark blue sans-serif phrase "Indicative cost of equity" centered horizontally. Over the lower half of the leftmost wide bar, place a dark brown rectangular callout box containing centered white sans-serif text on two lines that reads "Bar width = capital allocated to the business" and beneath the text, inside the box, a straight white double-headed arrow running horizontally from left to right with triangular arrowheads at both ends. At the bottom of the chart area, draw a long brown brace shaped like a flattened curly bracket spanning nearly the full width under the bars, and just below this brace add an italic medium-brown sans-serif caption reading "A range of our sub-lines of business". On the faces of the two tallest rightmost bars, place white sans-serif text "> 30%" near their tops, and at the extreme upper right margin of the chart, outside the plot area, add small brown sans-serif text ">> 30%". Finally, in the bottom right corner of the slide on the white background, place a very small light gray sans-serif page number "16" aligned close to the right and bottom edges.

prompt: Create an educational poster layout organized as a single vertical column of main sections centered between the left and right page edges, surrounded by small decorative illustrations along the margins. At the very top, design a full-width header band composed of three stacked rows: the top row contains scattered small cartoon characters and icons near the upper left, upper center, and upper right edges; the middle row contains a single large English word logo centered horizontally; and the bottom row contains two lines of large Chinese title text centered under the logo. Directly beneath this header, place a small rounded rectangular label box aligned near the upper left of the content area, containing a short Chinese phrase introducing basic core rules. Below the label, add the first main rule block as a wide rounded rectangle occupying the upper-middle of the page; inside it, arrange four vertical rows: row one is a title row with a circled number on the far left, followed by a line of Chinese rule text and then a horizontal arrow extending toward the right where a pair of simple subject and object icons sits; row two is a multi-line list of applicable singular subjects, built from several short text-and-icon groups stacked vertically and left-aligned; row three is a single narrow horizontal strip running across the width of the block with a concise explanation of how verbs change, including brief English verb examples; and row four consists of three example sentence groups stacked one above another, each group made of an English sentence on the first line and a Chinese explanatory sentence directly below it, all left-aligned. Immediately below this first rule block, place the second main rule block as another wide rounded rectangle of similar width, also divided into four vertical rows: row one again has a circled number on the far left, followed by Chinese rule text and a horizontal arrow pointing to a cluster of simple icons on the right; row two lists plural subjects with several short text-and-icon groups stacked vertically and left-aligned; row three is a single horizontal strip explaining plural verb changes with brief English examples; and row four contains exactly two example sentence groups stacked vertically, each group having an English sentence line with a Chinese sentence directly beneath. At the very bottom of the page, beneath the second rule block, add a long narrow footer band spanning the full width; inside this footer center a single line of Chinese text summarizing the core principle that the form and number of the verb must match the subject. Around all these central sections, distribute small decorative icons of animals, plants, and simple objects along the outer border areas without interfering with the structured header, label, two rule blocks, and bottom footer band.

prompt: Create a single-page travel poster layout with a clear structural organization. At the very top, place one horizontal header banner as the topmost section, centered across the page, containing the main tour title text in a single line. Around the outside of this central map, arrange eight rectangular day information panels as separate cards that do not touch each other. On the right side of the page, to the right of the map, create four vertically arranged day panels: near the upper-right, place the Day 1 panel, and directly below it place the Day 2 panel so that their right edges align; both of these are two-row, single-column cards with a scenic thumbnail image filling the upper row and a block of Day 1 or Day 2 text with a short list in the lower row. In the lower-right quadrant, below the map, place the Day 3 panel slightly inset from the right margin, and to its lower-right side near the bottom-right corner place the Day 4 panel so that Day 4 is the lowest right-side card; these two panels also use the same two-row layout with image on top and text below. On the left side of the page, to the left of the map, create three more day panels: position the Day 6 panel midway down the left edge, then place the Day 7 panel directly below Day 6 near the bottom-left corner, both flush toward the left page margin; each of these is again a two-row card with an image in the upper row and text in the lower row. Slightly to the right of Day 7 and below the left part of the map, place the Day 5 panel in the bottom-center-left area, aligned so it forms a loose triangle with Day 6 and Day 7 while still using the same image-above-text two-row structure. For the remaining day, position the Day 8 panel just below the header and slightly left of the page’s vertical centerline so that it overlaps the upper-left portion of the map area; this panel is also a rectangular two-row card with a thumbnail image on top and descriptive text below. Within the central map, ensure the eight numbered markers correspond spatially to the eight surrounding day panels, following the route from the right side toward the left and then back, and include transport icons like an airplane near the upper-right of the map and a vehicle along the route, plus at least one large animal illustration in the right-central area of the map. prompt: Create a wide educational poster layout organized into three main horizontal bands with two tall side illustrations. At the very top, place a full-width header section that contains three lines of centered text stacked vertically: a small account handle on the first line, a large Chinese main title on the second line, and a shorter subtitle line describing English sentence components on the third line. Below this header, after a subtle horizontal divider, add a central sentence analysis area arranged as a strict grid with three horizontal rows and eleven vertical columns, centered horizontally on the page. In each of the eleven columns, stack three elements vertically: at the top of the column, place one English word or multi-word phrase of the example sentence; directly below that, in the same column, place a grammatical role label in English such as “Subject,” “Predicate,” or similar; and directly below each label place a compact explanatory text element in Chinese. Ensure that all top words or phrases form a single continuous row, all grammatical labels form a second row aligned directly under the words, and all explanatory texts form a third row aligned directly under the labels, so that each column’s three elements are vertically aligned and the columns collectively form a neat 3-by-11 grid. Immediately beneath this grid, separated by another horizontal divider, create a bottom section divided into three equally sized vertical panels laid out as a single horizontal row spanning the full width. Each of these three panels is a self-contained rectangular area with its own internal vertical structure: at the top of each panel place a bold heading in Chinese, directly below add a smaller subtitle or label line, and beneath that include one or more blocks of explanatory Chinese text describing different categories of sentence parts. Align the top edges of the three panels along the same horizontal line so they read as three equal columns. Along the far left edge of the overall poster, overlay a tall cartoon rabbit detective character that extends from near the top header down into the region of the leftmost bottom panel, ensuring the figure partially overlaps both the central sentence grid area and the bottom left panel while remaining entirely to the left of the grid’s columns. Along the far right edge, overlay a tall cartoon fox character that similarly extends from near the top header down into the rightmost bottom panel, partially overlapping both the central area and the bottom right panel while remaining entirely to the right of the grid’s columns. Scatter small decorative icons such as donut-like shapes, clouds, and paw prints loosely around the header, between the grid and the panels, and within the bottom band, without aligning them into strict rows or columns so they act only as minor embellishments between the major structured sections. Overall, maintain a clear hierarchy of stacked horizontal bands (header, sentence grid, three-panel section) flanked on both sides by the two vertical character illustrations, with all textual content confined within the header, the grid cells, and the three lower panels.

prompt: Create a wide, landscape infographic layout organized into four main horizontal bands stacked vertically: a full-width header band at the top, an upper informational band beneath it, a central bottle timeline band in the middle, and a bottom data band spanning the full width at the bottom. prompt: Create a single-page farm scene illustration laid out as one continuous canvas organized loosely as a three-by-three implicit grid with a top horizontal band for distant background, a middle band for the main animals and water feature, and a bottom band for garden and foreground animals. In the top-left band, place a large barn structure with a storage tower behind it, and in front of this building arrange three cows spread across the sloping field; above the central cow, place a rectangular callout label containing three vertically stacked text lines (the English word COW in uppercase, an IPA pronunciation, and the Chinese name) with a small triangular pointer leading down to the cow. To the right of the cows, in the upper-center of the scene, put a single horse on a path that runs across the hillside; position the HORSE label between the cow label and the pigs below, close to the horse, again as a three-line rectangular label with a pointer to the horse. In the top-right portion, add a farmhouse near the upper-right edge and a tractor with a driver on a curved path slightly below and left of the house, both forming a grouped background element. Directly below the tractor and to the right of the horse, populate a pasture area with three sheep grouped together on the left side and, to their right, a goat standing on higher rocky ground; place a SHEEP label near the sheep with its pointer aimed at one of them and a GOAT label immediately to the right of the goat with its pointer aimed at the goat. In the central middle band, create an irregular muddy pool centered horizontally and fill it with four pigs in different positions; put a single PIG label just below the mud area, centered, with a pointer rising toward the pigs. Along the middle-left of the page beneath the cows, construct a chicken coop building at the back left with an open doorway, and extend a fenced run forward and to the right that contains several adult chickens and multiple chicks; at the front edge of this run, place a CHICKEN label in front of the birds with a pointer directed back into the flock. Immediately below this chicken zone and to the left of the pig mud pool, place a single turkey standing in the grassy foreground, and add a TURKEY label slightly to the right of the bird with its pointer aimed toward the turkey. On the middle-right side opposite the pigs, draw a pond occupying the right part of that band and put two adult ducks and two smaller ducklings in the water; position a DUCK label overlapping the left edge of the pond so that its pointer connects to one of the adult ducks. In front of the lower-right edge of the pond, place a goose standing on the grass; to the left of the goose, slightly below the DUCK label, add a GOOSE label whose pointer faces the goose. For the bottom band, fill the bottom-left corner with a rectangular vegetable garden plot inside a low fence, with several rows of plants; extend the fence from the garden rightward so it helps enclose a central grassy area. In the bottom-center of this fenced foreground, place a dog sitting on the grass, and above and slightly left of its head add a DOG label with its pointer descending to the dog. Continue the fence toward the bottom-right so that it forms a short post or platform where a cat lies between the dog and the rabbit hutch; position a CAT label just below and slightly right of the cat with a pointer pointing up at the cat. Finally, in the bottom-right corner, construct a multi-compartment rabbit hutch aligned with the page edges, featuring an upper enclosed section and a lower caged section, with rabbits visible both inside the hutch and outside on the ground and ramp at its front; place a RABBIT label in the grass in front of the hutch near the bottom edge of the page with its pointer directed toward the nearest rabbit. Ensure there are twelve rectangular labels in total (COW, HORSE, SHEEP, GOAT, PIG, CHICKEN, TURKEY, DUCK, GOOSE, DOG, CAT, RABBIT), each using the same three-line stacked text structure and each positioned close to its corresponding animal with a clear triangular pointer, while all other elements respect the described left, right, above, and below relationships within this single continuous farm layout.

prompt: Create a single-page infographic layout with a one-column structure. At the very top, place a single full-width row containing a centered title line of text. Immediately to the right of this central figure, align a vertical column of three small horizontal label boxes spaced evenly from top to bottom; each label is left-aligned and contains an English frequency label, from top to bottom reading Low Frequency, Mid Frequency, and High Frequency, with the top label roughly aligned with the upper part of the central figure, the middle label aligned with its middle, and the bottom label aligned with its lower part. On the far right side of the canvas, roughly centered vertically relative to the page and overlapping the height range of the central figure, place a tall rectangular text panel with rounded corners; inside this panel, arrange all content in a single vertical column of five rows: at the top row include a short heading sentence about working together on a project accompanied by a small crown icon near the text, followed by three successive rows where each row contains a small character icon on the left and a short explanatory English text block on the right, and finally at the bottom row add a concluding full-width sentence about everyone having their own level of learning and updating. On the left half of the canvas create three distinct small character scenes associated with levels and connected by a path: position the Level 1 scene in the upper-left to upper-central area between the banner and the central figure, placing the character in a small vehicle on top of a cloud with a speech bubble containing the English text Level 1; place the Level 2 scene lower down in the lower-left quadrant but above the bottom caption, with the character in a similar vehicle resting on rolling hills and a speech bubble labeled Level 2; and place the Level 3 scene in the upper-right quadrant, on top of mountain peaks to the right of the central nested figure and to the left of the right-side text panel, with a speech bubble labeled Level 3. Along the bottom edge of the illustration, within the left third of the width and beneath the Level 2 scene and part of the central figure, place a two-line left-aligned caption in English that reads This is the treasure we are looking for: Nested Learning (NL)!, positioned slightly above the lower frame border so it does not touch the edge. In the middle background behind the characters and main panels, add a continuous band of landscape elements that spans from the left to the right side of the page, with lower hills under and behind the Level 2 scene and the central figure and taller mountains rising behind the Level 3 scene, ensuring these landscape shapes remain visually behind the level scenes, the central nested figure, the labels, and both text panels. prompt: Create a vertical, poster-style layout with a full-page illustrated background and a clear structural division between a top header, a central sequence of six steps, and a bottom illustration band. At the very top, build a full-width header section divided into two side-by-side groups: on the left place a compact illustration of three children sitting together, and on the right place the main title text reading “FIGHT BULLYING,” with a single prohibition symbol positioned immediately to the left of the title and a second prohibition symbol positioned immediately to the right, so that this title block occupies the upper-right portion of the page. Below this header, construct the main body as a vertical flow of six step groups connected by a single dashed line. Each step group consists of a circular icon containing a simple symbol on the left and a rounded rectangular text box on the right, arranged in a short horizontal row where the icon and rectangle share a common vertical center; inside each rectangle, place one heading line of text at the top and a smaller explanatory paragraph directly beneath it. Arrange these six icon-and-rectangle groups so they alternate horizontally while stacking vertically: the first group sits below the header on the left side of the page, the second group is directly below the first on the left side forming a left column, the third group is placed further down but aligned to the right side of the page, the fourth group is directly under the third on the right side forming a right column, the fifth group appears below these but returns to the left side, and the sixth group is directly below the fifth on the left side, so that the layout forms an alternating left-right pattern as the viewer moves downward. Connect the centers of all six circular icons with a dashed path that begins at the topmost left icon, descends vertically, then jogs horizontally as needed to reach the icons on the opposite side, continuing until it reaches the bottommost icon, so that the path clearly links the six steps in reading order. Keep all six step groups within a central vertical band of the page, leaving comfortable margins at the extreme left and right for background and character illustrations. Around but not overlapping the text rectangles, position small character scenes: between the header and the first left step place children reading or sitting near the top-left; between and beside the early steps place playful children illustrations; near the right-side steps include a child with a small pet adjacent to the rectangles; and near the lower-left steps place two children and a taller adult figure close to but outside the text boxes. At the bottom of the page, add a wide illustration band acting like a footer: in the bottom-left corner, directly under the lowest left-aligned text box, depict a water area with two birds floating in it, and in the bottom-right corner, to the right of the lower text boxes, depict a standing parent holding a child with a dog on a leash, plus slightly above and to their left a child sitting and reading inside a tent on a picnic mat. Ensure the header, the six connected step cards, and the bottom illustrations are clearly separated vertically while remaining integrated within one continuous illustrated background.
