prompt: Create a single-page, portrait-oriented infographic layout. At the very top, spanning the entire width, place a broad rectangular header band containing a large, centered title line of text, with small decorative icons positioned near the far left and far right edges of the band. Directly beneath this header, add a narrower horizontal banner that also spans the page width and contains a centered subtitle, visually separating the header from the rest of the content. Below the subtitle, divide the remaining page into two main horizontal content bands stacked vertically: an upper content band occupying the upper half of the page and a lower content band occupying the lower half. In the upper band, arrange three main panels as a single row of three columns: a left panel, a central panel, and a right panel, all similar in height. The left panel is a rounded container that includes a circular island map graphic positioned in its upper area, with several short text lines and labels describing location, population, access, and facilities placed around and beneath the circular map, all kept within this same container. The central panel shows a schoolchild standing beside a bicycle in its upper portion, with the child and bicycle illustration centered horizontally; directly below this illustration, inside the same panel, place a bordered text block that lists the child’s name, age, status as the only student, and the geographic location, arranged in a compact vertical stack of lines. The right panel of the upper band is a taller, vertically oriented container whose content is stacked: at the top of this panel, place a small school building icon; beneath it, arrange a vertically flowing block of left-aligned text describing the size of the school, number of classrooms, subjects, and teacher information; closer to the bottom of this same panel, include a playground scene showing the child near swings, and attach a speech bubble overlapping the lower part of the panel that contains a brief playground-related remark. Center all three upper-band panels within the page width so their top edges align horizontally under the subtitle. Beneath these, form the lower content band as another single row of three main groups aligned with the columns above: a left timeline cluster, a central reason panel, and a right cost panel. The left timeline cluster consists of two rectangular text boxes stacked vertically inside a loose grouped area; the upper rectangle describes the situation until a certain month, and the rectangle directly below describes the situation since that month, with arrows connecting or pointing between these two boxes to indicate progression. Directly beneath this pair of rectangles, still in the left column area, place a small circular vignette illustration of the child engaged in outdoor activities, with a short caption placed immediately below or partially inside the circular frame. In the center of the lower band, directly under the central upper panel, place a single bordered rectangular panel that contains a left-aligned paragraph explaining the main reason for the island’s depopulation in its upper portion, and anchor a factory building illustration along the bottom edge of the same rectangle so that the factory graphic occupies the lower segment; from this factory area, draw an upward-pointing arrow that leads toward the cost panel in the right column. In the right column of the lower band, directly beneath the school panel, create a large bordered container dedicated to financial information; at the top of this panel, place one or two lines of left-aligned text describing the annual cost of schooling, and below this text, in the middle of the panel, center a large currency symbol alongside a calculator icon. Under these financial icons, add additional left-aligned text describing comparisons, including at least one comparison label accompanied by a cross or mark, all kept within the same panel. At the very bottom of this right-hand panel, include a small boxed note summarizing a parent’s worries, placed next to a small character illustration, both aligned along the lower edge of the container. Throughout the layout, ensure that text within panels is generally left-aligned, major illustrations are centered within their respective containers, arrows are used between key text boxes and panels to guide reading order, and the overall composition reads from the top header downward through the upper band and then through the lower band in a clear left-to-right and top-to-bottom flow.

prompt: Create a vertically oriented single-page layout organized as a single central column with three main stacked sections: a top header, a middle circular infographic, and a bottom call-to-action bar. In the topmost section, place a centered title block that spans the page width, consisting of a large leading numeral on the first line and two lines of heading text directly underneath it, all treated as one grouped header unit. Below this header, leave some clear space, then position a large circle centered horizontally on the page; this circle should dominate the middle portion of the layout. Inside this large circle, create a smaller inner circle at the exact center. Subdivide the area between the inner and outer circles into five equal wedge-shaped segments arranged radially, forming a five-part ring around the inner circle. Within each wedge, place a heading line of text near the inner area of the segment and a supporting line of text directly below that heading, so that each wedge serves as a self-contained text panel. On the boundary between the inner circle and each wedge, place one small numbered circular badge so there are five badges total; align badge 1 at the top, badge 2 on the right, badge 3 at the lower right, badge 4 at the bottom, and badge 5 on the left, each positioned midway along the dividing line that leads into its corresponding wedge. Draw separating lines that extend from each badge outward until they meet the perimeter of the large circle, clearly delineating the five radial segments. Keep the inner circle reserved for a central illustration, with its center aligned to the overall page center. Beneath the large circle, leave some space and then add a horizontal bar centered near the bottom of the page; this bar is a rounded rectangle or similar container divided into two equal side-by-side rectangular areas by a single vertical divider positioned at the midpoint. In the left half of this bar, place a short call-to-action phrase such as “Follow us,” and in the right half place a handle-like text such as “@reallygreatsite,” with the text in each half centered within its own area. Ensure there are no additional sections outside these three primary ones, and that all textual content resides in either the header block, one of the five wedge-shaped panels, or the bottom bar.

prompt: Create a vertically oriented single-page restaurant menu layout where all content is arranged in one main column from top to bottom. At the very top, place a horizontal decorative header row containing a utensil-and-flame icon anchored at the far left edge, a mirrored utensil-and-flame icon anchored at the far right edge, and an open central area between them. Directly beneath this decorative row, in the upper center of the page, position a title block consisting of two lines of text stacked vertically and centered horizontally: a smaller curved subtitle on top and a larger main title reading MENU below it. Immediately below the title block, place a single multi-line welcome paragraph, treated as one text block, centered horizontally and spanning the middle portion of the page. Under this paragraph, occupy the central body area with a menu_grid section configured as a 3-row by 2-column grid of six item groups, centered horizontally so that equal margins remain on both sides. Each of the three rows contains two item groups arranged side by side, one in the left column and one in the right column, forming a clear grid. Inside every item group, arrange elements in a consistent pattern: on the left side of the group place a circular food image; to the immediate right of this image place a horizontal title-and-price band aligned in the same horizontal row as the image; directly beneath this band, still to the right of the image, place a paragraph of description text, left-aligned within the text area. The three item groups in the left column are stacked vertically one under another, and the three item groups in the right column are also stacked vertically, with each right-column group horizontally aligned with the corresponding left-column group to form three distinct rows. Below the entire menu_grid, add a full-width footer bar that serves as the bottommost section. Divide this footer into two equal columns forming a single horizontal row: in the left column, place a phone information block with a phone icon on the far left and, to its right, a vertical stack of two lines of text consisting of a PHONE label above a phone number; in the right column, place a location information block with a location pin icon on the far left and, to its right, a vertical stack of two lines of text consisting of a LOCATION label above an address. Ensure that the decorative header, title block, intro paragraph, menu grid, and footer appear as distinct horizontal bands stacked sequentially from top to bottom, with the grid and footer elements internally aligned as described.

prompt: Create a single-page vertical infographic with a header–body layout whose primary purpose is to show a four-step management workflow. At the very top of the canvas, place a thin horizontal line that spans the entire width of the page. Slightly below this line and aligned to the left, add a compact rounded rectangular label containing the word “Project.” Directly beneath this label, still in the upper-left area, place a two-line main title reading “Management Workflow,” and directly below the title include a multi-line paragraph of placeholder text, all left-aligned as a single title block. In the upper-right quadrant of the page, horizontally level with the title block, create a grouped icon cluster inside an implicit container: in its left column place one larger landscape-oriented rectangular card, and in its right column place three smaller rectangular cards stacked vertically; connect the smaller right-column cards to the larger left card with short straight connector lines, and have the bottom edge or side of this cluster serve as the starting point for the main process path. From near the right side of this upper-right icon cluster, draw a thick continuous path that forms a large S-shaped curve descending through the central area of the page, first sweeping left across the upper-middle, then bending right in the midsection, then dropping down and sweeping left again across the lower portion. Place four circular markers on this path, spaced along the curve and labeled 1, 2, 3, and 4 in the order in which the viewer follows the path from its origin near the upper-right cluster toward the lower-left exit; marker 1 should sit on the upper-left segment of the path, marker 2 on the upper-right midsegment, marker 3 on the lower-right segment, and marker 4 on the lower-left segment. Over the middle and lower parts of this path, center a 2x2 grid of process description blocks. In the top row of the grid, put the Planning block in the left column directly above marker 1 and the Execution block in the right column directly above marker 2. In the bottom row, put the Review block in the left column directly above marker 4 and the Monitoring block in the right column directly above marker 3. Each of the four process blocks should be a vertically stacked mini-layout: at the top, an illustration box, immediately beneath it a step title (“Planning,” “Execution,” “Review,” or “Monitoring”), and under the title a multi-line paragraph of placeholder text, all left-aligned within the block. Ensure the S-shaped path visually passes behind these four blocks, running horizontally between the left and right blocks in each row near the circular markers so that each marker appears centered below its corresponding block. Add secondary background decoration only: include small dotted clusters as separate groups near the left-middle and right-middle edges of the page, and partial circular shapes tucked into the top-left and bottom-right corners, all positioned behind the main title, icon cluster, path, markers, and process blocks. Maintain an overall single-column composition where the upper-left title block and upper-right icon cluster form the top section, the S-shaped path with its four numbered markers forms a central vertical backbone, the 2x2 grid of process blocks occupies the central and lower body aligned over that backbone, and the numbered circular markers are centered underneath their respective process blocks along the path.

prompt: Create a vertically oriented family tree poster with a clear hierarchical layout composed of a top header banner, a centered subtitle, three horizontal rows of circular portrait nodes connected by lines and arrows, and a decorative tree and base shape in the background. At the very top, place a single wide rectangular banner spanning most of the width of the canvas, centered horizontally, with the main title text centered inside it; depict two short vertical hanging elements extending upward from the left and right ends of the banner to the top edge, suggesting the banner is suspended. Directly below this banner, center one line of subtitle text. Beneath the subtitle, construct the main diagram area containing three stacked horizontal rows of circular portrait frames, all positioned in front of a tree-like branching graphic that fills the central area of the page and extends into root-like shapes near the bottom. In the first diagram row under the subtitle, place exactly two circular portrait frames side-by-side in a single horizontal row, centered on the page so that they are at equal height; center a short name label under each circle. Between these two top circles, place a short double-headed horizontal arrow above a short vertical connector line that descends from the midpoint between the circles. This vertical connector continues downward and meets a branching connector associated with the second row. Directly below the first row, create a second diagram row consisting of exactly four circular portrait frames arranged in a single horizontal line, centered on the page, all with their centers at the same vertical position; each circle in this row has a name label centered directly underneath it. Above the four circles in this second row, draw a horizontal branching connector line that spans from leftmost to rightmost positions of the row, with four short vertical connectors dropping down from the branching line to the top of each of the four circles; the central portion of this branching line connects upward to the vertical connector descending from between the two top-row circles. Below the second row, create a third diagram row with exactly three circular portrait frames in a single horizontal line, centered on the page with all three circle centers aligned at the same vertical height; each of these three circles has a name label centered directly below it. The central circle in the third row is positioned directly beneath the second circle from the left in the second row; a vertical connector line links the bottom of that second-row circle to the top of the central third-row circle, emphasizing a straight vertical alignment. From the central third-row circle, extend two short horizontal connector lines, one to the left circle and one to the right circle in the same row, indicating siblings. Behind all three rows of circles, include a single stylized tree shape whose upper branching area sits behind the first and second rows and whose trunk and roots occupy the area behind the third row and extend into the lower portion of the page. Along the bottom edge of the canvas, add irregular overlapping shapes forming a base that partially covers the lower roots, creating a distinct bottom boundary for the composition while keeping all portrait circles and labels entirely above this base.

prompt: Create a vertically oriented poster-style page built as a single central column of stacked sections: at the very top, place a full-width header banner containing two centered lines of title text, with the smaller phrase “FUN FACT ABOUT” on the first line and the larger word “GIRAFFE” on the second line, both centered horizontally; overlap small decorative leaf elements near the top left and top right corners of the background around this header. Below this header, build five informational content rows, each spanning the main width and separated from the next by a full-width horizontal divider line. The first informational row directly under the header is a two-column horizontal band: in the left column place a rounded-rectangle image of a giraffe centered within the column, and in the right column place a text block with the label “KEY FACTS” as a heading on a single line above a multi-line left-aligned paragraph describing facts about giraffes. The second informational row beneath it is an asymmetric two-column band: in the central text area place the heading “FOOD” centered above a center-aligned multi-line paragraph about diet, and in a narrower right column place a rounded-rectangle close-up nature image centered within that column; leave the left side of this row without an image so the main text appears centered between the left margin and the right image. The fourth informational row beneath it mirrors the text-centered pattern: use a central text block where the heading “ECOSYSTEM ROLE” is centered above a center-aligned paragraph describing ecological function, and place a rounded-rectangle landscape image of a giraffe in a right-side column aligned with the text; again ensure this row has no image on the left so the text is roughly central with the image on the right. The fifth informational row directly above the footer returns to a two-column layout similar to the first and third rows: in the left column place a rounded-rectangle savanna landscape image, and in the right column place a left-aligned text block with heading “CONSERVATION” above a multi-line paragraph, all aligned so the image sits to the left of the text; a horizontal divider line runs below this row, separating it from the footer. Along the vertical mid-height edges of the page, place small decorative “X” shaped icons stacked near the middle on both the left and right sides as background ornamentation. At the very bottom, spanning the full width, add a footer banner containing a single centered line of text that reads “HAPPY WORLD ANIMALS DAY,” with a left-pointing arrow icon near the left edge of the banner and a mirrored right-pointing arrow icon near the right edge on the same baseline as the text; overlap additional decorative leaves near the lower left and lower right corners of the background outside the footer banner so that the main composition reads as a vertically stacked sequence of header, five horizontally aligned informational rows with alternating image placements, and a bottom call-to-action footer.

prompt: Create a single-page radial infographic layout with one large circular hub centered on the page and ten distinct vertical rectangular panels arranged around it in a loose ring. The central circle serves as the main title area and is divided into an upper title block and a lower text block; the upper portion contains a bilingual headline describing “Step 0: The Golden Rules” and the lower portion contains four numbered or bulleted guideline items stacked vertically. From the edge of this circle, draw ten curved connector lines radiating outward in different directions; each curved line terminates at one of the numbered surrounding panels and has a small character or mascot illustration placed near its outer end, visually grouping that character with its panel. Across the top of the page, place four numbered panels in a single horizontal row above the central circle: panel 1 at the top-left, panel 2 to its right, panel 3 to the right of panel 2, and panel 4 at the top-right. Each of these top panels is internally structured as a vertical stack with a heading row at the top, a content area in the middle, and a short caption area at the bottom. In panel 1, the middle content area contains a single row of two document-like thumbnails placed side by side above a stylized word element, all beneath the centered heading and above the caption. In panel 2, the middle area contains a grid of small character illustrations arranged as two rows and three columns, and beneath this grid is a row of two media-related icons above a line of explanatory text. In panel 3, the middle content consists of two stacked rows of example images, each row holding two side-by-side panels, forming a two-by-two grid under the heading. In panel 4, the middle portion has two example image rows one above the other, each row holding a pair of related images, all above a small caption block. On the right side of the page, place panel 5 at the right-center, horizontally aligned with the central circle; inside it, stack three vertical zones with a heading at the top, a central grid, and a caption at the bottom. The central grid has two rows and two columns: the upper row shows a floor-plan-like image on the left and a room-like image on the right, and the lower row shows another pair of corresponding images, with directional arrows indicating conversion between the upper and lower rows. Directly below panel 5, at the lower-right of the page, place panel 6, also divided into heading, middle, and caption zones; its middle section contains two example images stacked vertically in a single column. Slightly left of panel 6 and below the central circle, place panel 7; it has a heading row with a small brain-like icon, a middle region where a mathematical formula block occupies the middle-left area and a small interior-scene illustration sits toward the lower-right, and a caption line at the bottom. Directly left of panel 7 and roughly centered below the circle, place panel 8; it has a heading at the top, a large storyboard grid in the middle containing multiple small rectangular frames arranged in several rows and columns, and a caption at the bottom. To the left of panel 8 at the lower-left of the page, place panel 9, structured with a heading, a central area showing stacked wireframe layout diagrams and a smaller grid example, and a caption beneath them. On the left-center side of the page, to the left of the central circle and above panel 9, place panel 10; it has a heading at the top, a middle area with several square app-like icons arranged in a small block above short explanatory text, and at the bottom a single elongated rectangular control element with arrow indicators. Ensure all headings appear at the top of their respective panels, all captions appear at the bottom within each panel, and all ten panels are connected to the central circular hub by their own curved radial lines to recreate the overall mind-map structure.

prompt: Create a single-page vertical poster layout that is organized into three main horizontal bands stacked vertically: a top header, a central grid of cards, and a bottom footer, all centered within the page. In the top band, place a large irregularly shaped container centered horizontally; inside this container, position a small single-line brand or site name near the top center and a larger multi-line main title directly below it, both aligned to the vertical center of the page. Beneath this header container, in the middle band, build a 3 by 3 grid of nine uniformly sized cards that is centered horizontally: three cards in the top row, three directly below them in the second row, and three directly below those in the third row, forming three clearly aligned vertical columns. Each card is an individual irregular container; inside each card, place a small horizontal title bar centered at the top of the card and, directly below this bar, a multi-line descriptive text block, with both elements confined within the same card. Ensure that the rows of cards are evenly spaced vertically, that the columns stay perfectly aligned from the top row to the bottom row, and that the entire grid sits between the header and the footer without touching them. In the bottom band of the page, below the third row of cards, add a footer container that is smaller than the header container and centered horizontally near the bottom edge; place a single horizontal line of handle or contact text in the center of this footer container. Around these main sections, add decorative background elements that do not interfere with the main content: along the top left edge, include flowing abstract shapes with a small cluster of star-like icons located just below and to the left of the header container; near the top right corner, add a stack of concentric curved-line shapes; near the bottom left corner, place another stack of concentric curved-line shapes below the left column of the grid; and near the bottom right portion of the page, to the right of the footer container, include a small cluster of star-like icons adjacent to additional flowing background lines. Keep all main text and card structures aligned along a central vertical axis, with no sidebars, so the visual hierarchy runs from the header at the top, to the 3 by 3 card grid in the center, to the footer at the bottom.

prompt: Create a vertical anti-drug awareness poster with a solid background and a clear layout combining bold text on the left with an illustrative scene on the right and contact information along the bottom. At the very top-center of the poster, place a large three-line slogan stacked with all-uppercase text, with each line centered: the first line should read "CHOOSE TO BE FREE", the second line directly below it should read "NOT A PRISONER", and the third line directly below that should read "TO DRUGS". Below this main heading, aligned to the left side of the poster and occupying the upper-left and middle-left area, add two separate paragraphs of smaller body text. The first paragraph sits directly under the slogan and to the left of the illustration; it is composed of five lines, each left-aligned, reading line by line: "Drugs don't just damage", "your health; they steal", "your potential, disrupt", "your relationships, and", and "can derail your future.". Immediately below this, still on the left side and still to the left of the illustration, place the second paragraph, also five lines of left-aligned text that reads line by line: "Together, we can create a", "community that values", "health, happiness, and the", "promise of a brighter", and "tomorrow". On the right side of the poster, occupying the middle and lower-right areas, place an illustration of a large upright rectangular container or jar with a removable lid that is being opened from above by a person standing on a ladder, while another person is shown inside the container; ensure there is no additional text inside this illustration. Near the lower-left area of the poster, underneath the two paragraphs and still to the left of the illustration, add a rounded rectangular outline containing a block of call-to-action text; inside this outlined box, center or left-align eight short lines of text stacked vertically, reading exactly: "If you or someone", "you know is", "struggling with drug", "abuse, reach out for", "help. Your decision", "today can change", "your life and the lives", and "of those around you.". At the very bottom of the poster, spanning horizontally across the bottom-center, add a rounded rectangular strip that contains three separate segments of single-line text laid out in a single row from left to right; in the left segment place the website "reallygreatsite.com", in the center segment place the email address "hello@reallygreatsite.com", and in the right segment place the phone number "+123-456-7890". Ensure all text appears exactly as specified, with the same wording, punctuation, line breaks, and ordering, and keep the relative positions so that the slogan is at the top-center, the explanatory paragraphs and callout box are on the left, the illustration is on the right, and the contact information strip is at the bottom.

prompt: Create a vertically oriented educational poster layout about giraffes with multiple photo-and-text sections. In section 7, fourth row left, place a circular off-white plate centered in the cell, divided subtly into food sections, and label them with neat white chalk numbers such as “30 g”, “50 g”, and “20 g”; at the plate’s lower edge draw a small yellow rabbit and a white round mascot gazing at the food, and add a small rainbow-colored bar chart icon near the bottom right of the cell; surround this with English text about precise calorie counting and tracking macronutrients. In section 8, fourth row right, depict a black rectangular booklet or folder slightly opened, with the word “CONTRACT” written vertically or diagonally on the cover in condensed yellow sans-serif capital letters; place a blue pen next to it, and draw the white mascot character looking worried nearby; add at least two small yellow triangular warning signs with exclamation marks around the booklet, and write English annotations warning about hidden fees and possible twenty percent extra costs. In section 9, fifth row left, draw a dark folded restaurant menu booklet labeled “MENU” in yellow capital letters on its front cover; next to it, show the white mascot face and a short three-line vertical list of recommended dishes numbered 1, 2, and 3, with tiny red chili icons beside some lines; include English phrases about ordering local specialties when there is no picture menu. In section 10, fifth row right, compose a scene with a brown acoustic guitar with an hourglass body and round sound hole on the left, a simplified girl in a pink dress with medium-length hair standing in the middle, and the yellow rabbit mascot on the right wearing or holding a ribbon-like accessory; scatter a few small pink cube-shaped gift icons with ribbon crosses around them, and add English text describing creative wrapping plans and tying ribbon decorations. Finally, along the very bottom of the board beneath sections 9 and 10, draw an additional horizontal tip band: on the left, place a white round mascot holding a blue magnifying glass next to a bright yellow light bulb icon with a small rectangular base and short radial lines, and label this area with a phrase such as “Core method (Tip)” where the word Tip is written in a slightly bolder serif-like chalk font inside parentheses; on the right of the bottom band, draw another white mascot holding a grey camera positioned beside a sentence in white chalk English advising not to rely only on search engines but to consult on-site experts, followed by a short remark praising how strong visual AI recommendations are; ensure all text across the board is in English, translated from the original Chinese, and keeps a consistent chalk handwritten appearance.

prompt: Create a vertically oriented educational infographic in a soft 3D clay or plasticine style, showing information about the continent of Asia, with all text written in simplified Chinese characters but conceptually translating to ideas like “Chapter 6: The Continent We Live On — Asia.” The entire image sits on a smooth light warm beige gradient background that becomes slightly darker toward the edges and has no border, while all objects appear as raised, matte, fully opaque pieces with soft shadows. At the very top, place a wide pale yellow folded ribbon banner with rounded ends and gentle orange shading at the folds; on its central band, render the chapter title in dark brown bold rounded sans-serif Chinese characters. Directly underneath, add a narrower lavender-purple horizontal ribbon with curved ends carrying dark brown rounded sans-serif Chinese text meaning “Section 1: Location and extent.” Below this, slightly left of center, add a bright yellow pill-shaped capsule label with soft inner shading and a raised look; inside, write a dark blue bold rounded sans-serif Chinese heading meaning “The majestic eastern continent,” and underneath in smaller black sans-serif characters place a parenthetical subtitle meaning “(Geographical location).” To the right of this heading, place a light green speech-bubble label with a slightly darker green border and a small triangular tail pointing left; the bubble is a rounded rectangle and contains dark green sans-serif Chinese text meaning “Located in the eastern part of the Eurasian continent.” Under the heading area on the left, place two identical compasses arranged horizontally: perfect circles with orange outer rims, white faces, small evenly spaced tick marks, and central pivot dots; each has a red-and-blue needle and red Chinese characters marking the directions, one labeled for the Eastern Hemisphere and one for the Northern Hemisphere. Beneath them, add a single caption line in black rounded sans-serif Chinese that translates to “Mainly located in the Eastern Hemisphere and the Northern Hemisphere.” Just below this caption, align three glossy thermometers in a row: each is a vertical rounded rectangle with a circular bulb at the bottom and a narrow tube above, all with a glass-like shine. Color the left thermometer blue, the middle one green, and the right one red; above them place small icons respectively of a light blue snowflake, a mild yellow sun partly obscured by a white cloud, and a stronger orange-red sun, indicating cold, temperate, and hot. Under the thermometers, write three colored Chinese characters—blue, green, and red—meaning “cold,” “temperate,” and “hot,” followed by black Chinese characters meaning “three zones,” all in the same rounded sans-serif font. In the upper-middle area, slightly left of center, position a large smooth blue globe with faint darker blue longitude and latitude grid lines and raised yellow landmasses forming a simplified Eurasia, giving the whole sphere a toy-like look; the globe casts a circular shadow onto the beige background. Immediately to the right of the globe, place a rectangular map panel with a pale cyan base and rounded corners, also casting a shadow; on this panel, add a large contiguous yellow continent shape representing Eurasia and a distinct smaller white island-continent to the southeast reminiscent of Australia, plus a few tiny cyan land fragments. Label the surrounding seas with small black sans-serif Chinese text reading “Arctic Ocean” at the top, “Pacific Ocean” to the right, and “Indian Ocean” below. Flanking the globe and map together, draw two thick red curved arrows with smooth arcs and triangular arrowheads, one on the left and one on the right, both pointing upward; centered just below them, in small black sans-serif Chinese, write a caption translating to “Greatest latitudinal span (about 81°N–11°S).” Across the full width of the page beneath the location section, insert a horizontal separator made of evenly spaced small brown dots rather than a solid line; place a small yellow pill-shaped label on top of the center of this dotted line with dark brown bold rounded sans-serif Chinese characters meaning “Boundary lines.” Immediately under this, at the left, add three legend markers: three small slightly rounded squares colored orange, red, and green from left to right, each followed by black sans-serif Chinese text explaining that they represent, respectively, the northwest boundary between Europe and Asia, the western boundary with Africa, and the eastern boundary with North America. Below the legend, align three beige panels in a row, all equal-size rounded rectangles with soft shadows and matching corner radii. On the left panel, depict a simplified map of the Europe–Asia border using light orange land shapes, blue water, and small gray-brown triangular mountains, with black sans-serif Chinese labels naming a mountain range, a sea, and a strait. On the middle panel, show a pale blue canal cutting between beige landmasses of Asia and Africa, with a small red-and-blue ship sailing through, and black Chinese text labeling the canal. On the right panel, show a narrow blue strait separating a beige Asian landmass from another beige landmass to the east, with a black label naming the strait between Asia and North America. Further down, add another horizontal separator line that visually matches the first: small evenly spaced brown dots spanning almost edge to edge, overlapped at the center by a yellow capsule label with dark brown bold rounded sans-serif Chinese characters translating to “The world’s largest continent.” In the lower-left quadrant, construct an area-chart style composition: a thick blue L-shaped axis with arrowheads at the top of the vertical segment and at the right end of the horizontal segment, forming a right angle; next to and partly above the horizontal axis, place one irregular raised green landmass blob representing Asia’s area. Near the origin of the axes, set a small beige square icon resembling a weighing scale platform with an orange top surface. Beneath this group, in black sans-serif Chinese, write text translating to “Largest area (about 44 million km²),” writing “km²” with simple sans-serif Latin characters. To the right of this, position an orange circular medallion with a scalloped edge and a slight gradient, plus a small red ribbon tail hanging from the bottom; in the center of the medallion print “NO.1” in bold white sans-serif capital letters. Slightly overlapping its lower right side, add a tilted beige rounded rectangle note card outlined with a red border, containing red sans-serif Chinese text meaning “Exam hint,” while nearby black Chinese text lists in bullet-like lines that Asia has the greatest latitudinal span, the longest east–west distance, and the largest area. In the lower-right corner area, create a circular pie chart with a clean edge, divided into exactly six congruent wedge sectors separated by thin radial boundaries; color the wedges in distinct solid hues such as green, red, orange, purple, blue, and yellow. Within each wedge, place a small raised 3D icon—snow mountains for the northern region, a dome building, a pagoda, a palm tree, and other region-specific landmarks—each modeled in matching pastel colors. Below this chart, write black rounded sans-serif Chinese characters meaning “Divisions (6)” and use a Latin digit “6” inside the parentheses. Finally, at the very bottom right, overlay a faint, semi-transparent white rounded rectangular watermark banner partially touching the pie chart, containing a small red circular generic app-style logo and gray sans-serif ID text, representing a social-media style source mark.

prompt: Create a clean, brightly lit educational illustration of a wooden classroom table seen in three-quarter view, with the tabletop occupying most of the frame. The tabletop should be warm light brown with slightly darker brown wood-grain lines and seams between planks running roughly from left to right. In the background above the table, show a simple pale cream wall and a softly blurred beige-framed notice board in the upper-right corner. On the upper-left portion of the table place a small open rectangular cardboard crayon box made of light tan card, drawn in flat cartoon style with thin black outlines; the lid is folded back so the open side faces the viewer. The short front panel of the box has two solid green diagonal stripes near its top corners and, centered in the middle, a horizontal oval cutout window revealing four flat color swatches arranged from left to right in the order red, orange, yellow, and blue. Inside the box, depict exactly four visible crayons lying horizontally with their conical tips pointing toward the left; from bottom to top these are a bright red crayon, a vivid orange crayon, a strong yellow crayon, and a medium blue crayon, each only partially protruding from the box. Elsewhere on the tabletop, draw multiple loose crayons whose tips generally point toward the center of the composition. In the central area place one bright yellow crayon lying diagonally from lower left to upper right; directly below and parallel to it place a medium light green crayon; just below the green crayon, again parallel, place a medium blue crayon. On the right half of the table arrange three cool-colored crayons—a dark indigo blue, a violet purple, and a pinkish purple—fanning out with their tips aimed toward the middle. Near the right edge, add a separate light pink crayon oriented diagonally with its tip pointing toward the center of the table. In the lower-right quadrant, draw a rich brown crayon and a pure white crayon next to each other, both angled so their tips point toward the center, with the white crayon positioned between the brown crayon on its right and a solid deep black crayon on its left. At the bottom left, depict two gray crayons pointing roughly upward: a light gray crayon on the far left and a darker gray crayon immediately to its right. Include an additional bright red crayon lying outside the box on the tabletop. All crayons should share the same design: a solid colored wax body wrapped by a slightly lighter matching paper label that features a single long darker oval in the middle and two thin darker bands encircling the wrapper near each end, all outlined with thin black lines and filled with flat, non-textured color. Around the tips of the central group of crayons, scatter several small tan paper shavings and tiny colored fragments to suggest recently sharpened crayons. For every distinct color, add a neat label floating above or beside its crayon: use white rounded rectangles with thin solid borders colored to match the crayon they name (red border for red, orange for orange, yellow for yellow, green for green, blue for blue, dark indigo for indigo, pink for pink, brown for brown, light gray for the light gray crayon, darker gray for the darker gray crayon, black for the black crayon, and a neutral gray border for the white crayon label). From the bottom center of each label, draw a thin solid black leader line that connects directly to the associated crayon. Inside every label place three left-aligned lines of black sans-serif text: on the first line, the English color name in bold lowercase letters (for example, "red", "orange", "yellow", "green", "blue", "indigo", "pink", "brown", "gray", "white", "black"); on the second line, the phonetic transcription in slashes such as "/red/" or "/bluː/" in regular weight; and on the third line, the corresponding simplified Chinese color word in a slightly smaller regular-weight style. Provide two separate labels reading "gray /greɪ/" with identical Chinese text, each pointing to a different gray crayon, and allow the color red to have a label for the crayon in the box and another label for the loose red crayon. Keep the entire image in smooth cartoon vector style with clean black outlines, flat colors, and no shading gradients, suitable for a children’s educational poster teaching color vocabulary.

prompt: Create a vertically oriented digital poster illustrating sleep tips with a flat vector-cartoon style, using a deep navy blue night-sky background that transitions via a smooth vertical gradient to a slightly lighter navy at the bottom and is sprinkled with numerous tiny white and light-blue specks as distant stars. Frame the composition with three large soft cloud clusters in cream, light peach, and pale blue: one curling in from the upper left, one from the upper right, and one large one along the bottom left, each with rounded organic lobes and no outlines. Scatter many small pale yellow five-pointed stars and subtle glowing star shapes around the clouds and text. Center near the top the title text in bright white uppercase letters reading on two lines: top line “3 TIPS FOR” in a smaller rounded sans-serif font, and below it “QUALITY SLEEP” in a much larger slightly italic bold rounded sans-serif font, both fully opaque and placed directly on the sky. On the upper right, place the first tip text directly on the background without a panel: a heading in white uppercase italic sans-serif reading “CREATE A COMFORTABLE ROOM ATMOSPHERE” above a left-aligned multi-line paragraph in smaller regular-weight sans-serif sentence case, also in white. In the middle-left of the poster, add a dark navy rounded-rectangle panel slightly lighter than the background with soft corners and no stroke, semi-transparent so the starry sky subtly shows through; inside this panel center a heading in uppercase italic white sans-serif reading “REDUCE GADGET USAGE BEFORE BED,” with a left-aligned smaller white sans-serif paragraph beneath it. In the lower-right-middle area, add another similar dark navy pill-shaped rounded rectangle panel with no border; inside it, left-align an uppercase italic white heading “SET A REGULAR BEDTIME,” followed by a smaller regular sans-serif white paragraph, also left-aligned. Depict four human figures, each resting on its own light-blue cloud rendered in a soft gradient with subtle glow and no outlines: in the upper-left quadrant, below the title, draw a large cyan-blue horizontally elongated cloud with a small trailing cloud piece and place a relaxed person with medium-brown skin, short dark hair, bright yellow short-sleeved top, and light salmon-pink pants lying on their side on the cloud, knees slightly bent and arms hugging a white pillow, eyes closed. To the right of the middle tip panel, draw a smaller light cyan-blue cloud and place on it a child-like figure with medium skin tone wearing a pale yellow outfit, reclining with bent knees while holding and reading an open light-blue book, head tilted toward the pages. Below the middle panel toward the lower left, add a larger light-blue cloud and seat on it a person with medium skin tone wearing a black long-sleeved top, blue pants, and brown shoes, sitting upright with legs extended and slightly bent while holding a vertical brown walking cane planted on the cloud with one hand. In the lower-right corner, draw a very large multilayered cloud composed of overlapping lobes in light blue and pale cream, occupying much of the bottom-right area, and on it place a large figure with medium-brown skin wearing a white long-sleeved sleep outfit, lying sideways in a gentle curve with arms folded under the head as if using them as a pillow, eyes closed peacefully. Ensure there are exactly four human figures and three distinct tip text sections, all text in English, all paragraphs left-aligned, and all shapes and text rendered with solid edges and fills without any dashed or dotted lines.

prompt: Create a flat vector-style infographic on a solid pale turquoise background, in portrait orientation, titled at the top with a cream horizontal strip bearing the text “THE LONELIEST SCHOOLBOY IN BRITAIN?” in bold all-caps black sans-serif, directly above a narrower red tartan-patterned ribbon that stretches almost the full width and contains the subtitle “Aron’s Island Life in Out Skerries” in white mixed-case casual sans-serif; at the top left, include a red-and-white horizontally striped lighthouse with a dark gray roof standing on a small irregular green grassy rock surrounded by light blue water with a tiny brown boat nearby, all in simple outlined cartoon style; beneath this, on the left side, place a pale green circular map with small irregular dark green island blobs and the label “Out Skerries” plus dark gray body text about remote islands, population, access, and facilities, all inside or around the circle in a neat rounded sans-serif font, using slightly bolder uppercase for headings like “REMOTE ISLANDS:” and “POPULATIONS:”; to the immediate left of the center, add a larger pale green irregular rounded blob outlined in darker green containing more island information and numeric phrases such as “~280 km from Scotland coast” and “~70 people,” with the text in dark gray; in the visual center, draw a boy in simple cartoon style wearing a bright blue hoodie, dark teal pants, and dark shoes, standing upright beside a light green bicycle whose two large wheels are perfect circles with dark gray tires and spokes, all positioned on a light green irregular ground patch bordered by a continuous medium-thickness purple line that curves smoothly; directly underneath this figure, place a white rounded-rectangle identity box framed by the same purple line, containing three main lines of dark gray text in a hand-drawn-style sans-serif stating his name, that he is the only student since September, and that the location is Out Skerries, Scotland, with “ONLY STUDENT” in uppercase for emphasis; at the lower left, add a vertical stack of pale off-white rounded rectangles outlined in purple labeled “UNTIL JULY:” and “SINCE SEPTEMBER:” in uppercase bold dark gray, connected by curved solid red arrows with pointed arrowheads, and beneath them include a pale off-white box describing the main reason people moved away next to a small factory icon showing a pinkish rectangular building with a gray roof and a tall gray chimney emitting a single puff of white smoke rising into the turquoise sky; within this left-lower area, insert a small circular vignette containing the boy cycling on a green hill against a flat pale blue sky, again in the same simple outlined style; occupy the right half of the image with a large pale blue irregular rounded panel bordered by a solid medium-blue line, whose top part features the heading “SMALL SCHOOL:” in bold uppercase dark gray text and body text below describing “2 classrooms, art, music, library,” plus a small school house illustration composed of an orange-brown symmetrical building with a dark gray roof, two front windows flanking a central door, and a tiny blue ornament or flag on top, sitting above a thin gray baseline; underneath, still within the same blue panel, include another heading “TEACHER:” with text noting one teacher for all subjects, and, farther down, a ground-level playground vignette showing the boy wearing a backpack walking toward a simple yellow swing set made from thin yellow lines forming a rectangular frame on a green grassy strip, accompanied by a white speech bubble with a thick black outline and casual dark gray text explaining that all the playground is his and no one plays with him; near the bottom-right of the canvas, overlapping slightly with the central section, create a large pale orange irregular rounded area outlined with a darker orange line for the cost information, whose top line reads “COST: £75,357 per year” in large bold dark gray sans-serif, and inside this panel place an oversized smooth yellow pound currency symbol, a smaller dark gray rounded-rectangle calculator icon with a grid of tiny square buttons, and a bold red “X” mark formed from two thick diagonal bars crossing over a simplified side-view head silhouette to indicate a canceled comparison, along with smaller dark gray text mentioning “COMPARISON: 2 x Eton!” and the mother’s worries; decorate the upper-right border with a long vertical brown rope- or ribbon-like strip that twists to outline a green number “2” near the top and supports a couple of small white birds drawn in the same flat style; throughout the infographic, ensure all body text is dark gray to black on pale backgrounds (white, pale green, pale blue, pale orange), headings use slightly heavier weight or uppercase, speech and thought bubbles are white with black outlines and rounded shapes containing casual dark gray lettering, and thin solid red arrows in consistent width curve between relevant boxes to indicate narrative flow, all rendered with clean outlines, no gradients, and a cohesive flat vector aesthetic.

prompt: Create a vertical, portrait-oriented flat infographic poster on a solid light peach background with no gradient, featuring along the very bottom a slightly darker peach silhouette of a simplified city skyline made from overlapping rectangular and rounded-rectangular building shapes, plus a few small pale-peach oval clouds and tiny circular dots as decoration. Near the top-left to mid-top, center the main title in two lines: the first line reads “Soft Skills Needed” in vivid orange-red, using a bold uppercase serif or slab-serif typeface, and the second larger line beneath reads “In The Future” in dark teal, using a bold uppercase sans-serif typeface; both lines are tightly kerned, heavy in weight, and centered on their own baselines. Behind the title’s right side and extending up under the character, place one large soft-edged circular blob of slightly darker peach. In the upper-right quadrant, draw a single flat-style male character with a light skin tone and brown hair, wearing a long-sleeve white shirt, a dark navy tie, dark charcoal trousers, and black shoes; he stands in three-quarter view on a dark teal horizontal oval platform that has a smaller light gray circular base overlapping its right edge, as if he is on a raised disc. The character holds in his right hand a navy and purple telescope with a small golden yellow ring near the viewing end, raised to his eye, and beside him on his left stands one thin light beige vertical flagpole topped with a bright red, slightly wavy triangular flag that has a curved outer edge. Down the left half of the poster, aligned in a clean vertical column, place five skill sections, each consisting of a dark teal icon on the far left, a vivid orange-red heading to the right of the icon, and dark teal descriptive body text beneath the heading; all section text is left-aligned, with a straight left edge and ragged right edge, and no text crosses into the right margin where the character stands. For the first section, “Communication Skills,” use a bold uppercase serif heading in vivid orange-red on one line, placed to the right of a dark teal line icon composed of exactly two overlapping rounded rectangular speech bubble outlines with pointed tails; below it, add two lines of regular-weight uppercase sans-serif body text in dark teal reading, “It is important to communicate intelligently, practice begins now.” For the second section, “Adaptability,” give a similar bold uppercase serif heading in vivid orange-red on one line, to the right of a dark teal solid icon showing a simplified rounded human bust surrounded by two curved arrow shapes forming a circular motion, one arrow pointing clockwise and the other counterclockwise; beneath, place two lines of regular uppercase sans-serif body text in dark teal reading, “Sociable and friendly is the key for adapting in a new environment.” For the third section, “Problem Solving Skills,” again use a single-line bold uppercase serif heading in vivid orange-red to the right of a composite dark teal icon where a stylized human head profile made of interlocking puzzle-piece shapes transitions into a lightbulb base, surrounded by small gear-like and spark shapes; below, set two lines of regular uppercase sans-serif body text in dark teal reading, “Must be able to think critically, observe issues and find a resolution.” For the fourth section, “Cooperation Skills,” place a one-line bold uppercase serif heading in vivid orange-red beside a dark teal solid icon made from exactly three simplified hands and forearms arranged in a closed loop, each hand gripping the wrist of the next; under this heading, add two lines of regular uppercase sans-serif body text in dark teal stating, “Must be smart to respect and share opinions with others.” For the fifth and final section, “Time Management,” use the same bold uppercase serif style for the vivid orange-red heading on a single line next to a dark teal line icon of a symmetrical hourglass with a rectangular top and base and a narrow waist at the center; below it, include two lines of regular uppercase sans-serif body text in dark teal reading, “Good at managing time and setting priorities.” Maintain consistent vertical spacing between all five sections, consistent medium stroke thickness for all icons, and ensure the overall style is clean flat vector art with solid fills, no gradients, and no outlines around the poster edges.

prompt: Create a tall portrait-oriented educational infographic on a white background showing the distribution of global climate types in a cute flat cartoon style, with thin dark brown outlines and solid pastel fills. In the center, draw a large vertical ellipse representing the world, outlined with a thin soft purple stroke and taking up about two-thirds of the canvas height and half the width. Fill the ellipse interior with exactly nine equal-height horizontal pastel bands stacked from top to bottom in this precise order: pale lavender, very light sky blue, pale yellow, light peach, pale spring green, light peach again, pale yellow again, very light sky blue again, and pale lavender at the bottom; the band edges are crisp, straight, and have no border lines. Across the ellipse, add light gray dashed arcs following the curvature to mark major latitudes at heights corresponding to 60°, 40°, 20°, and 0° (the equator). Outside the ellipse on both left and right, place small black degree labels in a clean sans-serif font reading 0°, 20°, 30°, 40°, 50°, and 60°, vertically aligned with the corresponding latitudes so the scale is mirrored on both sides. At the very top of the page, above the ellipse, place a single-line English title that reads “World Major Climate Types Distribution Rules” in very large bold rounded sans-serif letters; fill the letters with a vertical color gradient from teal at the top to medium blue at the bottom and outline them with a darker blue stroke. Inside the ellipse at the top center, write the word “Polar” in dark gray regular-weight sans-serif and immediately below it draw a simple north indicator: a straight black vertical line with a triangular arrowhead pointing upward and a small black capital letter “N” just below the arrowhead; put another identical “Polar” label centered near the bottom of the ellipse. Represent each climate type with a white rounded rectangular card that slightly overlaps the colored bands; each card has smooth pill-shaped corners, a subtle pale gray drop shadow, and on its left edge a golden-orange circular badge that slightly protrudes beyond the rectangle, containing a bold white sans-serif number. Number the cards consecutively from 1 to 13, with no gaps. Inside each card, on the right of the badge, place dark gray English text in a small regular-weight sans-serif font: a short title such as “Tropical Rainforest Climate”, “Tropical Savanna Climate”, “Tropical Monsoon Climate”, “Tropical Desert Climate”, “Subtropical Monsoon Climate”, “Mediterranean Climate”, “Temperate Continental Climate”, “Temperate Monsoon Climate”, “Temperate Oceanic Climate”, “Subarctic Coniferous Forest Climate”, “Tundra Climate”, “Polar Continental Climate”, and “Highland Climate”, followed by a brief one-line description of representative regions. Above or beside the text on each card, add a colorful cartoon illustration of the climate. Along the central pale spring green equatorial band, place three cards spaced from left to right: one showing a lion sitting on yellow grass under a small green tree, one in the center showing a large green leafy rainforest tree with a smiling child standing beside it, and one on the right showing a gray elephant under a small gray rain cloud next to a curved brown palm tree with green fronds; all animals and the child have cute simple faces with dot eyes. On the light peach band just poleward of these, put a card on the left showing a warm beige desert with a tall green cactus and a tan camel, and in the mid-lower center band place a highland climate card showing cool gray mountains with white snow peaks and two brown yaks standing on green grass at the base. On the pale yellow midlatitude bands, position three cards: to the left, a Mediterranean climate card with a bunch of purple grapes and green grape leaves; in the center, a temperate continental climate card with a red barn, a fenced field, and rows of golden wheat; and to the right, a temperate monsoon card showing a stylized tree whose left half is green with flowers and whose right half is bare with orange leaves and snow, plus a small person beside it indicating changing seasons. On a light sky blue band between 40° and 60° in the northern half, place a temperate oceanic climate card featuring a person in a green raincoat holding a green umbrella under steady vertical blue raindrops. Near this cool band also include a subarctic coniferous forest card with a brown bear and dark green evergreen trees partly covered in snow. On the pale lavender cold bands, add a tundra climate card near the upper middle of the ellipse depicting an igloo on white snow, a brown reindeer, dark blue night sky, and a wavy bright green aurora band above them, and at the bottom center place a polar continental climate card showing two penguins and a gray seal standing on white ice floes floating on blue sea. Maintain the same white card, orange badge, and gray text style for every climate card. Around the outside of the ellipse, scatter several decorative icons: at least three large puffy white clouds with light blue shading and dark brown outlines, some of which have simple black dot eyes, pink cheeks, and a small smiling mouth; a few small five-pointed yellow stars and tiny pastel pink clouds; and a small yellow sun on the left. Near the upper right of the page, draw a single circular compass rose in soft pinks and beiges with a thin outline, a four-pointed star inside, and the letters N, E, S, and W in dark brown serif-like font. Near the lower left and lower right corners outside the ellipse, add exactly two small purple airplanes with white windows and dark outlines, each following a smooth curved flight path made of evenly spaced pale purple dots that arc around the ellipse. Ensure all elements use consistent thin outline strokes and flat pastel colors to match a cohesive cute educational infographic style.

prompt: Create a square infographic on a solid light cream background with no gradient, organized into three vertical regions: a tall light khaki-beige panel on the left for global origins, an open central column for a flavor analyzer, and an equally tall light khaki-beige panel on the right for key characteristics, with all major headings in dark coffee-brown uppercase sans-serif font. At the very top center, place the main title text “SPECIALTY COFFEE: ORIGIN & FLAVOR MAP” in bold dark coffee-brown uppercase sans-serif, with a smaller regular-weight sans-serif subtitle “A Guide to Global Coffee Characteristics” centered directly underneath. In the Latin America subsection, position a medium muted green silhouette map of Latin America on the left and a small dark brown coffee-cup icon near the text; in the Africa subsection, place a solid very dark greenish-brown silhouette of the African continent on the left; in the Asia-Pacific subsection, use a medium muted green silhouette of the Asia-Pacific region with a small dark brown jar or canister icon. Around the donuts, draw multiple thin pale beige solid connector lines that curve outward from several segments to small labels; terminate each connector with a simple triangular arrowhead. On the right side, construct the “KEY CHARACTERISTICS” panel: at its top, a slightly darker khaki header strip spanning the panel width with a small simple monochrome icon and the text “KEY CHARACTERISTICS” in dark coffee-brown uppercase sans-serif. Under this header, stack four khaki subsections. The first subsection is “AROMA”: place a small dark outline icon of a nose with two or three curved steam lines on the left and the heading “AROMA” in dark coffee-brown uppercase sans-serif to the right, followed by two short lines of dark gray regular-weight sans-serif text describing the smell. The second subsection is “ACIDITY”: again use a dark coffee-brown uppercase heading with a short dark gray explanation and, beneath this text, draw a horizontal indicator bar composed of three equal-sized touching rectangles with no outlines, colored green on the left, orange in the center, and red on the right, labeled in tiny sans-serif text as “Low,” “Medium,” and “High” respectively. The third subsection is “BODY” describing brightness and tanginess: position a small solid green droplet icon on the left and the heading “BODY” in dark coffee-brown uppercase sans-serif with dark gray explanatory text about brightness and tanginess on the right. The fourth subsection is another “BODY” describing weight and texture: write the heading “BODY” again in the same style, a short dark gray explanation about weight and texture beside it, and include three identical small outline cup or glass icons in a row, drawn with thin lines and not filled, to suggest different body levels. Near the lower third of the image, centered across all columns, add the title “THE JOURNEY OF COFFEE” in dark coffee-brown uppercase sans-serif medium bold, and draw a thin solid dark brown horizontal line directly beneath it as an underline extending most of the image width. Below this, create a green process diagram running left to right: a thin solid medium muted green horizontal line with three triangular arrowheads pointing right connecting four pictogram stages labeled beneath in dark brown uppercase sans-serif as “FARM,” “MILL,” “ROASTER,” and “BARISTA.” At the Farm stage, draw a simple green plant icon with two leaves emerging from a small base; at the Mill stage, use a minimalist green factory building; at the Roaster stage, depict a simple drum or roasting-machine silhouette; at the Barista stage, draw a green head-and-shoulders figure holding a cup; add a small simple smiley-face icon at the far right of the line. prompt: Create a tall, vertically oriented flat vector illustration infographic set outdoors in a bright grassy park under a clear sky, designed to teach parents how to fight bullying. The background sky at the top uses a smooth vertical gradient from medium turquoise blue at the top edge to pale mint green at the horizon, with a few small white cloud puffs and several tiny white bird silhouettes flying above a continuous band of overlapping rounded green hills and bushes in multiple tones of dark forest green and lighter leafy green. The lower two-thirds of the scene is a gently uneven field of bright grass-green with a subtle gradient to lighter yellow-green near the bottom, dotted evenly with many small white and pale yellow flowers with simple circular centers and petal shapes, plus scattered darker green blade strokes suggesting grass. In the upper-right quadrant, place the main heading “FIGHT BULLYING” in two lines of very large bold uppercase rounded sans-serif letters filled with white and outlined thickly in dark green, sitting on an irregular dark green rounded-rectangle blob; at the far left of the word “FIGHT” and the far right of the word “BULLYING” add two identical bright red prohibition symbols, each a red circle with a red diagonal slash and a white interior. Down the left and center of the image, stack exactly five horizontally oriented rounded-rectangle information panels of similar width with slight variations in height; each panel has a semi-transparent light yellow-green fill, very soft or no visible border, and a slight drop shadow against the grass. Inside each panel, left-align a dark green bold uppercase rounded sans-serif heading followed by one sentence of smaller dark gray regular-weight sentence-case sans-serif body text, organized as: panel 1 “RECOGNIZE THE SIGNS,” panel 2 “OPEN COMMUNICATION,” panel 3 “TEACH EMPATHY AND RESPECT,” panel 4 “CREATE A SAFE SPACE,” panel 5 “COLLABORATE WITH EDUCATORS,” and a final bottom panel overlapping a semi-transparent cool light blue rounded rectangle labeled “PROMOTE POSITIVE RELATIONSHIPS,” all with consistent margins from the panel edges. Along the left edge of these panels, align vertically five identical circular icons, one centered beside each panel, each icon consisting of a white circle with a pale green outline containing a solid golden-yellow five-point star; connect the centers of these circles with a single dark green dashed vertical line made of evenly spaced short dashes. In the upper-left foreground on the grass, depict a group of three characters sitting: an adult in a light blue long-sleeve top and dark shorts sits cross-legged in the middle with a simple rounded head, dot eyes, and pink cheeks, flanked by two children wearing blue-and-black striped tops, also with rounded cartoon faces, each child holding an open white book on their lap. On the mid-left below the top panels, add a walking group of three: an adult wearing a coral long-sleeve top and dark brown pants strides forward on the grass with a smiling child in a very light pink dress on one side and another child in a brighter coral pink dress on the other, all sharing the same flat outlined cartoon style with rosy cheeks and minimal facial details. To the right of the central panels, place three more children and a cat: near the panel mid-height draw two children jumping or dancing with arms raised, one in a yellow long-sleeve shirt with thin vertical stripes and bright blue pants, the other in a white long-sleeve top covered in small light blue dots and dark pants; slightly below them, on a pale pink flower-shaped mat, depict a child sitting cross-legged wearing an orange cap, round glasses, a coral shirt with small dots, and light pants, with a small orange cat sitting upright next to them, both facing forward in the same flat style. In the bottom-right foreground, show a caregiver and child with a pet: an adult wearing a long cream-colored dress, a light hat, and a dark red vest holds or carries a child dressed in bright red clothing with a backpack, while a pale pink dog with a darker pink collar and leash stands at their feet, all drawn with clean outlines and small dot eyes. Behind them, add a small front-facing triangular tent colored light peach, decorated along its entrance with a string of tiny multicolored triangular bunting; position the tent so that a large pale yellow checkered picnic blanket, drawn as a tilted rectangle with a grid of thin intersecting lines, lies under and in front of the tent, and inside the tent show a child in a blue shirt sitting cross-legged and reading. At the bottom-left, form a blue water area shaped like a right triangle whose straight diagonal edge meets the grass as a shoreline; inside this pond draw exactly two white swans with orange beaks and smoothly curved necks, each leaving short curved white ripple lines on the water, which has a very slight vertical gradient from medium blue near the grass edge to lighter blue toward the bottom. Maintain a consistent flat, colorful, child-friendly cartoon illustration style throughout, with smooth curves, simple shapes, solid fills, and thin dark outlines for all people, animals, and key objects.

prompt: Create a vertical restaurant menu poster on a solid light warm beige background with no gradients, using a flat vector and photo collage style. At the very top left and top right corners, place two mirrored frying-pan-with-flame illustrations: each pan is a dark chocolate brown oval body with a straight dark brown handle angled toward its nearest corner, holding bright orange contents with a stylized bright orange flame rising upward; all parts of these icons use flat solid fills and no outlines. Centered below these icons, add the title block: the phrase “SPECIAL FOOD” in uppercase dark brown sans-serif letters follows a shallow upward arc, medium weight with slightly increased letter spacing, and directly beneath it place the word “MENU” in very large, bold, uppercase dark brown sans-serif text on a straight baseline, occupying most of the page width. Under the title, add a two-line centered welcome paragraph in regular-weight dark brown sans-serif, sentence case, reading: “Welcome to our restaurant! Our menu is carefully curated to offer you a delightful dining experience.” Below this, arrange the menu items in three horizontal rows with two columns each, so there are exactly six items total. In each row, use a circular food photo paired with a mustard-yellow pill-shaped label and a description paragraph. Every food photo is a full-color image cropped to a circle; around each image add a white circular ring and then a thin outer dark brown circular outline. In the left column, center each circle vertically in its row slightly left of the page midline, and from the midpoint of each circle extend a horizontal rounded rectangle (pill shape) filled with solid mustard-yellow to the right; to the right of each pill shape place a left-aligned block of descriptive text. In the right column, mirror this layout: each circular photo sits slightly right of center, and from the midpoint of each circle a mustard-yellow pill-shaped label extends horizontally to the left, with a left-aligned description block positioned to the left of that label. Ensure all six labels share the same solid mustard-yellow color with no gradients or patterns. Inside every label, use bold uppercase dark brown sans-serif text: position the item name on the left, then a series of dots, then a price with a dollar sign near the right end of the pill. For the top-left row, use a burger photo in the circle and label it “BURGER ... $10”; place to its right a small regular-weight dark brown sans-serif paragraph describing burgers, such as “Our burgers are crafted to perfection.” For the top-right row, use a pizza photo and label it “PIZZA ........ $50”; on the left of its label add a similarly styled dark brown paragraph describing the pizza. For the middle-left row, use a pasta dish photo with the label “PASTA ....... $40” and a paragraph to the right describing pasta salads; for the middle-right row, use a hotdog or loaded sandwich style photo with the label “BURGER ... $20” and a descriptive paragraph to the left; for the bottom-left row, use a layered grilled sandwich photo with the label “SANDWICH $30” and its descriptive paragraph to the right; for the bottom-right row, use a steak or grilled meat photo with the label “BURGER ... $10” and its descriptive paragraph to the left. All six descriptive paragraphs must be smaller than the label text, in regular-weight dark brown sans-serif, sentence case, and left-aligned. At the bottom of the page, add a full-width horizontal band occupying about one sixth of the page height, filled with the same solid mustard-yellow color used for the labels and having no border. Inside this band on the left side, place a solid black circle containing a white or negative-space phone handset icon, and to the right of this icon add the word “PHONE:” in uppercase bold dark brown sans-serif with the phone number “+123-456-789” in smaller regular-weight dark brown or black sans-serif directly beneath it. Inside the same band on the right side, add a solid black circle containing a white or negative-space location pin icon, and to its right place the word “LOCATION:” in uppercase bold dark brown sans-serif with the address line “123 Anywhere st., Any City” in smaller regular-weight dark brown or black sans-serif beneath it. Ensure all borders, icon shapes, and typographic elements use solid fills or strokes with no dashed or dotted lines, keeping the overall composition clean, flat, and precisely aligned.
